 R.D. SINELNIKOV 辛尔尼科夫
# ATIAS OF HUMAN ANATOMY 人解剖学图谱

## 1 ATLAS OF HUMAN ANATOMY 人体解剖图谱

# ATLAS OF HUMAN ANATOMY 人体解剖图谱

# Volume I 第一卷

The Science of Bones, Joints, Ligaments and Muscles
> 骨骼、关节、韧带与肌肉的科学

# PREFACE 前言

This edition of Professor R.D. Sinclnikov's *Atlas of Human Anatomy* is a translation of the 5th Russian edition. The *Atlas* was first published in 1952-1958 and has for more than 30 years served in training several generations of medical students and physicians working in various branches of medicine. Adherence to the principles on which the book is based, as well as the continuing need for it, explain why five editions of it in Russian, four in Spanish, and two in Czech have been published. A translation of the *Atlas* into Arabic is currently being prepared for publication. In preparing every new edition extensive research was carried out to supplement and improve the material of the book and to update it on the basis of the achievements of modern anatomical science. Up till 1981 work on the *Atlas* was conducted by R.D. Sinelnikov with the collaboration of Ya.R. Sinelnikov and in recent years only by Ya.R. Sinelnikov.

> 本版《R.D. Sinelnikov人解剖学图谱》是第五版俄文版的译本。该图谱最初于1952年至1958年间出版，至今已有三十多年历史，一直被用于培养各医学领域多个世代的医学生和医生。该书所遵循的原则以及其持续的使用需求，解释了为何已出版了五版俄文版、四版西班牙文版和两版捷克文版。目前，该图谱的阿拉伯文译本正在准备出版中。在准备每一版新书时，都进行了广泛的研究，以补充和改进书中的内容，并根据现代解剖学的最新成就对内容进行更新。直到1981年，该图谱的编撰工作由R.D. Sinelnikov与Ya.R. Sinelnikov合作完成，而近年来则仅由Ya.R. Sinelnikov单独完成。

Several new drawings have been added to this edition and new sections introduced pertaining to age features, particularly to those of children. To bring the fundamental theoretical discipline, which is what human anatomy is, closer to clinical practice, much space among the illustrations has been allotted to radiographs.

> 本版新增了若干插图，并新增了有关年龄特征的章节，尤其是针对儿童的特征。为使作为基础理论学科的人体解剖学更贴近临床实践，插图中专门留出大量篇幅用于X线影像。

The illustrations and the terminology in the text conform with the Paris Nomina Anatomica (PNA)<sup>1</sup> . However, some Latin names on the illustrations are given according to the Basle Nomina Anatomica (BNA). Many drawings have been redrawn to show the anatomical structures included in the new nomenclature, the text has been supplemented accordingly, and the subject index has been updated.

> 图示及文中的术语均符合《巴黎解剖学名词》（PNA）<sup>1</sup>。然而，部分图示中的拉丁名称是根据《巴塞尔解剖学名词》（BNA）提供的。许多图示已重新绘制，以展示新命名法中包含的解剖结构，文本也相应进行了补充，主题索引也已更新。

The material is presented in line with the dialectical principles of studying the body as an organic whole with consideration of all the links binding its systems. Special attention is focused on the data of macro-microscopic anatomy.

> 内容的呈现遵循了研究人体作为有机整体的辩证原则，充分考虑了各系统之间相互联系的各个环节。特别注重宏观与微观解剖学数据。

The material in this edition is arranged in the following manner. The first volume consists of sections on osteology, arthrology, and myology. The second volume is devoted to splanchnology and angiology. The third volume provides descriptions of the nervous system, the sense organs, and the endocrine glands.

> 本版内容的编排方式如下。第一卷包含骨学、关节学和肌学部分。第二卷专门介绍内脏学和血管学。第三卷提供对神经系统、感觉器官及内分泌腺的描述。

T h e sections on the science of the muscles and viscera point out the connection of every skeletal muscle and each internal organ with its nerves and vessels. Moreover, the description of the bony canals, grooves, and foramina is supplemented by information about the nerves and vessels which they transmit, thus making it easier to understand the interrelations of the body's different systems. The *Atlas* contains illustrations of complex preparations allowing simultaneous study of several parts of the body, which ensures a deeper knowledge of the links binding the different parts into a single whole.

> 关于肌肉和内脏科学的章节指出了每块骨骼肌和每个内脏器官与其神经和血管的联系。此外，对骨性管道、沟槽和孔的描述还补充了有关其所通过的神经和血管的信息，从而更易于理解身体各系统之间的相互关系。该图谱包含复杂标本的插图，允许同时研究身体的多个部分，这有助于更深入地理解将不同部分联结为一个整体的联系。

Some of the illustrations in the *Atlas* demonstrate not only the features of normal anatomy, but the existing topographo-anatomical correlations so as to bring anatomical concepts close to the requirements of medical practice.

> 一些图示不仅展示了正常解剖结构的特征，还展示了现存的解剖拓扑关系，以便将解剖学概念更贴近临床实践的要求。

The illustrations to this edition were made by different artists over a span of more than thirty years. Foremost among these illustrators were A.A. Alekseev and F.K. Kovbasa, who made the greater number of original drawings. With the addition of new illustrations which was necessary as the systematic revision of the *Atlas*  progressed, the old ones of the former editions were corrected and updated.

> 本版插图由多位艺术家在三十多年间陆续绘制。其中最主要的插图作者是A.A.阿列克谢夫和F.K.科瓦巴萨，他们绘制了大多数原始插图。随着《图谱》系统修订的推进，新增插图成为必要，原有的旧版插图则被进行修正和更新。

<sup>&#</sup>x27; English equivalents to the Latin terms are given according to the Birmingham Revision (BR) of the Paris Anatomical Nomenclature (PNA) *(Butterwortks Medical Dictionary,* 1978, second edition, Editor-in-Chief MacDonald Critchlcy).

> 根据巴黎解剖学名词（PNA）的伯明翰修订版（BR）（Butterworths 医学词典，1978年第二版，总编辑 MacDonald Critchlcy），给出了拉丁术语的英文对应词。

## 1 ABBREVIATIONS AND SYMBOLS 缩略语和符号

A., a., Aa., aa. - arteria, arteriae.

> A., a., Aa., aa. - 动脉，动脉（复数）[!note]- 术语说明  
“A., a.” 为“arteria”的缩写，分别表示单数和复数形式；“Aa., aa.” 为“arteriae”的缩写，分别表示单数和复数形式，用于拉丁文医学文献中表示动脉。

V., v., VV., vv.-vena, venae.

> V., v., VV., vv.—静脉，静脉（复数）[!note]- 术语说明  
“V.” 通常代表“vena”（单数静脉），而“v.” 也可作为“venae”（复数静脉）的缩写形式，具体使用需根据上下文判断。在医学文献中，通常会根据具体血管名称进行标注，如“V. cava inferior”表示“下腔静脉”。

M., m., Mm., mm.-musculus, musculi.

> M., m., Mm., mm.—肌肉，肌肉们。

Lig., lig., Ligg., ligg.-ligamentum, ligamenta.

> 韧带，韧带，Ligg., ligg.-韧带，韧带。

> [!note]- 术语说明
>  “Ligamentum” 是拉丁语，意为“韧带”，在中文医学文献中通常直接使用“韧带”一词，复数形式为“韧带”。

Gl., gl., Gll., gll.-glandula, glandulae.

> Gl., gl., Gll., gll.—腺体，腺体（复数）。

N., n., Nn., nn.-nervus, nervi.
> N., n., Nn., nn.—神经，神经的。

> [!note]- 术语说明 
> “nervus” 和 “nervi” 是拉丁语，分别表示“神经”（单数）和“神经”（复数），在中文医学文献中通常直接使用“神经”一词。

R., r., Rr., rr.-ramus, rami.

> R., r., Rr., rr.—主支，支，主支与支，主支与支

> [!note]- 术语说明  
"Ramus" 通常译作“主支”或“支”，在解剖学中用于描述骨骼或神经的分支结构。"R." 代表 "Ramus"，"r." 代表 "ramus"（小写表示单数），"Rr." 表示 "Ramus et ramus"（主支与支），"rr." 表示 "rami et rami"（主支与支的复数形式）。

S., seu, sive-or.

> S., seu, sive-  or.

C1, C2, C3-first, second, third cervical nerve.

> C1、C2、C3——第一、第二、第三颈神经。

Th<sub>1</sub>, Th<sub>2</sub>, Th<sub>3</sub>-first, second, third thoracic nerve.

> T<sub>1</sub>、T<sub>2</sub>、T<sub>3</sub>——第一、第二、第三胸神经。

L1, L2, L3-first, second, third lumbar nerve.

> L1、L2、L3——第一、第二、第三腰神经。

S<sub>1</sub>, S<sub>2</sub>, S<sub>3</sub>-first, second, third sacral nerve.

> S<sub>1</sub>、S<sub>2</sub>、S<sub>3</sub>——第一、第二、第三骶神经。

Constantly present nerve segments are put in round brackets, e.g. (C1, C2), (Th1, Th2).
> 持续存在的神经节段用圆括号表示，例如：(C1, C2)，(Th1, Th2)。

Inconstantly present nerve segments are put in round brackets which are enclosed within square brackets, e.g.  $[C_1(C_2)-C_7(C_8)]$ .
> 不规则出现的神经节段以圆括号标出，圆括号内再用方括号括起，例如：

(1/1), (1/2) etc. in the captions show the proportion of the size of the drawings to the natural size.
> （1/1）、（1/2）等在图注中表示图示尺寸与实际尺寸的比例。

# THE SCIENCE OF THE BONES *Osteologia* > 骨骼学

The skeleton formed by bones and joints is the supporting structure of the human body.
>由骨骼和关节组成的骨架是人体的支撑结构。

The bones *(ossa)* give rigid support to the soft tissues of the body and form levers which move due to muscle contraction.
>骨骼（ossa）为身体的软组织提供坚实的支撑，并形成杠杆，因肌肉收缩而产生运动。

In the whole body the bones form the skeletal system *(systema skeletale)* (Figs 1, 2, and 3b) which is made up of the axial skeleton *(skeleton axiale)* and the appendicular skeleton *(skeleton appendiculart).* The skull *(cranium),* the spinal column *(columna vertebratis),*  and the chest bones *(ossa thoracis)* form the axial skeleton. The appendicular skeleton consists of the bones of the upper limbs *(ossa membri superioris)* and the bones of the lower limbs *(ossa membri inferioris).*

> 在整个身体中，骨骼构成了骨骼系统（systema skeletale）（图1、图2和图3b），该系统由轴状骨骼（skeleton axiale）和附肢骨骼（skeleton appendiculare）组成。头骨（cranium）、脊柱（columna vertebralis）和胸骨（ossa thoracis）构成了轴状骨骼。附肢骨骼由上肢的骨骼（ossa membri superioris）和下肢的骨骼（ossa membri inferioris）组成。

The skeletal system includes over 200 bones, 85 of which are paired.
> 骨骼系统包括超过200块骨头，其中85块为成对的骨头。

Each bone is a complex organ composed of various types of connective tissue; it contains bone marrow which is supplied with vessels and nerves.
> 每块骨都是一种复杂的器官，由多种类型的结缔组织组成；其内部含有骨髓，骨髓内有血管和神经供应。

Most bones of a human adult consist of a bony and cartilaginous framework, as a result of which a bony part *(pars ossea)* and a cartilaginous part *(pars cartilaginosa)* are distinguished in the skeletal system. The bony part makes up most of the bone. The articular cartilages *(cartilagines articulates),* the epiphyseal cartilages *(cartilagines epipkysiaUs)* (Figs 5, 6, and 7), and the costal cartilages *(cartilagines costales)* form the cartilaginous part of the skeletal system.

> 大多数成年人的骨骼由骨性和软骨性框架组成，因此在骨骼系统中可以区分出骨性部分（pars ossea）和软骨性部分（pars cartilaginosa）。骨性部分构成了骨骼的大部分。关节软骨（cartilagines articulates）、骺软骨（cartilagines epiphyseal）（见图5、6和7）以及肋软骨（cartilagines costales）构成了骨骼系统的软骨性部分。

On the outside the bone is covered with a fine connective-tissue membrane, the periosteum (Fig. 4), in which a fibrous and an osteogenic layer are distinguished. The superficially situated fibrous layer is connected to the bone by fibres penetrating the bone and contains blood and lymph vessels and nerves. From this layer the vessels and nerves pass into the bone through nutrient foramina *(foramina nutricia)* and thence into the nutrient canal *(canalis nutricius).* The inner osteogenic layer contains osteogenic cells (osteoblasts) which take part in the processes of development and reorganization of bony tissue under normal conditions and after injuries and fractures. At the junction with the articular cartilage covering the ends of the bone, the periosteum is continuous with the perichondrium. As a result the bone as an organ is covered with a continuous connective-tissue membrane. This membrane covers the surface of the bone and all the structures situated on it: processes, spines, cristae, tubers, tubercles, roughened lines *(lineae asperae),* pits and depressions *(fovea, fossae),* etc.

> 骨的外层覆盖着一层细密的结缔组织膜，即骨膜（图4），其可分为纤维层和成骨层。位于表面的纤维层通过穿入骨质的纤维与骨相连，内含血管、淋巴管和神经。这些血管和神经从该层通过营养孔（foramina nutricia）进入骨内，并通过营养管（canalis nutricius）进入骨内部。内层的成骨层含有成骨细胞（成骨细胞，osteoblasts），在正常情况下以及在损伤和骨折后，这些细胞参与骨组织的发育和重组过程。在与覆盖于骨端的关节软骨相接处，骨膜与软骨膜（perichondrium）相连续。因此，作为器官的骨被一层连续的结缔组织膜所覆盖。该膜覆盖骨的表面及其上所有的结构：突起、棘、嵴、隆起、结节、粗糙线（lineae asperae）、凹陷（fovea, fossae）等。

The bone is lined on the inside by a finer membrane, the endosteum.
> 骨的内侧被一层更薄的膜所覆盖，称为内骨膜。

According to shape, long bones *(ossa longa),* short bones *(ossa brevia),* and flat bones *(ossa plana)* are distinguished (Fig. 3a). Some bones contain cavities filled with air and are called pneumatic *(ossa pneumatica).*

> 根据形状，骨可分为长骨（ossa longa）、短骨（ossa brevia）和扁骨（ossa plana）（见图3a）。一些骨内含有充满空气的腔室，称为含气骨（ossa pneumatica）。

The long bones (humerus, clavicle, femur, phalanges, etc.) have a middle part, the diaphysis, and two end parts, the epiphyses. The epiphysis located closer to the axial skeleton is called the proximal epiphysis *(epiphysis proximalis),* the epiphysis of the same bone but situated further from the axial skeleton is called the distal epiphysis *(epiphysis distalis)* (Fig. 5). The wider parts of long bones between the diaphysis and the epiphysis are known as metaphyses. Their boundaries are visible only in the bones of children and adolescents when a cartilaginous layer, the epiphyseal cartilage *(cartilago epiphysialis)* (Figs 5, 7), still remains between the diaphysis and epiphyses. The bone grows intensely in length at the expense of this cartilage, which is later replaced by bony tissue forming the epiphyseal line *(linea epiphysialis),* which can hardly be detected with age.

> 长骨（如肱骨、锁骨、股骨、指骨等）具有中部部分，称为骨干（diaphysis），以及两端部分，称为骨端（epiphysis）。靠近轴状骨骼的骨端称为近侧骨端（epiphysis proximalis），而同一条骨中远离轴状骨骼的骨端称为远侧骨端（epiphysis distalis）（图5）。骨干与骨端之间的较长部分称为骨骺（metaphysis）。骨骺的边界仅在儿童和青少年的骨骼中可见，因为此时骨干与骨端之间仍存在软骨层，称为骨骺软骨（cartilago epiphysialis）（图5、图7）。骨通过该软骨在长度方向上迅速生长，随后该软骨被骨组织替代，形成骨骺线（linea epiphysialis），随着年龄增长，骨骺线几乎难以被检测到。

In a cross-section of a long bone (Fig. 6) one can distinguish the compact substance *(substantia compacta)* forming the outer layers of the bone and the spongy (cancellous) substance *(substantia spongiosa)* found deeper than the compact substance, mainly in the epiphyses and mctaphyses. In the diaphyses of long tubular bones the compact substance surrounds the medullary canal *(cavitas medullaris)* shaped like a tube.

> 在长骨的横断面（图6）中，可以区分出致密骨质（substantia compacta），它形成骨的外层；以及位于致密骨质深层的松质（cancellous）骨质（substantia spongiosa），主要分布于骨骺和骨髓端。在长管状骨的骨干部分，致密骨质围绕着呈管状的骨髓腔（cavitas medullaris）。

A cross-section of short bones (Fig. 3a) (vertebrae, carpal bones, tarsal bones, etc.) reveals on the surface a thinner layer of compact substance surrounding the trabeculae of the spongy substance which forms the greater part of the bone. The trabeculae of the spongy substance form a complex meshwork but are arranged in each bone of the skeletal system strictly in accordance with the functional loads.

> 短骨的横断面（图3a）（如椎骨、腕骨、跗骨等）显示，表面有一层较薄的致密骨质覆盖在松质骨的骨小梁上，而松质骨构成了骨的大部分。松质骨的骨小梁形成复杂的网状结构，但在骨骼系统的每一骨中，其排列方式均严格遵循功能负荷的分布。

In the flat bones (Fig. 3a) (the bones of the skull cap, shoulder blade, pelvic bone, etc.) the spongy substance, in contrast, usually forms a thinner layer and is surrounded on both sides by plates of the compact substance. In the bones of the skull cap, however, the spongy substance is sufficiently developed. It is known as diploe (Fig. 8) and is sandwiched between the outer and inner plates of the compact substance *(lamina externa* and *lamina interna).* Diploetic canals *(canales diploici)* providing for the passage of venous vessels extend through the spongy substance of the skull-cap bones.

> 在扁骨（图3a）（如颅骨盖骨、肩胛骨、骨盆骨等）中，松质骨通常形成较薄的一层，并被两侧的密质骨板所包围。然而，在颅骨盖骨中，松质骨则发育良好。这种结构称为板障（图8），位于外板（lamina externa）和内板（lamina interna）之间。板障内有板障孔（canales diploici），允许静脉血管通过。

The meshes of the spongy substance and the medullary canal contain the bone marrow *(medulla ossium).* Red marrow *(medulla ossium rubra)* and yellow marrow *(medulla ossium flava)* are distinguished.

> 海绵质的网状结构和骨髓腔内含有骨髓（骨髓）。红骨髓（红骨髓）和黄骨髓（黄骨髓）可以区分。

The red marrow possesses high functional activity and is capable of forming blood cells of the myeloid series. With the development and growth of the organism, the red marrow is gradually replaced by the yellow marrow. The yellow marrow is less active and plays a reserve role, but under certain conditions it may be activated.

> 红骨髓具有高度的生理活性，能够生成髓系血细胞。随着机体的发育和生长，红骨髓逐渐被黄骨髓所取代。黄骨髓活性较低，主要起储备作用，但在某些条件下也可能被激活。

## 2 Spongy substance of skull cap (diploë) (3/5).

> 2 颅盖松质骨（骨内板）（3/5）

(Skull, from the right side, with the lower jaw removed. The outer table, lamina externa, of the skull bones is removed.)

> （右侧颅骨，下颌骨已去除。颅骨骨板的外板、外层骨板已被去除。）

# THE BONES OF THE TRUNK 躯干的骨骼

The vertebrae, ribs, and the sternum arc the bones of the trunk.
> 椎骨、肋骨和胸骨构成了躯干的骨骼。

## 1 THE VERTEBRAE 椎骨

The vertebrae, 33 or 34 in number, arc rings placed one above another which form a single column, the vertebral or spinal column *(columna vertebralis)* (Fig. 9).

> 椎骨共有33或34块，呈环状排列，逐节叠置形成一个整体，即椎骨或脊柱（vertebral column，columna vertebralis）（图9）。

The vertebral column has the following parts: the cervical part *(pars cervicalis),* the thoracic part *(pars tkoracica),* the lumbar part *(pars lumbalis)^* (he sacral part *(pars sacralis),* and the coccygeal part *rs coccygea).* In accordance with this, five groups of vertebrae are distinguished: cerebral *(vertebrae cervkae)* (7), thoracic *(vertebrae thoracicae)* (12), lumbar *(vertebrae lumbales)* (5), sacral *(vertebrae sacrales)* (5), and coccygeal *(vertebrae coccygeae)* (4 or 5).

> 椎骨列可分为以下部分：颈段（pars cervicalis）、胸段（pars thoracica）、腰段（pars lumbalis）、骶段（pars sacralis）以及尾段（pars coccygea）。根据这一划分，可将椎骨分为五组：颈椎（vertebrae cervicae）（7块）、胸椎（vertebrae thoracicae）（12块）、腰椎（vertebrae lumbales）（5块）、骶椎（vertebrae sacrales）（5块）以及尾椎（vertebrae coccygeae）（4或5块）。

The vertebral column of a human adult forms four *curvatures*

> 人类成年人的椎骨列形成四个弯曲

in the sagittal plane: cervical, thoracic, lumbar (abdominal), and sacral (pelvic). The cervical and lumbar curvatures are concave anteriorly, the condition being known as *lordosis* (Gk); the thoracic and pelvic curvatures are convex anteriorly and arc called *kyphosis*  (Gk).

> 在矢状面上：颈椎、胸椎、腰椎（腹部）、骶椎（骨盆）。颈椎和腰椎的曲度向前凹陷，这种状况称为脊柱前凸（lordosis，源自希腊语）；胸椎和骨盆的曲度向前凸出，称为脊柱后凸（kyphosis，源自希腊语）。

In addition, all vertebrae are separated into two groups—true and false vertebrae; the cervical, thoracic, and lumbar vertebrae make up the first group, while the sacral vertebrae fused to form the sacrum *(os sacrum)* and the coccygeal vertebrae fused to form the coccyx *(os coccygis)* belong to the second group.

> 此外，所有椎骨均可分为两类——真椎骨和假椎骨；颈椎、胸椎和腰椎组成第一类，而融合形成骶骨（os sacrum）的骶椎以及融合形成尾骨（os coccygis）的尾椎则属于第二类。

## 2 THE VERTEBRA 椎骨

The vertebra (sec Figs 22 and 23) has a body, an arch, and processes.
> 椎骨（见图22和图23）由椎体、椎弓和突起组成。

![](_page_25_Picture_10.jpeg)
The vertebral body *(corpus vertebrae)* is the anterior, thickened part of the vertebra and is bounded superiorly and inferiorly by surfaces facing, respectively, the adjacent proximal and distal vertebrae, anteriorly and laterally by a slightly concave surface, and posteriorly by a flat surface. The body of the vertebra, its posterior surface in particular, has very many nutrient foramina *(foramina nutriaa),* which serve for the passage of vessels and nerves into the bone substance. The bodies of the vertebrae are joined by means of intervertebral discs (cartilages), as a result of which a very flexible column, the vertebral column, is formed.

> 椎体（vertebral body，corpus vertebrae）是椎骨的前部、增厚的部分，其上、下界分别与相邻的近端和远端椎骨的表面相接。椎体的前外侧为稍凹陷的表面，后侧则为平坦的表面。椎体，尤其是其后表面，具有大量营养孔（foramina nutritionis），这些孔为血管和神经进入骨质提供通道。椎体之间通过椎间盘（intervertebral discs，cartilages）相连，从而形成一个非常灵活的结构，即椎列（vertebral column）。

The vertebral arch *(arcus vertebrae)* forms the posterior and lateral boundaries of the vertebral foramen *(foramen vertebraU).* The foramina are placed one above the other to form the vertebral canal *(canalis vertebralis)* which lodges the spinal cord. The arch arises from the posterolateral edges of the vertebral body as a narrow segment, the pedicle of the vertebral arch *(pedicvlus arcus vertebrae).* The superior and inferior surfaces of the pedicle bear the superior vertebral notch *(incisura vertebralis superior)* and the inferior vertebral notch *(incisura vertebralis inferior).* The superior notch of one vertebra adjoins the inferior notch of the proximally situated vertebra to form the intervertebral foramen *(foramen intervertebrale),*  which transmits the spinal nerves and vessels.

> 椎弓（arcus vertebrae）构成了椎孔（foramen vertebrae）的后侧和外侧边界。椎孔彼此上下排列，形成椎管（canalis vertebralis），容纳脊髓。椎弓从椎体的后外侧缘延伸出，形成一个狭窄的段，称为椎弓根（pediculus arcus vertebrae）。椎弓根的上下面分别有上椎切迹（incisura vertebralis superior）和下椎切迹（incisual vertebralis inferior）。一个椎体的上椎切迹与上方椎体的下椎切迹相接，形成椎间孔（foramen intervertebrale），椎间孔内通过脊神经和血管。

![](_page_20_Figure_2.jpeg)

9. *Vertebral column (columna vertebralis)* (%). A—from the right side; B —anterior aspect; C—posterior aspect.

> 椎骨列（vertebral column）(%). A—右侧视图；B—前面观；C—后面观。

The vertebral processes *(processus vertebrates),* seven in number, project from the vertebral arch. One of them, unpaired, extends from the middle of the arch and to the back and is called the spine of the vertebra or spinous process *(processus spinosus).* The other processes are paired. One pair, the superior articular processes *(processus articulares superiores)* arise from the superior surface of the arch; the other pair, the inferior articular processes *(processus articu-*

> 椎骨棘突（processus vertebralis），共七个，从椎弓发出。其中有一个未配对的棘突，从椎弓中部延伸至后方，称为椎骨的棘突或棘突（processus spinosus）。其余的棘突均为成对的。一对为上关节突（processus articulares superiores），起自椎弓的上表面；另一对为下关节突（processus articulares inferiores），起自椎弓的下表面。

*lares inferiores)* project from the inferior surface of the arch; the third pair, the transverse processes *(processus transversi),* extend from the lateral surfaces of the arch (see Figs 24-27).

> 下关节突（foramina inferiora）从椎弓的下表面突出；第三对，即横突（processus transversi），从椎弓的外侧表面延伸出来（见图24-27）。

The superior articular processes have superior articular facets *(fades articulares superiores);* the inferior processes bear similar inferior articular facets *(fades articulares inferiores).* Each vertebra articulates with the adjacent distal vertebra by means of these facets.

> 上关节突具有上关节面（fades articulares superiores）；下关节突则具有类似的下关节面（fades articulares inferiores）。每个椎骨通过这些关节面与相邻的远端椎骨相连接。

## 3 CERVICAL VERTEBRAE 颈椎

The cervical vertebrae *(vertebrae cervicales)* (Figs 9-21), seven in number, are characterized by small low bodies (with the exception of the first two vertebrae) which gradually become wider in the direction of the last, seventh vertebra. The superior surface of the body is slightly concave from right to left, while the inferior surface is concave from front to back.

> 颈椎（vertebrae cervicales）（图9-21），共7块，其特点是椎体较小且较低（除第一、二颈椎外），椎体逐渐向第七颈椎方向变宽。椎体的上表面从右向左略呈凹陷，下表面则从前向后略呈凹陷。

The vertebral foramen *(foramen vertebrale)* is wide and almost triangular in shape.

> 椎间孔（vertebral foramen）呈宽而近似三角形的形状。

The articular processes *(processus articulares)* are relatively short, extend obliquely, and their articular facets are smooth or slightly convex.

> 关节突（processus articulares）相对较短，呈斜向延伸，其关节面光滑或略呈凸起。

The spines or spinous processes *(processus spinosus)* increase in length gradually from the second to the seventh vertebra. Except in the case of the first and seventh vertebrae the spinous process is bifid and slightly inclined downward.

> 棘突或棘突过程（processus spinosus）从第二椎体到第七椎体逐渐变长。除第一椎体和第七椎体外，棘突通常呈双叉状，并略微向下倾斜。

The transverse processes *(processus transversus)* are short and project laterally. The superior surface of each process carries a deep groove for the spinal nerve *(suUus newt spinalis)* (Fig. 13). It separates an anterior tubercle from a posterior tubercle *(tuberculum animus* et *tuberculum posterius)* situated on the end of the transverse process.

> 横突（processus transversus）较短，向外侧突出。每个横突的上表面有一个深沟，容纳脊神经（nervis spinalis superior）（图13）。该沟将横突末端的前结节（tuberculum anterius）与后结节（tuberculum posterius）分开。

Tuberculum posterius 后突起

The sixth vertebra has a particularly developed anterior tubercle. The common carotid artery *(arteria carotis communis)* stretches in front of and close to the tubercle and can be pressed to it when haemorrhage occurs. Hence the name carotid tubercle *(tuberculum caroticum).*

> 第六节椎体具有特别发达的前结节。颈总动脉（动脉carotis communis）位于该结节的前方并靠近其位置，在发生出血时，可将动脉按压至该结节上。因此得名颈动脉结节（carotid tubercle）。

The transverse process of the cervical vertebrae is formed of two processes. The anterior one is the costal process *(processus costarius),* which is a rib rudiment; the posterior one is the true transverse process *(processus transversus).* Together they form the border of the foramen transversarium transmitting the vertebral artery, veins, and the attendant sympathetic nerve plexus.

> 颈椎的横突由两个突起构成。前者的为肋骨残迹，称为肋突（costal process，processus costarius）；后者为真正的横突（true transverse process，processus transversus）。两者共同构成横孔的边界，横孔内通过椎动脉、静脉及伴随的交感神经丛。

In the cervical spine, the first vertebra, the atlas, the second, the axis, and the seventh, the vertebra prominens, differ from the common type of cervical vertebrae.

> 在颈椎中，第一椎体（寰椎）、第二椎体（枢椎）和第七椎体（隆突椎）与常见的颈椎椎体有所不同。

The first cervical vertebra, the atlas (Figs 10, II, and 14) has neither a body nor a spine but is a ring formed of two arches, anterior and posterior *(arcus anterior* et *arcus posterior),* joined to one another by two more developed lateral masses *(massae lalcralcs).* Each of these masses carries an oval concave superior articular facet *(fovea articularis superior),* on the superior surface for articulation with the occipital bone, and an almost flat inferior articular facet *(fovea*

> 第一颈椎即寰椎（图10、图II和图14），它既无椎体也无椎突，而是一个由前弓和后弓（arcus anterior et arcus posterior）构成的环形结构，这两弓通过两个更为发达的侧块（massae lacerae）相互连接。每个侧块均具有一个椭圆形的上关节面（fovea articularis superior），位于其上表面，与枕骨形成关节；同时还具有一个几乎平坦的下关节面（fovea articularis inferior）。

![](_page_21_Figure_16.jpeg)

10. *First cervical vertebra (atlas);* superior aspect (/,).

> 第一颈椎（寰椎）；上方面（/）

11. *First cervical vertebra (atlas)*; inferior aspect *(Yx).*

> 第一颈椎（寰椎）；下部（Yx）

*articularis inferior),* on the inferior surface for articulation with the second servical vertebra.

> 关节面下部（articularis inferior），位于下表面，与第二颈椎形成关节。

The anterior arch *(arcus anterior)* bears an anterior tubercle *(tuberculum anterius)* on the anterior surface and a small facet for the odontoid process *(fovea dentis)* on the posterior surface which articulates with the dens of the axis.

> 前弓（arcus anterior）的前表面有一个前结节（tuberculum anterius），后表面有一个小凹陷（fovea dentis），与齿突（odontoid process）相接，该齿突属于寰椎（axis）。

The posterior arch *(arcus posterior)* has a posterior tubercle *(tuberculum posterius)* representing the spine. On the superior surface of the arch is a groove for the vertebral artery *(sulcus arteriae vertebralis);* the groove is sometimes converted into a canal.

> 后弓（arcus posterior）有一个后突（tuberculum posterius），代表脊椎。在弓的上表面有一个沟槽，为椎动脉沟（sulcus arteriae vertebralis）；该沟槽有时会转变为管腔。

The second cervical vertebra, the axis (Figs 12-14), carries an odontoid process (the *dens)* projecting upward from the body. The atlas together with the skull rotates about the dens like about an axis.

> 第二颈椎，即枢椎（图12-14），其椎体向上伸出一个齿突（齿状突）。寰椎与颅骨围绕齿突旋转，如同围绕一个轴。

The anterior aspect of the dens carries an anterior articular

> 齿突的前部具有前关节面

facet *(fades articularis anterior)* for articulation with fovea dentis of the atlas. The posterior surface has a posterior articular facet *(fades articularis posterior),* which the transverse ligament of the atlas *(ligamentum transversum atlantis)* adjoins. There arc neither anterior and posterior tubercles nor a groove for the spinal nerve on the transverse processes.

> 椎体前缘有前关节面（fades articularis anterior），与寰椎齿突凹（fovea dentis of the atlas）形成关节。椎体后缘有后关节面（fades articularis posterior），由寰椎横韧带（ligamentum transversum atlantis）相连。横突上既无前、后结节，也无脊神经沟。

The seventh cervical vertebra, vertebra prominens (Fig. 19), is distinguished by its long and non-bifid spinous process which is easily palpated under the skin, thus the name. Furthermore, its transverse processes are long, the foramen transversarium is very small (it provides passage for the vertebral vein) and is even absent in some cases.

> 第七颈椎，即隆突椎（图19），因其较长且不分叉的棘突而易于辨认，该棘突在皮肤下可触及，因此得名。此外，其横突较长，横突孔非常小（仅允许椎静脉通过），在某些情况下甚至可能缺失。

A costal facet *(fovea costalis),* a mark left by articulation with the head of the first rib, is often present on the lower edge of the lateral surface of the body.

> 肋骨关节面（fovea costalis），为第一肋头关节面留下的痕迹，常出现在椎体侧缘的下缘。[!note]- 术语说明
“costal facet” 通常译作“肋骨关节面”，是椎体与肋骨连接处的凹陷结构，属于椎体的解剖标志之一。

## 4 **THORACIC VERTEBRAE**

> 4 胸椎

The thoracic vertebrae *(vertebrae tkorackae)* (Figs 9, 22-25), 12 in number, arc much higher and thicker than the cervical vertebrae; iheir bodies gradually increase in size downwards toward the lumbar vertebrae.

> 胸椎（thoracic vertebrae）（图9、图22-25），共12块，比颈椎高而厚得多；其椎体自上而下逐渐增大，直至腰椎。

The posterolatcral surface of the body carries two facets, the superior cosial facet *(fovea costalis superior),* and the inferior costal facet *(fovea costalis inferior).* Th e inferior facet of one vertebra joins the superior facet of the adjacent distal vertebra to form a complele articular facet, the site of articulation with the head of the rib. The body of the first thoracic vertebra is an exception; it has a complete cosial facet for articulation with the head of the first rib on the superior part of the posterolatcral surface and a semifacet for articulation with the head of the second rib on the inferior part. The tenth vertebra has one semifacet on the superior edge of the body. The bodies of the eleventh and twelfth vertebrae each bear only one complete costal facet in the middle of each lateral surface.

> 椎体的后外侧表面具有两个面，即上肋凹（superior costal facet，fovea costalis superior）和下肋凹（inferior costal facet，fovea costalis inferior）。一个椎体的下肋凹与相邻椎体的上肋凹相接，形成一个完整的关节面，这是与肋头形成关节的部位。第一胸椎的椎体是一个例外，其上肋凹位于椎体后外侧表面的上部，用于与第一肋头形成关节；其下肋凹为半肋凹，位于椎体后外侧表面的下部，用于与第二肋头形成关节。第十椎体的椎体上缘有一个半肋凹。第十一和第十二椎体的椎体在每个侧面上中部各有一个完整的肋凹。

The arches of the thoracic vertebrae form nearly circular vertebral foramina which are, however, relatively smaller than those of the cervical vertebrae.

> 胸椎的椎弓形成近似圆形的椎孔，但这些椎孔相对于颈椎的椎孔来说则相对较小。

The transverse process extends laterally and a little to the back and carries a small costal facet *(fovea costalis transoersalis)* for articulation with the tubercle of the rib.

> 横突向侧方和略向后延伸，并带有一个小的肋头关节面（横突肋面，fovea costalis transversalis），用于与肋骨的肋头形成关节。

The articular surface of the articular processes lies in the frontal plane and faces backwards on the superior process but forwards on the inferior process.

> 关节突的关节面位于冠状面上，其在上关节突上朝向后方，在下关节突上则朝向前方。

The spinous processes arc long, three-sided, pointed, and slope downwards. Those of the middle thoracic vertebrae fit one over the other like tiles.

> 棘突呈长形、三棱状、尖锐并向下倾斜。中胸椎的棘突相互重叠，如同瓦片般排列。

The lower thoracic vertebrae are shaped more like the lumbar vertebrae. The transverse processes of the twelfth thoracic vertebra have on their posterior surface an accessory process *(processus accessorius)* and a mamillary process *(processus mamillaris).*

> 下胸椎的形态更接近腰椎。第十二胸椎的横突在其后表面具有一个辅助突（processus accessorius）和一个乳头突（processus mamillaris）。

![](_page_25_Picture_10.jpeg)

> 第八胸椎（vertebra thoracica VIII）；上部（J/J）

## 5 LUMBAR VERTEBRAE

> 5 腰椎

The lumbar vertebrae *(vertebrae lumbalis)* (Figs 9, 26-29), five in number, are distinguished from other vertebrae by their large size. The body of these vertebrae is bean-shaped, the arches are strongly developed, and the vertebral foramen is larger than in the thoracic segment and is shaped like an irregular triangle.

> 腰椎（vertebrae lumbalis）（图9、26-29），共五块，与其他椎骨相比具有较大的体积。腰椎椎体呈豆形，椎弓发育明显，椎孔比胸段椎骨的椎孔更大，呈不规则三角形。

Each transverse process is in front of the articular process; it is compressed from front to back, and projects laterally and a little to the back. Its greater part is a rudiment of the rib and is therefore called the costal process *(processus costarius).* On the posterior surface of the base of the costal surface is a slightly detectable accessory process *(processes accessorius),* which is a rudiment of the transverse process.

> 每个横突位于关节突的前方，从前向后呈压缩状，并向侧方和略向后突出。其大部分为肋骨的残余结构，因此称为肋骨突（costal process，processus costarius）。在肋骨面基部的后表面，有一个略可察觉的副突（accessory process，processes accessorius），它是横突的残余结构。

The spinous process is short and wide, thickened and rounded at the end. The articular processes arise from the arch, project to the back of the transverse process almost vertically. The articular surfaces lie in the sagittal plane; the superior facet is concave and faces medially, while the inferior surface is convex and faces laterally.

> 棘突短而宽，末端增厚且呈圆形。关节突起自椎弓，几乎垂直地向横突的后方延伸。关节面位于矢状面上；上关节面凹陷且朝向内侧，下关节面凸起且朝向外侧。

Upon articulation of two adjacent vertebrae, the superior articular processes of one vertebra embrace the sides of the inferior articular processes of the other. The superior articular process bears a small mamillary process *(processus mamillaris)* on its posterolatcral edge, the site of insertion of muscles.

> 当相邻椎骨发生关节运动时，一个椎骨的上关节突拥抱另一个椎骨下关节突的两侧。上关节突在其后外侧缘具有一个小的乳头状突起（processus mamillaris），是肌肉附着的部位。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_27_Picture_7.jpeg)

> 第三腰椎（腰椎 III）；上表面（' /）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_28_Picture_2.jpeg)

> 第三腰椎（腰椎 III）；右侧（K）

## 7 THE SACRUM 骶骨

In an adult the sacral vertebrae *(vertebrae sacralcs)* five in number, are fused to form a single bone, the sacrum.

> 在成人的骶骨（骶椎，vertebrae sacralcs）共有五块，它们融合形成一块单一的骨骼，即骶骨。

The sacrum *(os sacrum)* (Figs 9 and 30-35) is wedge-shaped; it is located below the last lumbar vertebra and contributes to the formation of the posterior wall of the true pelvis. The anterior and posterior surfaces, two lateral borders, a base (with the wide part directed upwards), and an apex (with the narrow part directed downwards) are distinguished in the sacrum. A curved sacral canal *(canalis sacralis)* runs through the bone from base to apex (Figs 33-35).

> 骶骨（os sacrum）（图9和图30-35）呈楔形；其位于最后一节腰椎的下方，并参与构成真骨盆的后壁。骶骨可区分出前表面、后表面、两侧缘、基底部（宽的部分朝上）以及尖端（窄的部分朝下）。一条弯曲的骶管（canalis sacralis）贯穿骶骨，从基底部延伸至尖端（图33-35）。

The anterior surface of the sacrum is smooth and concave and faces the pelvic cavity, thus it is called the pelvic surface *(fades pel vina).* It has traces of fusion of the bodies of the five sacral vertebrae in the form of four parallel transverse ridges *(Hneac transversae).* Laterally to these ridges on each side are four anterior sacral foramina *(foramina sacralis peloina)y* which lead posteriorly and medially into the cavity of the sacral canal (they transmit the anterior rami of the sacral spinal nerves and the attendant vessels).

> 骶骨的前面光滑且凹陷，朝向骨盆腔，因此称为骨盆面（fades pel vina）。其上有五块骶椎体融合的痕迹，表现为四条平行的横行骨嵴（Hneac transversae）。这些骨嵴的两侧各有一对前骶孔（foramina sacralis peloina），这些孔向后方和内侧开口，通向骶管腔（它们传递骶神经的前支及伴随血管）。

The dorsal sacral surface *(fades dorsalis)* (Fig. 31) is convex longitudinally, narrower than the pelvic surface, and rough. It carries five bony tubercles descending one after another. They arc formed from fusion of the spinous, transverse, and articular processes of the sacral vertebrae.

> 背侧骶面（背侧面，fades dorsalis）（图31）纵向隆凸，比盆侧表面更窄，表面粗糙。其上承载五个骨性隆起，依次向下排列。这些隆起是由骶椎的棘突、横突和关节突融合形成。

The spinous tubercles of the sacrum *(crista sacralis mediana)* arc formed as the result of fusion of the spinous processes of the sacral vertebrae and are represented by four tubercles one above the other, sometimes fused to form a single rough crest.

> 骶骨的棘突结节（中线骶骨嵴）是由于骶椎棘突的融合形成的，表现为自下而上排列的四个结节，有时会融合成一个粗糙的嵴。

On both sides and lateral to the crista mediana and almost parallel to it stretches a weakly pronounced articular tubercle of sacrum *(crista sacralis intermedia);* it is formed by fusion of the superior and inferior articular processes. Still laterally on each side is a well pronounced series of transverse tubercles *(crista sacralis lateralis).*

> 在中线两侧、位于中线嵴（crista mediana）的外侧，并几乎与其平行处，有一处弱而明显的骶骨关节突（crista sacralis intermedia）；它是由上关节突和下关节突融合形成的。在更外侧的两侧，还有一系列明显突出的横突（crista sacralis lateralis）。

![](_page_30_Picture_10.jpeg)

30. *Sacrum (os sacrum);* anterior aspect i (Pelvic surface.)

> 骶骨（os sacrum）；前面观（盆面）

T H E SACRUM 33

> 第 33 章 骶骨

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_31_Picture_1.jpeg)

3 1. *Sacrum (os sacrum)*'; posterior aspect (%). (Dorsal surface.)

> 3 1. 骶骨（骶骨）；后侧（背面）。

which are formed from fusion of the transverse processes. On each side between the articular and transverse tubercles there are four dorsal sacral foramina *(foramina sacralis dorsalia).* They are somewhat smaller than the corresponding pelvic sacral foramina and transmit the posterior rami of the sacral nerves.

> 这些结构是由横突融合形成的。在每个侧面上，位于关节突和横突结节之间有四个背侧骶孔（背侧骶孔）。这些孔比相应的盆部骶孔略小，并通过这些孔传递骶神经的后支。

The sacral canal *(canalis sacralis)* runs along the entire length of the sacrum; it curves and is wider on top and narrower downwards. It is a direct downward continuation of the spinal canal. The sacral canal communicates with the sacral foramina anteriorly and posteriorly via intervertebral foramina *(foramina intervertebralia)* lying in the bone.

> 骶管（canalis sacralis）沿整个骶骨的长度走行，其形态呈弯曲状，上部较宽，下部较窄。骶管是脊髓管直接向下延续的部分。骶管通过位于骨内的椎间孔（foramina intervertebralia）与骶前孔和骶后孔相通。

The base of the sacrum *(basis ossis sacri)* (Figs 30 and 35) carries a iransversc-oval depression for articulation with the inferior surface of the body of the fifth lumbar vertebra. At the site of the articulation the anterior border of the base projects markedly into the pelvic cavity to form the promontory *(promontorium).* The superior articular processes *(processus artkulares superiores)* of the first sacral vertebra project upward from the posterior part of the sacral base. Their superior articular facets *(fades articulares superiores)* face backwards and medially and articulate with the inferior articular processes of the fifth lumbar vertebra. The entry into the sacral canal is bounded by the posterior border of the base (arch) of the sacrum with the superior articular processes projecting from it.

> 骶骨底（basis ossis sacri）（图30和图35）具有一个横卵圆形凹陷，与第五腰椎体的下表面形成关节。在关节部位，骶骨底的前缘明显突入盆腔，形成突出部（promontorium）。第一骶椎的上关节突（processus articularis superiores）从骶骨底的后部向上突出。它们的上关节面（facies articularis superiores）朝向后方和内侧，与第五腰椎的下关节突相接。进入骶管的入口由骶骨底（弓）的后缘以及由此延伸出的上关节突所界定。

The apex of the sacrum *(apex ossis sacri)* is narrow, blunt, and has a small oval surface for articulation with the superior surface of the coccyx. Here is the sacrococcygeal joint *(junctura sacrococcygea)* (symphysis) (see Fig. 9).

> 骶骨的尖端（apex ossis sacri）较窄、钝圆，并有一个小的椭圆形表面与尾骨的上表面形成关节。此处为骶尾关节（junctura sacrococcygea）（骨性联合）（见图9）。

Behind the apex, on the posterior surface of the sacrum, the articular tubercles terminate as two small downward projections, the sacral cornua *(cornua sacralia).* The posterior surface of the apex and the sacral cornua form the borders of the opening of the sacral canal, the sacral hiatus *(hiatus sacralis).*

> 在骶骨尖端后方，骶骨的后表面，关节结节终止为两个向下的小突起，称为骶骨角（骶骨角，cornua sacralia）。骶骨尖端的后表面和骶骨角共同构成骶管开口的边界，称为骶管裂孔（骶管裂孔，hiatus sacralis）。

![](_page_32_Figure_2.jpeg)

*lis),* **is formed by fusion of the transverse processes of the sacral vertebrae.**

> （骶骨的横突融合形成）由骶椎的横突融合形成。

**The lateral surface of these parts (Fig. 32) has an articular auricular surface** *(fades auricularis),* **which articulates with the auricular surface of the ilium (see** *The Bones of the Pelvic Girdle).*

> 这些部分的外侧表面（图32）具有关节耳状面（耳状关节面），与骨盆带骨的耳状面（见《骨盆带骨》）相接。

**To the back and medially of the auricular surface is a well pronounced sacral tuberosity** *(tuberositas sacralis)\** **the site of attachment of the interosseous sacro-iliac ligaments.**

> 耳状面的后方和内侧有一个明显的骶骨粗隆（骶骨粗隆），是骨间骶髂韧带的附着处。[!note]- 术语说明  
“tuberositas sacralis” 通常译作“骶骨粗隆”，是骶骨的标志性骨性突起，常用于解剖学定位。

**The sacrum of a male is longer, narrower, and more curved than that of a female.**

> 男性的骶骨比女性的更长、更窄且更为弯曲。

33. *Sacrum (os sacrum);* from the right side (%). **(Median longitudinal section.)**

> 骶骨（os sacrum）；右侧（%）。 （中矢状断面。）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_33_Picture_2.jpeg)

34. Sacrum (os sacrum); superior aspect ( $\frac{3}{5}$ ).

> 骶骨（os sacrum）；上部表面（  ）。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_33_Picture_4.jpeg)

35. Sacrum (os sacrum) ( $\frac{3}{5}$ ). (Horizontal section through the second sacral vertebra.)

> 骶骨（os sacrum）（ ）。（横断面通过第二骶椎。）

36 T H E COCCYX

> 36 尾骨

## 8 THE COCCYX

> 8 尾骨

The coccygeal vertebrae *(vertebrae coccyge&e),* four or five and less frequently three or six in number, fuse in the adult to form the coccyx.

> 尾椎骨（coccygeal vertebrae）在成人中通常融合形成尾骨，数量一般为四至五块，偶尔为三块或六块。

The coccyx *(os coccygis)* (Figs 9, 36 and 37) has the shape of a curved pyramid the base of which faces upwards and the apex downwards. The vertebrae forming it possess only bodies. On each side of the first coccygeal vertebra are the remnants of the superior

> 尾骨（os coccygis）（图9、36和37）呈一弯曲的四棱锥体，其基底朝上，尖端朝下。构成尾骨的椎骨仅具有椎体。在第一尾骨椎的两侧，可见到上位椎骨的残余结构。

articular processes in the form of small projections, coccygeal cornua *(cornua coccygea)>* extending upwards and articulating with the sacral cornua.

> 关节突呈小突起状，尾骨角（尾骨角）向上延伸并与骶骨角形成关节。

The superior surface of the coccyx has a slightly concave area which articulates with the apex of the sacrum by means of the sacrococcygeal joint *(junctura sacrococcygea).*

> 尾骨的上表面有一个略微凹陷的区域，通过骶尾关节（junctura sacrococcygea）与骶骨的尖端相接。

![](_page_34_Picture_7.jpeg)

36. *Coccyx (os coccygis)* (\*/). A—anterior aspect; B—posterior aspect.

> 尾骨（os coccygis）（\*/）。A—前侧；B—后侧。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_34_Picture_9.jpeg)

6 — ischial bone

> 6 — 骶骨

<sup>3—</sup>coccyx

> <sup>3—</sup>尾骨

T H E RIBS 37

> 第37页  
肋骨

## 9 THE RIBS

> 9 肋骨

The ribs *(costae)* (Figs 38-41) are narrow, curved strips of bone differing in length. There are twelve pairs of them arranged symmetrically on both sides of the thoracic segment of the vertebral column.

> 肋骨（costae）（图38-41）是狭长、弯曲的骨条，长度各不相同。共有12对肋骨，对称地排列在椎骨胸段的两侧。

Each rib has a long bony part *(os cosiaU),* a short cartilaginous part, the costal cartilage *(cartilago costalis),* and two ends, an anterior or sternal, and a posterior or vertebral.

> 每根肋骨都有一个长的骨性部分（肋骨体），一个短的软骨性部分，即肋软骨（肋软骨），以及两个端部，分别为前侧的胸骨端和后侧的椎体端。

The bony part of the rib has a head, neck, and a body, or shaft. The head of the rib *(caput costae)* is at the vertebral end of the rib and is a thickened part carrying the articular facet of the head of the rib *(fades articutaris capitis costae).* From the second to tenth rib this facet is separated by a horizontal crest of the head of the rib *(crista capitis costae)* into a smaller, superior, and a larger, inferior, part, each articulating correspondingly with the costal facets of the two adjacent vertebrae.

> 肋骨的骨性部分可分为头、颈和体（或骨干）。肋骨头（caput costae）位于肋骨的椎体端，是一个增厚的部分，其上具有肋骨头关节面（fossa articularis capitis costae）。从第二肋到第十肋，该关节面被肋骨头的水平嵴（crista capitis costae）分为上下两部分：上部较小，下部较大，分别与相邻两个椎骨的肋凹相接。

The narrowest and rounded part of the rib, the neck of the rib

> 肋骨最狭窄且呈圆形的部分，即肋骨颈

*(collum costae)* carries a crest *(crista colli costae)* on its superior border (it is absent on the first and twelfth ribs).

> （肋骨颈）在其上缘具有一个骨嵴（肋骨颈嵴）。（该结构在第一肋骨和第十二肋骨上缺失。）

At the junction with the shaft, the neck of the upper ten ribs has a small tubercle of the rib *(tuberculum costae)^* which has an articular facet of tubercle of the rib *(fades articularis tuberculi costae)*  articulating with the transverse costal facet of the corresponding vertebra.

> 在与骨干连接处，上十肋的肋颈处有一个肋头小结节（肋头小结节，tuberculum costae），其肋头小结节关节面（肋头小结节关节面，facies articularis tuberculi costae）与相应椎骨的横突肋凹形成关节。

The shaft of the rib *(corpus costae)* stretching from the tubercle of the rib to the sternal end, is the longest segment of the bony part of the rib. At some distance from the tubercle the shaft curves considerably to form the angle of the rib *(angulus costae).* The angle coincides only with the tubercle of the first rib, but on the other ribs the distance between these structures increases (down to the eleventh rib); the shaft oF the twelfth rib does not form an angle. The shaft of the rib is flattened along its entire distance and therefore two surfaces can be distinguished: an internal concave and an

> 肋骨的主体部分（肋骨体，corpus costae）从肋头延伸至胸骨端，是肋骨骨性部分中最长的段落。在距肋头一定距离处，肋骨体显著弯曲形成肋角（肋角，angulus costae）。肋角仅与第一肋的肋头相吻合，但在其余肋骨中，这两结构之间的距离逐渐增大（直到第十一肋）；第十二肋的肋骨体不形成肋角。肋骨体在其全长范围内呈扁平状，因此可以区分出两个面：内侧凹面和外侧凸面。

![](_page_35_Picture_9.jpeg)

38. *Right ribs (costae);* superior aspect A—first rib; B —second rib.

> 右侧肋骨（costae）；上侧视图 A—第一肋；B—第二肋。

**38 THE RIBS**

> 38 肋骨

![](_page_36_Figure_1.jpeg)

Tin: RIBS 39

> tin: 肋骨 39

![](_page_37_Picture_1.jpeg)

41. *Right twelfth rib (costa XII);* inner aspect

> 右侧第十二肋（costa XII）；内侧表面

external convex surface; and two borders: a rounded upper and a sharp lower border. A costal groove *(sulcus costae)* (Fig. 39) lodging the intercostal artery, vein, and nerve stretches on the internal surface along the lower border.

> 外侧凸面；以及两条缘：一条上缘为圆形，一条下缘为锐角。一个肋间沟（肋间沟，sulcus costae）（图39）位于内侧面上沿下缘延伸，容纳肋间动脉、静脉和神经。

The shape and position of a rib are as follows: the internal surface is concave, the external surface is convex; the upper and lower borders have the shape of a spiral; the rib itself twists about its long axis.

> 肋骨的形态和位置如下：肋骨的内侧表面凹陷，外侧表面凸起；上下缘呈螺旋状；肋骨本身围绕其长轴旋转。

The anterior end of the bony part of the rib bears a facet with a slightly rough surface which is attached to the costal cartilage.

> 肋骨骨性部分的前端有一个面，表面略粗糙，附着于肋软骨。

The costal cartilages *(cartilagines costales)* are a continuation of the bony parts of the ribs. There are twelve pairs of them also. They become longer gradually from the first to the seventh rib and articulate directly with the sternum. The upper seven pairs of ribs are referred to as true ribs *(costae verae).* The lower five pairs of ribs are called false ribs *(costae spuriae);* the eleventh and twelfth ribs are known as floating ribs *(costae jluctuantes).* The cartilages of the eighth, ninth, and tenth ribs do not reach the sternum directly, but each joins the cartilage of the rib directly above it. The cartilages of the eleventh and twelfth (and sometimes the tenth) ribs do not reach the sternum and their ends He freely in the muscles of the abdominal wall.

> 肋软骨（cartilagines costales）是肋骨骨性部分的延续。它们也有十二对。从第一对肋到第七对肋，肋软骨逐渐变长，并直接与胸骨相连。前七对肋骨被称为真肋（costae verae）。后五对肋骨称为假肋（costae spuriae）；第十一和第十二对肋骨称为浮肋（costae fluctuantes）。第八、第九和第十对肋骨的软骨并不直接连接到胸骨，而是每对分别与上方肋骨的软骨相连。第十一和第十二对（有时也包括第十对）肋骨的软骨并不到达胸骨，其末端在腹壁肌肉中自由游动。

T h e first two and last two ribs possess some specific features. The first rib *(costa I)* (Fig. 38) is shorter but wider than the other ribs and its superior and inferior surfaces lie almost horizontally like the external and internal surfaces of the remaining ribs. The anterior part of the superior surface carries a tubercle for the attachment of the scalenus anterior muscle called the scalene tubercle *(tuberculum musculi scalenx anterioris).* Laterally and posteriorly of the tubercle is a shallow groove for the subclavian artery *(sulcus arteriae subclaviae)* to the back of which is a small roughened surface for insertion of the scalenus inedius muscle *(musculus scalenus rnedius).* To the front and medially of the tubercle is a less distinct groove for the subclavian vein *(sulcus venae subclaviae).* The articular facet on the head of the first rib has no separating crest; the neck is long and thin; the angle of the rib coincides with the tubercle.

> 前两根和后两根肋骨具有一些特殊特征。第一根肋骨（肋骨I）（图38）较短但更宽，其上下面几乎呈水平位，如同其余肋骨的外侧和内侧表面。上表面的前部有一个突起，称为前 scalenus 肌的附着点，称为 scalene 突（musculi scaleni anterioris tuberculum）。该突起的外侧和后方有一个浅沟，为锁骨下动脉（subclaviae 动脉沟）所经过，该沟的后方是一个小的粗糙面，为中 scalenus 肌的附着点（musculus scaleni medius 的附着面）。该突起的前方和内侧有一个较不明显的沟，为锁骨下静脉（subclaviae 静脉沟）所经过。第一根肋骨的头上的关节面没有分隔嵴；颈部较长且较细；肋骨的角与突起位置一致。

On its external surface the second rib *(costa II)* (Fig. 38) has a roughened area, the tubercle for the serratus anterior muscle *(tuberositas musculi serrati anterions)* from which this muscle arises.

> 在其外侧表面，第二肋（costa II）（图38）有一个粗糙区域，即前锯肌的起始点（tuberositas musculi serrati anteriors），前锯肌由此起始。

The eleventh and twelfth ribs *(costa XI* ct *costa XII)* (Fig. 41) have articular facets which are not separated by a crest. The angle of the rib, neck, tubercle, and costal groove are poorly marked on the eleventh and absent on the twelfth rib.

> 第十一和第十二肋骨（costa XI 和 costa XII）（图41）其关节面之间没有被嵴分隔。第十一肋骨的肋角、肋颈、肋头和肋沟标记不明显，而第十二肋骨则完全缺失这些结构。

## 10 THE STERNUM

> 10 胸骨

The breast-bone, or sternum (Figs 42-44), is an elongated unpaired bone with a slightly convex anterior surface and a correspondingly concave posterior surface. It occupies part of the anterior wall of the thorax. The manubrium, body, and xiphoid process are distinguished on it. All these parts are joined to one another by layers of cartilage which ossify with age.

> 胸骨（图42-44）是一块细长的单骨，其前表面略呈凸起，后表面则相应地凹陷。它占据了胸廓前壁的一部分。胸骨可分为胸骨柄、胸骨体和剑突三个部分。这三部分之间由软骨层相连，随着年龄增长，这些软骨层会逐渐骨化。

The manubrium sterni is the widest part of the sternum, thick in its upper part and thinner and narrower below. It bears on the upper border the jugular notch *(incisura jugularis)* which is easily palpated through the skin. Lateral to it on each side is the clavicular notch *(incisura clavicularis),* the place of articulation of the sternum with the sternal end of the clavicle.

> 胸骨柄是胸骨最宽的部分，其上部较厚，下部则较薄且狭窄。胸骨柄上缘有颈静脉切迹（jugular notch，incisura jugularis），可通过皮肤轻易触及。在颈静脉切迹两侧各有一锁骨切迹（clavicular notch，incisura clavicularis），为胸骨与锁骨胸骨端的关节部位。

![](_page_38_Figure_2.jpeg)

A little lower on the lateral border is the costal notch for the ' first rib *(incisura costalisl)* providing for articulation with the cartilage of the first rib. Still lower is a small depression, the upper part of the notch for the second rib. The lower part of this notch is on the body of the sternum.

> 在侧缘稍下方是第一肋骨的肋凹（incisura costalis I），用于与第一肋骨软骨形成关节。再往下一点是一个小凹陷，为第二肋骨的肋凹上部。该肋凹的下部位于胸骨体上。

The body of the sternum *(corpus sterni),* though narrower, is almost three times longer than the manubrium. It is shorter in females than in males.

> 胸骨体（corpus sterni）虽然较窄，但其长度几乎约为胸骨柄（manubrium）的三倍。女性的胸骨体较男性短。

The anterior surface of the sternum bears poorly marked transverse lines which are traces of fusion during embryonal development.

> 胸骨的前表面可见不明显的横行线，这些线条是胚胎发育过程中骨化融合的痕迹。

The cartilaginous union of the upper border of the body with the lower border of the manubrium is called the manubriosternal joint *(synckondrosis manubriosternalis)* (see Fig. 195). The body and the manubrium meet at an obtuse open to the back angle of the sternum *(angulus sterni).* This projection is on a level with the articulation of the second rib with the sternum and is easily palpated through the skin.

> 身体上缘与锁骨切迹下缘之间的软骨连接称为胸骨柄胸骨关节（胸骨柄胸骨软骨联合）（见图195）。胸骨体与胸骨柄在胸骨后方形成一个钝角（胸骨角）。此突起与第二肋与胸骨的连接处处于同一水平，可通过皮肤轻而易举地触及。

Four complete and two incomplete costal notches *(incisurae cos-*

> 四个完整的和两个不完整的肋骨切迹（incisurae costales）

*tales)* are distinguished on the lateral border of the body. They serve for articulation of the sternum with the cartilages of the second to seventh ribs. One of the incomplete notches is on the top of the lateral border and corresponds to the cartilage of the second rib, while the other is at the bottom of the lateral border and corresponds to the cartilage of the seventh rib. The four complete notches are arranged between them and correspond to the third to sixth ribs.

> 这些凹陷（notches）位于胸骨体的外侧缘。它们用于胸骨与第二至第七肋软骨的连接。其中一个不完整的凹陷位于外侧缘的顶部，对应第二肋软骨；另一个不完整的凹陷位于外侧缘的底部，对应第七肋软骨。位于二者之间的四个完整凹陷对应第三至第六肋软骨。

The areas of the lateral borders between two adjacent costal notches are shaped like crescentic depressions.

> 两相邻肋骨切迹之间的侧缘区域呈新月形凹陷。

The xiphoid process *(processus xiphoideus)* is the shortest part of the sternum and varies in size and shape. Its pointed or blunt apex faces either forwards or backwards and has a bifid end or is perforated in the middle. A half-notch for articulation with the cartilage of the seventh rib is located in the superolateral part of the process.

> 剑突（processus xiphoideus）是胸骨最短的部分，其大小和形状存在变异。其尖端或钝端朝向前方或后方，并且末端可能是双叉状或在中部有穿孔。在剑突的上外侧部分有一半月状凹陷，用于与第七肋软骨的连接。[!note]- 术语说明
“半-notch”在此处译为“半月状凹陷”，指用于与第七肋软骨连接的半圆形凹陷结构，是胸骨剑突的典型特征。

By old age the xiphoid process undergoes ossification and becomes fused with the body of the sternum.

> 随着年龄增长，剑突发生骨化，并与胸骨体融合。

## 11 THE THORACIC CAGE

> 11 胸廓

The thoracic cage *(thorax)* (Figs 44-47, a and b) is formed by the thoracic segment of the spine, the ribs, and the sternum.

> 胸廓（胸腔）（图44-47，a和b）由胸段脊柱、肋骨和胸骨构成。

The thorax has the shape of a truncated cone with the wide base facing downwards and the truncated apex upwards. An anterior, posterior, and lateral walls and an upper (inlet) and lower (outlet) apertures are distinguished in the thorax.

> 胸腔呈截断圆锥形，宽大的基底朝下，截断的顶端朝上。胸腔可区分出前壁、后壁和侧壁，以及上口（入口）和下口（出口）。

The anterior wall is shorter than the other walls and is formed by the sternum and the costal cartilages. It slopes and therefore its lower parts project forwards more than the upper parts. The posterior wall, which is almost vertical, is longer than the anterior one and is formed by the thoracic vertebrae and the parts of the ribs from the heads to the angles.

> 前壁较其他壁短，由胸骨和肋软骨形成。前壁呈倾斜状，因此其下部比上部更向前突出。后壁几乎呈垂直方向，比前壁长，由胸椎和肋骨的头部至角部的肋骨部分构成。

On both sides of the external surface of the posterior wall, between the vertebral spines and the angles of the ribs, there is a groove, the dorsal sulcus, accommodating the deep dorsal muscles. On the internal surface of the wall, between the projecting vertebral bodies and the angles of the ribs, there are grooves, the pulmonary sulci *(sulci pulmonale)* (Fig. 46). The posterior borders of the lungs adjoin these sulci.

> 在椎体棘突与肋骨角之间，后壁的外侧面上有一个沟槽，称为背侧沟（dorsal sulcus），容纳深层的背侧肌。在壁的内侧面上，位于突出的椎体与肋骨角之间，也有沟槽，称为肺沟（pulmonary sulcus，sulci pulmonale）（图46）。肺的后缘与这些沟槽相邻。

The lateral walls are longer than either the anterior or posterior wall. They are formed by the shafts of the ribs and are more or less convex, depending on the individual features.

> 侧壁比前壁或后壁更长。它们由肋骨的骨干形成，其形态在不同程度上呈凸起状，具体取决于个体的特征。

The spaces bounded by two adjacent ribs superiorly and inferiorly, by the lateral border of the sternum anteriorly, and by the vertebrae posteriorly, are called intercostal spaces *(spatia intercos-* *talia).* They are filled by ligaments, intercostal muscles, and membranes.

> 由两根相邻肋骨在上方和下方、胸骨的侧缘在前方、以及椎骨在后方所围成的空间称为肋间隙（spatia intercostalia）。这些间隙内填充有韧带、肋间肌和膜。

The thoracic cavity *(cavum tkoracis)* is bounded by the thoracic walls and has two apertures (inlet and outlet).

> 胸腔（cavum thoracis）由胸壁围成，具有两个开口（入口和出口）。

The inlet of the thorax *(apertura tkoracis superior)* is smaller than the outlet and is bounded by the upper border of the manubrium anteriorly, by the first ribs laterally, and by the body of the first thoracic vertebra posteriorly. It is transversely-oval in shape and lies in a plane sloping from back to front and downwards. The upper border of the manubrium sterni is on a level with the space between the second and third thoracic vertebrae.

> 胸腔的入口（thoracic inlet，apertura thoracis superior）比出口小，其前界为胸骨柄的上缘，侧界为第一肋，后界为第一胸椎体。入口呈横椭圆形，位于由后向前、由上向下倾斜的平面上。胸骨柄的上缘与第二和第三胸椎之间的间隙处于同一水平。

The outlet of the thorax *(apertura tkoracis inferior)* is bounded by the xiphoid process and the costal arch formed by the ends of the false ribs anteriorly, by the free ends of the eleventh and twelfth ribs laterally, and by the lower borders of the twelfth ribs and the body of the twelfth vertebra posteriorly.

> 胸腔的下口（胸腔下口，apertura thoracis inferior）由以下结构围成：前方为剑突和假肋的末端所形成的肋弓；侧方为第11对和第12对肋骨的自由端；后方为第12对肋骨的下缘和第12胸椎的椎体。

At the xiphoid process the costal arch *(arcus costalis)* forms the infrasternal angle *(angulus injrasternalis)* which is open downwards.

> 在剑突处，肋弓（肋骨弓）形成胸骨下角（胸骨下角），该角向下开放。

The thorax of different individuals varies in shape (it may be flat, cylindrical or conical). A narrow thoracic cage is longer than a wide one, its infrasternal angle is more acute, and the intercostal spaces arc wider. The thorax of males is longer, wider, and more conical than that of females. Furthermore the shape of the thorax depends on the individual's age.

> 不同个体的胸廓形状存在差异（可能为扁平型、圆筒型或圆锥型）。胸廓较窄者比胸廓较宽者更长，其胸骨下角更尖锐，肋间隙弧度更宽。男性的胸廓比女性的更长、更宽且更呈圆锥形。此外，胸廓的形状还取决于个体的年龄。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_40_Picture_2.jpeg)

44. Thorax; anterior aspect  $(\frac{2}{5})$ .

> 胸腔；前侧面

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_41_Picture_2.jpeg)

45. Thorax; posterior aspect  $(\frac{2}{5})$ .

> 胸腔；后侧方面

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_42_Picture_2.jpeg)

46. Skeleton of thoracic segment (½). (Relation of ribs to vertebra [fourth] and sternum.)

> 胸段骨架（半剖面）。(肋骨与第四胸椎及胸骨的关系。)

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_43_Picture_2.jpeg)

> 12 胸部（X线片）

- 1-left clavicle
- 2—descending aorta
- 3 hilum oflung
- 4 —pulmonary trunk
- 5—heart
- 6—left lung
- 7—diaphragm (left dome)
- 8—diaphragm (right dome)
- 9—ascending aorta
- 10 —apex of right lung
- 11— arch of aorta
- 12-ribs
- 13 right clavicle
- 14 —right scapula
- 15 —left scapula

> 1—左锁骨  
2—降主动脉  
3—肺门  
4—肺动脉干  
5—心脏  
6—左肺  
7—膈肌（左穹隆）  
8—膈肌（右穹隆）  
9—升主动脉  
10—右肺尖  
11—主动脉弓  
12—肋骨  
13—右锁骨  
14—右肩胛骨  
15—左肩胛骨

# DEVELOPMENT OF BONES AND AGE FEATURES OF THE TRUNK SKELETON<sup>1</sup>

> 骨骼的发育与躯干骨骼的年龄特征<sup>1</sup>

The skeleton develops from the mesenchyme which is poorly differentiated embryonic connective tissue. The bones of the calvaria and those of the face develop in connective tissue (endesmal or intramembranous ossification), others develop in cartilage, perichondrally (later, with the appearance of the periosteum, periosteally) or endochondrally. All these processes begin at the end of the second month of the intrauterine period, when all other types of tissues are already present in the body of the embryo.

> 骨骼来源于分化较差的胚胎结缔组织——间充质。头骨的骨板和面部骨骼是在结缔组织中发育的（膜内成骨或软骨内成骨），其余骨骼则在软骨中发育，通过软骨膜内成骨（后期随着骨膜的出现，也可称为骨膜内成骨）。所有这些过程均始于胚胎宫内期的第二个月末，此时胚胎体内已存在所有其他类型的组织。

The bones forming in connective tissue are known as primary bones and pass through two developmental stages: membranous and bony. Bones developing in cartilage arc called secondary and go through three stages: connective-tissue, cartilaginous, and bony.

> 在结缔组织中形成的骨称为原发骨，其发育经历两个阶段：膜性阶段和骨性阶段。而在软骨中形成的骨称为继发骨，其发育经历三个阶段：结缔组织阶段、软骨阶段和骨性阶段。

In intramembranous ossification, islets of ossification appear at the site of the future bones as condensations of mesenchymal cells (which take part in the formation of fibrous fibres) and a great number of blood vessels. Osteoblasts differentiate from the mesenchymal ceils and produce intercellular substance consisting of osscin and calcium salts. The fibrous fibres are impregnated with this substance and the osteoblasts are embedded in them. After that the i^irubhists develop into mature cells of bony tissue, the ostcocytes. Perichondral (periosteal) ossification occurs in a similar manner at the expense of the cells of the perichondrium (periosteum). Eudochondral ossification takes place through growth of blood vessels from the surrounding mesenchyme into the cartilaginous germs of the bones. Mesenchyme adjoining the forming bone develops into the periosteum. The external layer of the dura mater serves as the periosteum for the internal surface of the skull bones.

> 在膜内成骨过程中，成骨岛在将来骨骼的部位形成，这是间充质细胞（参与纤维组织纤维的形成）和大量血管的聚集。成骨细胞由间充质细胞分化而来，并产生由骨盐和钙盐组成的细胞间物质。纤维组织纤维被这种物质浸染，成骨细胞则嵌入其中。之后，这些成骨细胞逐渐发育为成熟的骨细胞（osteocytes）。软骨膜（骨膜）成骨过程与上述过程类似，由软骨膜（骨膜）细胞完成。软骨内成骨则通过来自周围间充质的血管向骨骼软骨原基生长而发生。与形成中的骨骼相邻的间充质则发育为骨膜。硬脑膜的外层在颅骨内表面起骨膜的作用。

The process of osteogenesis continues with the formation of osteoclasts (bone destroyers) from mesenchymal cells surrounding the vessels.

> 骨生成过程继续进行，随着围绕血管的间充质细胞分化形成破骨细胞（骨破坏细胞）。

Cartilaginous tissue with a great number of ossification nuclei

> 具有大量骨化中心的软骨组织

called primary prevails in the skeleton of a newborn (Fig. 47a). Secondary ossification nuclei appear later. The primary, like the secondary nuclei, appear earlier in girls than in boys. In tubular bones, the ossification nuclei first appear in the central parts of the diaphysis and then in the epiphyscs.

> 称为原发性骨化中心，在新生儿的骨骼中占主导地位（图47a）。继发性骨化中心则稍后出现。原发性骨化中心与继发性骨化中心一样，在女孩中出现的时间早于男孩。在管状骨中，骨化中心最初出现在骨干的中央部分，随后才出现在骺端。

At the end of the second month of the embryonic period the vertebrae (except for the coccygeal vertebrae) have two nuclei in their arch, which form from the fusion of several nuclei, and one main nucleus in the body. In the first year of life, the nuclei of the arch develop dorsally and unite. This process occurs more rapidly in the cervical than in the coccygeal vertebrae. The arches usually fuse by the age of 7 years. An exception is the first sacral vertebra (sometimes the sacral segment does not close until the age of 15-18 years). At the age of 3-6 years, bony union of the nuclei in the arch with the nucleus in the body occurs, first in the thoracic vertebrae.

> 胚胎期第二个月末，椎骨（除尾椎骨外）的椎弓中有两个核，这些核由多个核融合形成，而椎体中则有一个主要核。在出生后第一年内，椎弓的核向背侧发育并相互融合。这一过程在颈椎比在尾椎中发生得更快。椎弓通常在7岁前完成融合。例外情况是第一块骶椎（有时骶段的闭合直到15-18岁才完成）。在3-6岁时，椎弓核与椎体核之间发生骨性融合，首先在胸椎中出现。

Epiphyseal rings appear on the borders of the vertebral body at the age of 8 years in girls and at the age of 10 in boys. The vertebral spines and transverse processes which have additional secondary ossification nuclei on the apices are completely ossified in the pubertal period or a little later.

> 8岁时女孩和10岁男孩的椎体边缘会出现骨骺环。在青春期或稍晚一些的时候，椎体的棘突和横突在尖端处具有额外的次级骨化中心的部位会完全骨化。

The atlas and axis develop in a different manner. The anterior and posterior arches of the atlas fuse to form a single bone at the age of 5-6 years. Before the anterior bony arch forms, an area with its own paired ossification nucleus appears in the cartilaginous germ of the arch and fuses with the axis to form the dens at the age of 4- 5 years. The dens articulates with the internal surface of the anterior arch of the atlas by means of the atlanto-axial articulation.

> 寰椎与枢椎的发育方式不同。寰椎的前弓和后弓在5-6岁时融合形成一块骨。在前部骨弓形成之前，弓的软骨胚中会出现一个具有自己成对骨化中心的区域，在4-5岁时与枢椎融合形成齿突。齿突通过寰枢关节与寰椎前弓的内侧表面相接。

The five sacral vertebrae fuse to form the sacrum quite late, in the 18-25th year of life. The three lower vertebrae begin to fuse from the age of 15, the two upper vertebrae fuse by the age of 25.

> 五块骶骨椎体在18至25岁之间才逐渐融合形成骶骨。其中，下部的三块椎体从15岁开始逐渐融合，上部的两块椎体则在25岁前完成融合。

The rudimentary coccygeal vertebrae arc distinguished by the extremely irregular appearance of the ossification nuclei: in the 2nd-3rd week after birth in the first vertebra, at the age of 4-8 years in the second, at 9-13 years of age in the third, and, finally, at the age of 15 in the fourth vertebra. Their fusion, first the lower and then the upper vertebrae, continues after the age of 30 years.

> 原始的尾椎骨在骨化中心的形态上表现出极不规则的外观：第一椎体在出生后第2-3周出现骨化中心，第二椎体在4-8岁时出现，第三椎体在9-13岁时出现，第四椎体则在15岁时出现。其椎体融合过程，首先从下位椎体开始，随后向上进行，通常在30岁以后继续完成。

<sup>1</sup> In view of the fact that students will study the age [featur.es](http://featur.es) after they are acquainted in detail with the anatomy of a human adult, in this section as well as in other similar sections we dwell only on some age peculiarities. For details of development we refer the reader to a textbook of embryology.

> <sup>1</sup> 考虑到学生在详细了解了成人人体解剖结构之后才会学习年龄特征，因此在本节以及其他类似章节中，我们仅简要介绍一些与年龄相关的特殊性。关于发育的详细内容，请参阅胚胎学教材。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_45_Picture_2.jpeg)

47a. *Bones of trunk* (of a newborn).

> 47a. 躯干骨骼（新生儿）

- 1— second cervical vertebra 4 —sacrum
- 2 —third thoracic vertebra 5 sternum
- 3 —second lumbar vertebra 6 —thorax

> 1— 第二颈椎 4 — 骶骨  
2 — 第三胸椎 5 — 胸骨  
3 — 第二腰椎 6 — 胸腔

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_46_Picture_2.jpeg)

47b. *Sagittal section through lumbar segment of vertebral column of a child* (photograph).

> 47b. 儿童椎骨腰段的矢状断层标本（照片）。

With age the vertebral column as a whole goes through different stages of changes in size and shape. It grows particularly intensively during the first two years of life, it almost doubles in length; until the age of 16 growth in length is slower, after which the spinal column grows again actively and in an adult its length is more than three times the length of a newborn's spinal column. It is believed that until the age of 2 years the vertebrae grow just as intensively as the intervertebral discs, but after the age of 7 the relative si/c of the disc diminishes markedly. The nucleus pulposus contains a lot of water and is much larger in a child than in an adult. The vertebral column of a newborn is straight in the antcroposterior direction. Later, as the result of a number of factors (the effect of the work of muscles, independently maintained sitting posture, the weight of the head, etc.), curvatures form in it. Th e cervical curvature (cervical lordosis) forms in the first 3 months of life. The thoracic curvature (thoracic kyphosis) appears by the age of 6-7 months, the lumbar curvature (lumbar lordosis) is expressed sufficiently by the end of the first year of life.

> 随着年龄的增长，整个椎骨列在大小和形态上经历不同的变化阶段。在生命前两年，椎骨列生长特别迅速，其长度几乎增加一倍；到16岁以前，长度增长速度减缓，之后脊柱再次积极生长，成年后的脊柱长度是新生儿脊柱长度的三倍以上。人们认为，到2岁以前，椎体的生长速度与椎间盘一样迅速，但到7岁以后，椎间盘的相对比例明显减少。髓核含有大量水分，儿童的髓核比成人要大得多。新生儿的脊柱在前后方向上是直的。后来，由于多种因素（肌肉活动的影响、自主保持的坐姿、头部的重量等），脊柱中形成弯曲。颈椎曲度（颈椎前凸）在出生后前3个月内形成。胸椎曲度（胸椎后凸）在6-7个月时出现，腰椎曲度（腰椎前凸）在出生后第一年结束时已充分表现出来。

The ribs are laid down as mesenchyme which lies between the muscle segments and is then replaced by cartilage. Pcrichondral ossification of the ribs begins from the second month of the intrautcrine period, cndochondral ossification occurs some time later. The bone tissue in the shaft of the rib grows anteriorly, the ossification nuclei appear in the region of the angle and head at the age of 15-20 years-

> 肋骨最初由位于肌肉节段之间的中胚层组织（mesenchyme）形成，随后被软骨替代。肋骨的软骨内成骨作用（perichondral ossification）始于胚胎期的第二个月，而骨化作用（endochondral ossification）则稍晚发生。肋骨骨干的骨组织向前生长，15至20岁左右，在肋骨的角部和头部区域出现骨化中心。

The anterior ends of the upper nine ribs are joined on both sides by cartilaginous sternal bands which, approaching one another first in the upper and then in the lower parts, unite to form the sternum. This process takes place in the 3rd-4th month of in-Irauterine life. The sternum contains primary ossification nuclei for the manubrium and body and secondary nuclei for the clavicular notches and the xiphoid process. Ossification occurs irregularly in the different parts of the sternum. In the manubrium, for instance, the primary ossification nucleus appears in the 6th intrauterine month, the parts of the body fuse by the 10th year of life and unite finally by the age of 18. The xiphoid process often remains cartilaginous even though a secondary ossification nucleus appears in it by the age of 6 years. The sternum as a whole ossifies at the age of 30 to 35 years, sometimes still later or even not at all.

> 上九根肋骨的前缘在两侧通过软骨性胸骨韧带相连，这些韧带在上部首先相互靠近，随后在下部逐渐融合，形成胸骨。这一过程发生在胎儿生命内的第3～4个月。胸骨内含有胸骨柄和胸骨体的原发性骨化中心，以及锁骨切迹和剑突的继发性骨化中心。胸骨的不同部分骨化过程是不规则进行的。例如，在胸骨柄中，原发性骨化中心在胎儿生命内的第6个月出现；胸骨体的各部分在生命第10年时开始融合，并最终在18岁左右完全融合。剑突常常在6岁之前仍保持软骨性，尽管此时已出现继发性骨化中心。整个胸骨一般在30～35岁左右完成骨化，有时甚至更晚，甚至终生不骨化。

The thorax, formed by twelve pairs of ribs, twelve thoracic vertebrae, and the sternum together with the articular-ligamentous apparatus, goes through a scries of developmental stages. The development of the lungs, heart, and liver, and the position of the body (lying down, sitting, walking) alter in respect to age and function and thus cause changes in the thorax. The main parts of the thorax (the dorsal sulci, lateral walls, upper and lower apertures, costal arch, and infrasternal angle) alter during the various periods of development acquiring each time more and more features of the thorax of an adult. It is believed that the development of the thorax occurs in four main periods: from birth to the age of 2 years it develops very intensively; in the second stage, between the ages of 3 and 7, its development is quite rapid, though slower than in the first period; the third stage, from 8 to 12 years of age, is characterized by rather slow development; the fourth stage, the pubertal period, is also marked sometimes by intensive development. After this, slow growth continues to the age of 20-25 and then stops.

> 胸腔由十二对肋骨、十二块胸椎以及与之相连的肋软骨和胸骨共同构成，并伴随着关节-韧带装置形成一系列发育阶段。肺、心脏和肝脏的发育以及身体姿势（如平躺、坐位、站立）会随着年龄和功能的变化而改变，从而引起胸腔形态的变化。胸腔的主要部分（包括背侧沟、侧壁、上下开口、肋弓和胸骨下角）在不同的发育阶段中逐渐发育，每次发育都越来越接近成年胸腔的特征。人们认为胸腔的发育可分为四个主要阶段：从出生到2岁，胸腔发育非常迅速；在第二阶段，即3至7岁期间，胸腔发育相当快，但比第一阶段稍慢；第三阶段，即8至12岁，胸腔发育则较为缓慢；第四阶段即青春期，有时也会出现较剧烈的发育。此后，胸腔的生长速度逐渐减缓，持续到20至25岁后停止。

# THE BONES OF THE HEAD

> 头骨的骨骼

## 1 *Ossa capitis*

> 1 颅骨骨（头骨）

The bones of the cranium *(ossa cranii)* and the bones of the face parts of the alimentary tube and respiratory tract. gans).

> 颅骨的骨（ossa cranii）、食管和呼吸系统部分的面部骨（gans）。

The skull *(cranium)* (Figs 48-53) is the skeleton of the head. The facial bones form the framework of the face and the initial

> 头骨（颅骨）（图48-53）是头的骨架。面部骨构成面部的框架以及初始的[!note]- 术语说明
“initial”在此处可能指“最初的”或“起始的”，根据上下文，此处应理解为“面部的初始结构”，即面部的基本框架。

*(ossajaciei)* are distinguished in it. The skull is the receptacle for Both parts of the skull are composed of individual bones the brain and organs of sense (visual, acoustic, and olfactory or- joined to one another by means of sutures *(suturae)* and cartilagi-

> (ossajaciei) 在其中有所区别。头骨是容纳脑和感觉器官（视觉、听觉和嗅觉）的容器。头骨的两个部分均由单独的骨组成，并通过骨缝（suturae）和软骨（cartilagi）相互连接。

![](_page_48_Picture_5.jpeg)

48. *Skull (cranium)\* anterior aspect

> 头骨（颅） / 前部表面

nous articulations, or joints (synchondroses), which allow no movement. The lower jaw is an exception, its joint is movable.

> 骨连接（软骨连接，synchondroses）不允许任何运动。下颌骨是一个例外，其关节是可动的。

The occipital, parietal, frontal, sphenoid, temporal, and ethmoid bones are topographically referred to the bones of the cranium; the bones of the face are the inferior nasal conchae, the lacrimal and nasal bones, the vomer, the maxilla, the palatine and zygomatic bones, the mandible, and the hyoid bone.

> 枕骨、顶骨、额骨、筛骨、颞骨和蝶骨在解剖学上统称为颅骨骨；面部的骨包括下鼻甲、泪骨和鼻骨、犁骨、上颌骨、腭骨和颧骨、下颌骨以及舌骨。

The following bones are related to the bones of the cranium

> 以下骨骼与颅骨骨骼相关

(ossa cranii) in terms of its development: unpaired bones: occipital (os occipitale), sphenoid (os sphenoidale), frontal (os frontale), ethmoid (os ethmoidale), vomer; paired bones: temporal (os temporale), parietal (os parietale), inferior nasal concha (concha nasalis inferior), lacrimal (os lacrimale), nasal (os nasale).

> （颅骨）按其发育情况可分为：不成对骨：枕骨（os occipitale），蝶骨（os sphenoidale），额骨（os frontale），筛骨（os ethmoidale），犁骨（vomer）；成对骨：颞骨（os temporale），顶骨（os parietale），下鼻甲（concha nasalis inferior），泪骨（os lacrimale），鼻骨（os nasale）。

51

> 51

The bones of the face (ossa faciei) are the paired maxilla, palatine (os palatinum), and zygomatic (os zygomaticum) bones and the unpaired mandible (mandibula) and hyoid bone (os hyoideum).

> 面部骨骼（ossa faciei）包括成对的上颌骨（maxilla）、腭骨（os palatinum）和颧骨（os zygomaticum），以及不成对的下颌骨（mandibula）和舌骨（os hyoideum）。

## 3 THE BONES OF THE CRANIUM

> 3 颅骨的骨骼

## 4 THE OCCIPITAL BONE

> 4 颧骨

The occipital bone *(os occipitale)* (Figs 54-56) is an unpaired bone forming the posteroinferior part of the skull. Its external surface is convex, the internal (cerebral) surface is concave. Its antcroinferior part contains the foramen magnum *(foramen occipitale magnum)* by means of which the cranial cavity communicates with the vertebral canal. On the basis of development, the following four parts surrounding foramen magnum are distinguished in the occipital bone: the basilar part *(pars basilaris)* situated in front of the foramen magnum, paired condylar parts *(paries laterales),* and the squamous part *(squama occipitalis)* situated to the back of the foramen.

> 枕骨（os occipitale）（图54-56）是一块不成对的骨，构成颅骨的后下方部分。其外表面呈凸面，内表面（脑面）呈凹面。其前下方部分包含枕骨大孔（foramen magnum，foramen occipitale magnum），通过该孔颅腔与椎管相交通。根据发育情况，枕骨围绕枕骨大孔可分为以下四个部分：位于枕骨大孔前方的基底部（pars basilaris），成对的髁状部（paries laterales），以及位于枕骨大孔后方的鳞部（squama occipitalis）。

The basilar part *(pars basilaris)* is short, thick, and quadrangu-

> 基底部分（pars basilaris）较短、较厚，呈四方形。

lar in shape. Its posterior border is free, smooth, and slightly tapered, and forms the anterior border of the foramen magnum. Its anterior border is thick and rough and articulates with the body of the sphenoid bone by means of cartilage to form the spheno-occipital joint *(synckondrosis sphenooccipitalis).*

> 呈卵圆形。其后缘游离、光滑且略呈锥形，构成枕骨大孔的前缘。其前缘厚而粗糙，通过软骨与蝶骨体相接，形成蝶枕关节（蝶枕软骨联合）。

In adolescence the cartilage is replaced by bone tissue and both bones fuse to form a single bone. The superior surface ol the basilar part facing the cranial cavity is smooth and slightly concave. Together with the part of the sphenoid bone situated in front of it, the basilar part forms the clivus facing the foramen magnum (it lodges the medulla oblongata, the pons, and the basilar artery and its branches). On the middle of the inferior external, slightly

> 青春期时，软骨被骨组织取代，两骨融合形成一块单一的骨。基底部的上表面朝向颅腔，表面光滑且略呈凹陷。基底部与位于其前方的蝶骨部分共同构成枕骨大孔（foramen magnum），该结构容纳延髓、脑桥和基底动脉及其分支。在基底部的下外侧中部，略呈...

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_54_Picture_8.jpeg)

54. *Occipital bone (os occipitale);* outer aspect (%).

> 枕骨（os occipitale）；外侧表面（%）

![](_page_55_Picture_2.jpeg)

55. *Occipital bone (os occipitale);* inner aspect (%),

> 枕骨（os occipitale）；内侧表面（%）

convex, surface oi the basilar part are a small pharyngcal tubercle *(tuberculum pharyngeum)*— the site of attachment of the anterior longitudinal ligament and the fibrous membrane of the pharynx called pharyngobasilar fascia *(fascia pkaryngobasilaris),* and rough lines marking the insertion of the rectus capitis anterior and longus capitis muscles.

> 基底部的凸面有一个小的咽突（咽突起，tuberculum pharyngeum）——这是前纵韧带和称为咽基筋膜（咽基筋膜，fascia pharyngobasilaris）的咽部纤维膜的附着处，同时还可见到粗糙的线纹，标志着头直肌前头肌（rectus capitis anterior）和长头肌（longus capitis）的附着点。

The external slightly uneven border of the basilar part and condylar parts of the occipital bone adjoin the posterior margin of the petrous part of the temporal bone. Between them is the petrooccipital fissure *(fissura petrooccipitalis),* which in an unmaccralcd skull is filled with cartilage forming the petro-occipital joint *(synchondrosis petrooccipitalis)* that as a remnant of the cartilaginous skeleton ossifies with age.

> 枕骨基底部和髁部的外侧略不平整的边缘与颞骨岩部的后缘相连。二者之间为岩枕裂（fissura petrooccipitalis），在未闭合的颅骨中，该裂隙被软骨填充，形成岩枕关节（synchondrosis petrooccipitalis）。该关节作为软骨性骨架的遗迹，随着年龄增长会逐渐骨化。

The condylar parts *(paries lateralcs)* of the occipital bone are slightly elongated, thickened in the posterior parts and narrowed a little in the anterior parts. They form the lateral borders of the foramen magnum and fuse anteriorly with the basilar part and posteriorly with the squamous part.

> 枕骨的髁部（外侧部）略呈延长状，后部增厚，前部稍变窄。它们构成枕大孔的外侧边界，前方与基底部融合，后方与鳞部融合。

On the external border of the internal (cerebral) surface (Fig. 55) is a narrow groove for the inferior petrosal sinus *(sulcus sinus petrosi inferioris)* which meets the inferior border of the petrous part of the temporal bone and joins the similarly named groove on the temporal bone to form a sort of canal in which the venous inferior petrosal sinus *(sinus petrosus inferior)* lies.

> 在大脑(脑)表面的内侧（即脑的内侧面）的外侧缘（图55），有一条狭窄的沟槽，称为下岩窦沟（sinus petrosi inferioris sulcus），它与颞骨岩部的下缘相接，并与颞骨上的同名沟槽相连，形成一个类似管道的结构，其中容纳着下岩窦（静脉窦，sinus petrosus inferior）。

On the inferior, external, surface of cither condylar part is an elongated oval in shape and convex articular process, the occipital condyle *(condylus occipitalis);* both condyles with the articular surface converge anteriorly but diverge posteriorly; they articulate with the superior facets of the atlas. To the back of the occipital condyle is a condylar fossa *(fossa condylaris)* lodging the posterior condylar emissary vein *(vena emissaria condylaris).*

> 在每一侧髁状部的下方和外侧表面，有一个呈椭圆形且凸起的关节突，称为枕髁（occipital condyle，condylus occipitalis）；两侧的枕髁关节面向前会合，向后分开；它们与寰椎的上关节面相接。枕髁的后方有一个髁状窝（fossa condylaris），内容纳后髁孔静脉（vena emissaria condylaris posterior）。

The external border of the condylar part bears a large jugular

> 髁状突部的外侧缘有一条较大的颈动脉孔 [!note]- 术语说明
"jugular" 在此处指“颈动脉”，而非“颈静脉”。在解剖学中，“jugular” 通常指颈动脉（jugular artery），但有时也可能指颈静脉（jugular vein）。根据上下文，此处应译为“颈动脉”。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_56_Picture_2.jpeg)

56. *Occipital bone (os occipitale);* from the right side (%).

> 枕骨（os occipitale）；右侧（%）。

notch *(incisura jugularis)* with smooth edges. A small intrajugular process *(processus intrajugularis)* projects on the notch.

> 颈静脉切迹（incisura jugularis）边缘光滑。颈静脉切迹内有一小的内颈突（processus intrajugularis）。

The jugular notch of the occipital bone and the jugular notch of the petrous pari of the temporal bone fonn the jugular foramen *(foramen jugulare).*

> 枕骨的颈静脉切迹与颞骨岩部的颈静脉切迹共同构成颈静脉孔（foramen jugulare）。

The intrajugular processes of both bones separate this foramen into two parts: a larger posterior part lodging the upper bulb of the internal jugular vein *(bulbus superior vena jugularis internae),* and a smaller anterior part transmitting the glossopharyngeal, vagus, and accessory nerves *(nervi glossopharyngeus, vagus* et *accessorius).*

> 两骨的椎体间孔将此孔分为两部分：较大的后部容纳颈内静脉上膨部（bulbus superior vena jugularis internae），较小的前部则穿行舌咽神经、迷走神经和副神经（nervi glossopharyngeus, vagus et accessorius）。

The jugular notch is bounded by the jugular process *(processus jugularis)* posteriorly and laterally. On the external surface of ils base is a small paramastoid process *(processus paramastoideus)* into which the reetus eapitis lateralis muscle is inserted.

> 颈静脉切迹由颈静脉突（processus jugularis）的后外侧界所围成。在颈静脉切迹基底的外侧面有一个小的乳突前突（processus paramastoideus），该处为头夹肌（musculus reetus capitis lateralis）的附着点。

A wide sigmoid groove *(sulcus sinus sigmoidei)* stretches behind the jugular process on the internal surface of the skull; it is a continuation of the sigmoid groove of the temporal bone. A smooth jugular tubercle *(tuberculum jugulare)* is located anteriorly and medially.

> 一个宽大的 sigmoid 沟（sulcus sinus sigmoidei）延伸在颅骨内面的颈动脉突（jugular process）后方；它是颞骨 sigmoid 沟的延续。一个光滑的颈静脉结节（tuberculum jugulare）位于其前方和内侧。

To the back of and downwards from the jugular tubercle, be-

> 在颈静脉结节的后方及下方。

tween the jugular process and the occipital condyle the hypoglossal canal *(canalis nervi hypoglossi)* passes through the bone; it transmits the hypoglossal nerve.

> 在舌骨突与枕骨大孔之间，舌下神经管（canalis nervi hypoglossi）穿过颅骨；它传递舌下神经。

The squamous part of the occipital bone *(squama occipitalis)*  forms the posterior border of the foramen magnum and makes up the greater part of the occipital bone. This is a wide triangular curved plate with a concave internal (cerebral) surface and a convex external surface.

> 枕骨鳞部（squama occipitalis）构成枕骨大孔的后缘，并构成了枕骨的大部分。这是一块宽大的三角形弯曲板，其内面（脑面）凹陷，外面凸起。

The edge of the squamous part is separated into two parts: a larger strongly serrated superior part called the lambdoid border *(margo lambdoideus)* which articulates with the occipital border of the parietal bones to form the lambdoid suture *(sutura lambdoidea);*  a smaller less serrated inferior part called the mastoid border *(margo mastoideus)* which unites with the edge of the mastoid process of the temporal bone by means of the occipitomastoid suture *(sutura oedpitomastoidea).*

> 鳞部的边缘分为两部分：较大的、强锯齿状的上部称为 lambdoid 边缘（margo lambdoideus），它与顶骨的枕边相接，形成 lambdoid 矢状缝（sutura lambdoidea）；较小的、锯齿较浅的下部称为 mastoid 边缘（margo mastoideus），通过 occipitomastoid 矢状缝（sutura occipitomastoidea）与颞骨的乳突突起边缘相连。

The external occipital protuberance *(protuberantia occipitalis externa)* (Fig. 54) is in the middle of the external surface of the squama where it is most convex. The protuberance is easily palpated through the skin. Laterally from it diverge paired raised superior nuchal lines *(linca nuckae superiores),* above and parallel to which are encountered accessory highest nuchal lines *(linca nuchae suprcmae).*

> 外枕突（protuberantia occipitalis externa）（图54）位于鳞状骨外表面的中部，此处最为凸起。该突起可通过皮肤轻易触及。从其外侧向外侧发出一对对称的隆起的上项线（linea nuchae superiores），在其上方且平行于上项线的位置可遇到副最高项线（linea nuchae supercmae）。

The external occipital crest *(crista occipitalis externa)* descends from the external occipital protuberance to the foramen magnum.

> 外枕骨粗隆（crista occipitalis externa）自外枕骨隆突向下延伸至枕骨大孔。

In the middle of the distance between the foramen magnum and the external occipital protuberance, the crest gives rise to the inferior nuchal lines *(linca nuckae inferiores),* which diverge laterally and pass to the edges of the squamous part parallel to the superior lines. All these lines mark the insertion of muscles. The surface of the squamous part below the superior nuchal lines is the site of attachment of muscles terminating on the occipital bone.

> 在枕骨大孔与外枕突之间的中点，骨 crest（骨嵴）形成下颈线（inferior nuchal lines，linca nuckae inferiores），这些线条向两侧分开，并平行于上颈线，延伸至鳞部的边缘。所有这些线条均标志着肌肉的附着点。上颈线以下的鳞部表面是肌肉附着的部位，这些肌肉最终附着于枕骨。

The cerebral surface *(fades cerebralis)* of the squamous part

> 大脑表面（fades cerebralis）的鳞部

bears the eminentia cruciata *(eminentia crudformis)* in the middle of which is the internal occipital protuberance *(protuberantia oedpitalis intcrna)* (Fig. 55). It corresponds to the external occipital protuberance on the external surface.

> 承载着十字隆起（粗隆形十字隆起）（图55），其中央为内枕隆起（内枕突）（protuberantia occipitalis interna）。它对应于外表面的外枕隆起（外枕突）。

The cruciate eminence gives rise to a groove for the transverse sinus *(sulcus sinus transversi)* passing laterally on either side, an ascending superior groove for the sagittal sinus *(sulcus sinus sagittalis superioris),* and an internal occipital crest *(crista occipitalis intcrna)*  descending to the posterior semicircumference of the foramen magnum.

> 十字韧带隆起处形成一个沟槽，供横窦（横窦沟）通过，该沟槽向两侧横向延伸；还有一个向上方的上矢状窦沟（上矢状窦沟），以及一个内侧枕骨隆起（内侧枕骨嵴），该嵴向后方延伸至枕骨大孔的后半周。

Processes of the dura mater with the venous sinuses embedded in them are attached to the borders of the transverse and sagittal grooves and the internal occipital crest.

> 硬膜的突起与其中嵌入的静脉窦相连，附着于横沟和矢状沟的边缘以及内枕骨隆起。

#### 4.1.1 T H K FARI I TA L BON E

> 4.1.1 胸椎体 / 胸椎骨
