## 8 THE BONES OF THE LEG

> 8 下肢的骨胳

The tibia, located medially, and the fibula, located laterally, are the bones of the leg (Fig. 160).

> 胫骨位于内侧，腓骨位于外侧，二者是下肢的骨骼（图160）。

#### 8.1.1 **T HE TIBIA**

> 8.1.1 胫骨

The tibia (see Figs 143, 159-163, 165) is a long tubular bone. It has a body (shaft) and two ends, upper and lower.

> 胫骨（见图143、159-163、165）是一根长管状骨。其结构包括一个骨干（体）和两个端部，即上端和下端。

The body (shaft) of the tibia *(corpus tibia)* is trihedral. It has three borders: anterior, interosseous (lateral), and medial, and three surfaces: medial, lateral, and posterior.

> 胫骨（tibia）体（shaft）呈三棱柱状。其有三个缘：前缘、骨间缘（外侧）和内侧缘，以及三个面：内侧面、外侧面和后面。

The anterior border *(margo anterior)* is tapered like a ridge and in the upper part of the bone is continuous with the tubercle of the tibia *(tuberositas tibiae).* The interosseous border *(margo interosseus)*  is sharp and faces the interosseous border of the fibula. The medial border *(margo medialis)* is rounded. The medial, or anteromedial surface of the bone *(fades medialis)* is slightly convex. Like the anterior border limiting it the medial surface can be easily felt through the skin.

> 前缘（前缘）呈脊状隆起，向骨的上部逐渐变细，并与胫骨粗隆（胫骨粗隆）连续。骨间缘（骨间缘）锐利，朝向腓骨的骨间缘。内侧缘（内侧缘）呈圆弧状。骨的内侧或前内侧表面（内侧面）略呈凸起。如同前缘所限，内侧表面可通过皮肤轻易触及。

The lateral, or anterolateral surface of the bone *(fades lateralis)*  is slightly concave. The posterior surface *(fades posterior)* is flat and bears the soleal **line** *(linea museuli soUa)* running from the lateral condyle downwards and medially. Below it is the nutrient foramen *(foramen nutridum)* leading into the distally stretching nutrient canal *(canalis nutridus).*

> 骨的外侧或前外侧表面（fades lateralis）略呈凹陷。其后表面（fades posterior）为平坦，承载着从外侧髁向下并向内侧延伸的腘线（linea musculi solei）。其下方为滋养孔（foramen nutritivum），通向远端延伸的滋养管（canalis nutritivus）。

The **upper,** or proximal end of the tibia *(extremitas superior* s. *epiphysis proximatis tibiae)* is expanded. Its sides are called the medial condyle *(condylus medialis)* and the lateral condyle *(condylus lateralis).* The lateral aspect of the lateral condyle carries a flat fibular articular surface *(fades articularis Jibularis).* In the middle of the proximal surface of the upper end of the bone is the intercondylar eminence *(eminentia intercontiylaris).* Two tubercles arc distinguished in it, the medial intercondylar tubercle *(tuberculum intercondylare mediate)* to the back of which is the posterior intercondylar area *(area intercondylaris posterior)* and the lateral intercondylar tubercle *(tuberculum intercondylaris laterale)* in front of which is the anterior intercondylar area *(area intercondylaris anterior).* Both areas give attachment to the cruciate ligaments of the knee. To both sides of the intercondylar eminence the superior articular surface of the bone *(fades articularis superior)* carries two concave articular surfaces, medial and lateral, corresponding to the condyles. These surfaces are limited on the periphery by the infra-articular border of the tibia.

> 胫骨的上端（即近端，extremitas superior s. epiphysis proximatis tibiae）呈膨大状。其两侧称为内侧髁（condylus medialis）和外侧髁（condylus lateralis）。外侧髁的外侧面上有一个平坦的腓骨关节面（facies articularis fibularis）。在骨的上端近侧表面中部，有一个髁间隆起（eminentia intercondylaris）。此隆起上可区分出两个隆起，即内侧髁间隆起（tuberculum intercondylare mediale），其后方为后髁间区（area intercondylaris posterior）；外侧髁间隆起（tub,erculum intercondylare laterale），其前方为前髁间区（area intercondylaris anterior）。这两个区域分别附着于膝关节的十字韧带。在髁间隆起的两侧，骨的上关节面（facies articularis superior）上具有两个凹陷的关节面，分别为内侧和外侧，与髁相对应。这些关节面的外围由胫骨的关节下缘所限制。

The lower, or distal **end** of the tibia *(extremitas inferior* s. *epiphysis distalis tibiae)* is quadrangular. On its lateral surface is the fibular notch *(incisura jibularis)* which receives the lower end of the fibula. The groove for the tibialis posterior muscle *(sulcus malleolaris)* is situated on the posterior surface of the distal end of the bone. In front of the groove the medial border of the distal end of

> 胫骨的下端（即远端，extremitas inferior s. epiphysis distalis tibiae）呈四方形。其外侧面上有腓骨切迹（incisura fibularis），容纳腓骨的远端。在骨的远端后面上有胫后肌沟（sulcus malleolaris）。在该沟的前方，胫骨远端的内侧缘处...

![](_page_155_Picture_11.jpeg)

160. *Right tibia andfibula\*  anterior aspect (%).

> 右胫骨和腓骨前侧（%）

THE TIBIA

> 胫骨

![](_page_156_Picture_2.jpeg)

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_156_Picture_3.jpeg)

161. Right tibia; posterior aspect  $(\frac{2}{5})$ .

> 右胫骨；后侧方面

162. Right tibia; lateral aspect  $(\frac{2}{5})$ .

> 右胫骨；外侧方面

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_157_Picture_2.jpeg)

163. *Proximal end of right tibia;* superior aspect (%

> 右胫骨近端；上部表面 (%)

the tibia is continuous downwards with the prominent medial malleolus *(malleolus medialis)* which is easily felt through the skin. The lateral surface of the malleolus is occupied by the articular

> 胫骨向下延续为明显的内侧踝突（内踝），可通过皮肤轻易触及。踝突的外侧表面为关节面。

facet *(fades articularis malleoli)* which extends to the inferior surface of the bone where it is continuous with the concave inferior articular surface *(fades articularis inferior tibiae)* (Fig. 165).

> 关节面（外踝关节面）延伸至骨的下表面，在此处与凹陷的下关节面（胫骨下关节面）连续（图165）。

#### 8.1.2 **T HE FIBULA**

> 8.1.2 腓骨

The fibula (see Figs 143, 159, 160, 164, 165) is a long and thin tubular bone. It has a body and two ends, upper and lower.

> 腓骨（见图143、159、160、164、165）是一块细长的管状骨。它具有一个骨干和两个端部，分别为上端和下端。

The body (shaft) *(corpusfibulae)* is trihedral, prismatic in shape. It is twisted about the longitudinal axis and curved to the back. The three surfaces of the bone, the lateral *(facies lateralis),* anterior (facies medialis), and posterior *(facies posterior),* are separated one from another by three borders, or crests. The anterior border *(margo anterior)* is the sharpest and separates the lateral surface from the medial one; the **medial** crest *(crista medialis)* is between the posterior and medial surfaces of the bone. The posterior surface carries a nutrient foramen *(foramen nutridum)* leading into a distally running nutrient canal *(canalis nutridus).* The interosseous

> 骨干（shaft）（corpus fibulae）呈三棱柱状。其围绕纵轴发生扭转，并向后弯曲。骨的三个面——外侧面（facies lateralis）、前面（facies medialis）和后面（facies posterior）——由三个缘或嵴分隔开。前缘（margo anterior）最为锐利，将外侧面与内侧面分开；内侧嵴（crista medialis）位于骨的后面与内侧面之间。骨的后面有一个营养孔（foramen nutridum），其通向向远端延伸的营养管（canalis nutridus）。骨间膜（interosseous membrane）...

border *(margo interosseus)* is on the anterior surface of the bone.

> 骨的前表面有骨间缘（margo interosseus）。

The upper, or proximal end of the fibula *(extremitas superior* s. *epipkysis proximalisfibulae)* forms the head *(caput fibulae).* The upper end of the head tapers and is called the styloid process of the fibula or the apex of the head *(apex capitis fibulae).*

> 腓骨的上端（即近端，extremitas superior s. epiphysis proximalis fibulae）形成腓骨头（caput fibulae）。腓骨头的上端逐渐变细，称为腓骨的骨突（styloid process of the fibula）或腓骨头的尖端（apex capitis fibulae）。

The lower, or distal end of the fibula *(extremitas inferior* s. *epipkysis distalis fibulae)* forms the lateral malleolus *(malleolus lateralis).*  The lateral surface of the malleolus is easily felt through the skin. The medial surface bears an articular facet of the lateral malleolus *(fades articularis malleoli)* by means of which the fibula articulates with the lateral surface of the talus, and above the facet is a rough area for articulation with the fibular notch of the tibia.

> 腓骨的下端（extremitas inferior s. epiphyse distalis fibulae）形成外踝（malleolus lateralis）。外踝的外侧表面可通过皮肤轻易触及。外踝的内侧表面具有一个关节面（facies articularis malleoli），通过该关节面，腓骨与距骨的外侧面相接；在该关节面之上，还有一个粗糙区域，与胫骨的腓切迹形成关节。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_158_Picture_2.jpeg)

164. *Right fibula* (%). A—anterior aspect; B — posterior aspect; C—medial aspect.

> 右腓骨 (%). A—前侧；B—后侧；C—内侧。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_159_Picture_2.jpeg)

165. *Distal ends of right tibia and fibula;*  inferior aspect *(%).*

> 右胫骨和腓骨远端；下侧（%）

## 9 THE BONES OF THE FOOT

> 9 足部的骨骼

The bones forming the tarsus of the foot (see Figs 143, 166-179) are as follows: the talus, calcaneum, navicular bone, cuboid, and the three cuneiform bones (medial, intermediate, and

> 构成足部跗骨的骨（见图143、166-179）如下：距骨、跟骨、舟骨、骰骨以及三块楔骨（内侧、中间和外侧楔骨）。

lateral). The metatarsus is formed of five metatarsal bones. The phalanges of the digits are named like those of the hand.

> 外侧。足骨由五块跖骨组成。足趾的指骨命名方式与手指的指骨相同。

#### 9.1.1 T HE TARSAL BONES

> 9.1.1 跗骨

The tarsal bones *fossa tarsi)* (see Figs 143, 166-170) are arranged in two groups: proximal, formed by the talus and calcaneum, and distal, composed of the navicular, cuboid, and three cuneiform bones. The bones of the tarsus articulate with the leg bones, the distal row articulates with the metatarsals.

> 距骨窝（tarsal bones fossa tarsi）（见图143、166-170）可分为两组：近侧组由距骨和跟骨组成，远侧组由舟骨、骰骨及三块楔骨组成。距骨组与小腿骨相接，远侧行则与跖骨相接。

#### 9.1.2 T H E TALUS

> 9.1.2 距骨

The talus (Fig. 171) is the only bone of the foot that articulates with the leg bones. Its posterior part, the body *(corpus tali),* is continuous forwards with a constricted part called the neck *(collum tali).* The neck joins the body with the anteriorly directed head of the talus *(caput tali).* The bones of the leg embrace the talus from above and on the sides like a fork. A joint forms between the leg bones and the talus, for which purpose the upper surface of the body of the talus bears an articular pulley-shaped area called the trochlea *(trocklea tali).* On the sides of the body arc the lateral and medial mallcolar facets —the malleolar facet of the lateral surface and the malleolar facet of the medial surface of the talus *(fades malleolaris lateralis* et *fades malleolaris medialis).* The surface of the trochlea is convex sagittally and concave transversely.

> 距骨（图171）是足部唯一与小腿骨形成关节的骨。其后部为距骨体（corpus tali），向前与一个狭窄部分相连，称为距骨颈（collum tali）。距骨颈连接距骨体与向前突出的距骨头（caput tali）。小腿骨从上方及两侧包绕距骨，形似叉状。小腿骨与距骨之间形成关节，为此，距骨体的上表面具有一个呈轮辐状的关节面，称为距骨 trochlea（trochlea tali）。距骨体的两侧为外侧和内侧跟距关节面——即距骨外侧表面的跟距关节面（facies malleolaris lateralis）和距骨内侧表面的跟距关节
[!note]- 术语说明
“fades malleolaris lateralis et fades malleolaris medialis” 应译为“跟距关节面”，其中“fades”为“facies”的误写，此处应理解为“面”或“面状结构”。

The lateral and medial malleolar facets are flat. The lateral malleolar facet continues onto the superior surface of the lateral tubercle of the talus *(proussus lateralis tali)* located in the lower part of the lateral surface of the bone. On the posterior surface of the talus passes obliquely a groove for the flexor hallucis longus tendon *(sulcus tendinis musculi jlexoris halluds longi).* The groove divides the posterior border of the bone into two tubercles, a larger medial tubercle *(tuberculum mediate)* and a smaller lateral tubercle *(tuberculum laterale).* Both tubercles and the groove between them form the posterior tubercle of the talus *(processus posterior tali).* Sometimes the lateral tubercle of the posterior process occurs as a separate os trigonum.

> 距骨的外侧和内侧骨髁面均为平坦状。外侧骨髁面延续至距骨外侧突（外侧距骨突，proussus lateralis tali）的上表面，该外侧突位于骨的外侧表面下部。在距骨的后表面，有一条斜行的沟槽，为趾长屈肌腱通过的沟（趾长屈肌腱沟，sulcus tendinis musculi flexoris hallucis longi）。该沟将距骨的后缘分为两个骨突，较大的为内侧骨突（内侧距骨突，tuberculum mediate），较小的为外侧骨突（外侧距骨突，tuberculum laterale）。这两个骨突及其间的沟槽共同构成距骨的后突（距骨后突，processus posterior tali）。有时，距骨后突的外侧骨突可单独形成一个三角骨（os trigonum）。

In the posterolateral part the lower surface of the body carries

> 在椎体的后外侧部分，椎体的下面承载着

a concave posterior calcancan facet *(fades articularis calcanea posterior).* The anteromedial parts of this surface are bounded by the groove of the talus *(sulcus tali)* which stretches forwards and laterally. In front and lateral of this groove is the middle calcanean facet *(fades articularis calcanea media},* in front of which is the anterior calcanean facet *(fades articularis calcanea anterior).*

> 一个凹陷的后跟关节面（后跟关节面 posterioris）（fades articularis calcanea posterior）。该表面的前内侧部分由距骨沟（sulcus tali）所界定，该沟向前方和外侧延伸。距骨沟的前方和外侧为中跟关节面（fades articularis calcanea media），中跟关节面的前方为前跟关节面（fades articularis calcanea anterior）。

By means of the facets the lower part of the talus articulates with the calcaneum. The head of the talus *(caput tali)* is slightly compressed from top to bottom. Its anterior part carries a spherical navicular facet *(fades artteularis navicularis)* for articulation with the navicular bone.

> 通过关节面，距骨的下部与跟骨相接。距骨头（caput tali）自上而下略呈压缩状。其前部具有一个球状的舟骨关节面（fossa articularis navicularis），与舟骨形成关节。

#### 9.1.3 T H E CALCANEUM

> 9.1.3 跟骨

The heel bone, or calcaneum *(calcaneus* s. *os calcis)* (Figs 172-174) is below and to the back of the talus. Its posteroinferior part is called the posterior surface of the calcaneum *(tuber calcanei).*  The inferior parts of this surface on the lateral and medial sides are continuous, respectively, with the lateral tubercle of the calcaneum *(processus lateralis tuberis calcanei)* and the medial tubercle of the calcaneum *(processus medialis tuberis calcanei).*

> 跟骨，或称跟骨（calcaneus s. os calcis）（图172-174）位于距骨的下方和后方。其后下方部分称为跟骨的后表面（跟骨结节，tuber calcanei）。该表面的下部在侧方和内侧分别与跟骨的外侧结节（跟骨结节外侧突，processus lateralis tuberis calcanei）和跟骨的内侧结节（跟骨结节内侧突，processus medialis tuberis calcanei）相连。

The anterior surface of the calcaneum carries a saddle-shaped facet for the cuboid *(fades articularis cuboidea)* for articulation with the cuboid bone.

> 跟骨前表面有一个鞍形关节面（fades articularis cuboidea）与骰骨形成关节。

In the anterior part of the medial surface of the calcaneum is a short and thick process called the sustentaculuin tali. A groove for the flexor hallucis longus tendon *(sulcus tendmis musculijlexoris halluds longi)* passes on its lower surface.

> 在跟骨内侧面上部有一个短而粗的突起，称为跟骨支撑突（sustentaculum tali）。足底长屈肌腱沟（flexor hallucis longus tendon sulcus，sulcus tendinis musculi flexoris hallucis longi）位于其下表面。

On the lateral surface of the calcaneum, in the anterior part, is

> 在跟骨的外侧面上，前部区域是

a small peroneal tubercle *(trochlea fibularis)* to the back of which stretches the groove for the tendons of the peroneus muscles *(sulcus tendinus musculi fibularium).*

> 一个小型的腓骨结节（腓骨滑车，trochlea fibularis），其后方延伸出腓肌肌腱沟（腓肌肌腱沟，sulcus tendinus musculi fibularium）。

A large posterior facet for the talus *(fades articularis talaris posterior)* is located in the middle of the upper surface of the calcaneum. To the front of it is a groove of the calcaneum *(sulcus calcanei)* running from back to front and laterally. Still more to the front, on the medial border of the bone, are two articular surfaces. One is the middle facet for the talus *(fades articularis talaris media)*  and the other, anterior to it, is the anterior facet for the talus *(fades articularis talaris anterior),* which fit into the respective facets on the talus. When the talus is fitted to the calcaneum the anterior parts of the groove of the talus and the groove of the calcaneum form a pit called the sinus tarsi which is felt through the skin as a small depression.

> 距骨的后关节面（fades articularis talaris posterior）位于跟骨上表面中部。其前方为一自后向前、向侧方延伸的跟骨沟（sulcus calcanei）。更靠前方，在骨的内侧缘有二个关节面。一个是距骨的中关节面（fades articularis talaris media），另一个位于其前方，为距骨的前关节面（fades articularis talaris anterior），它们分别与距骨上的相应关节面相接。当距骨与跟骨相接时，距骨沟的前部和跟骨沟共同形成一个窝，称为跟窦（sinus tarsi），可通过皮肤触及到一个微小的凹陷。

#### 9.1.4 THF. NAV1CU.AR HU M

> 9.1.4 THF. NAV1CU.AR HU

The navicular bone *(os naviculare)* (Fig. 175) is flat anteriorly and posteriorly. On its posterior surface it carries a concave facet for articulation with the navicular facet of the head of the talus. The upper surface of the bone is concave. The anterior surface bears three facets by means of which it articulates with the three cuneiform bones. The facets are separated from one another by small ridges.

> 舟骨（os naviculare）（图175）前后均为扁平状。其后表面具有一个凹陷面，与距骨头的舟骨凹面形成关节。骨的上表面为凹陷状。前表面有三个关节面，通过这三个关节面与三块楔骨相接。这三个关节面之间由细小的嵴分隔。

On the lateral surface of the body is a small facet for articulation with the cuboid bone. The lower surface of the navicular bone is concave. In its medial part is the tuberosity of the navicular bone *(tuberositas ossis navicularis)* which can be easily felt through the skin.

> 在骨体的外侧面上有一个小的关节面，与骰骨形成关节。舟骨的下面呈凹陷状。在其内侧部分有一个舟骨结节（tuberositas ossis navicularis），可通过皮肤轻易触及。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_161_Picture_2.jpeg)

166. Bones of right foot (ossa pedis) (%). (Dorsal surface.)

> 右足骨（ossa pedis）（%）。 （背侧表面）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_162_Picture_2.jpeg)

167. *Bones of right foot (ossa pedis)*  (Plantar surface.)

> 右足骨（ossa pedis）（足底面）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_163_Picture_2.jpeg)

> 右足10块骨（X线片）

- 2-ulm

- 4 lube rosily of [iuvnul.ii](http://iuvnul.ii) bone

- 13—fifth metatanal bone 14 — lateral cuneiform bone
- 15—tubercle of fifth melatarsal bone
- 16—cuboid bone
- 17—fibula

2 — 腕骨  
3 — 跟骨

7 — 中间楔骨  
8 — 第一跖骨  
9 和 10 — 趾骨

13 — 第五跖骨

15 — 第五跖骨粗隆  
16 — 舟骨

## 11 T H E CUNEIFORM BONES

> 11 楔形骨

The cuneiform bones *(ossa cuneiformia)* (Figs 176-178) are three in number and wedge-shaped. They are located in front of (he navicular bone. The intermediate cuneiform bone is shorter than the other bones as a result of which the anterior (distal) surfaces of these bones are at different levels. They have facets for articulation with the respective metatarsal bones.

> 楔骨（ossa cuneiformia）（见图176-178）共有三块，呈楔形。它们位于舟骨的前方。中间楔骨较短，因此这些骨的前方（远端）表面处于不同的水平。它们具有与相应跖骨关节的关节面。

The base of the wedge (the wider part) of the medial cuneiform bone faces downwards, the bases of the intermediate and lateral bones face upwards.

> 楔骨（ medial cuneiform bone ）的楔形底部（较宽部分）朝下，中间骨和外侧骨的底部朝上。

The posterior surfaces of the bones have facets for articulation with the navicular bone.

> 骨的后表面有 facet 与舟骨形成关节。

The medial cuneiform bone *(os cunciforme mediate)* has two facets on its lateral concave surface for articulation with the intermediate cuneiform bone and the second metatarsal bone.

> 内侧楔骨（os cuneiforme mediate）其外侧凹面有两个关节面，分别与中间楔骨和第二跖骨形成关节。

The intermediate cuneiform bone *(os cuneiform\* intermedium)*  has a facet on the medial surface for articulation with the medial cuneiform bone and a facet on the lateral surface for union with the lateral cuneiform bone.

> 中间楔骨（os cuneiformis intermedium）* 的内侧面上有一个关节面，与内侧楔骨形成关节；外侧面上有一个关节面，与外侧楔骨相连。

The lateral cuneiform bone *(os cuneiform\* lateralc)* carries facets on the medial surface for articulation with the intermediate cuneiform bone and base of the second metatarsal bone, and a facet on the lateral side for articulation with the cuboid bone.

> 外侧楔骨（os cuneiforme laterale）在内侧面上具有与中间楔骨及第二跖骨基底部相接的关节面，外侧面上则具有与骰骨相接的关节面。

#### 11.1.1 T H E CUBOID BONE

> 11.1.1 跟骨

The cuboid bone *(os cuboideum)* (Fig. 179) is placed outwardly to the lateral cuneiform bone, in front of the calcaneum, and to the back of the bases of the fourth and fifth metatarsal bones.

> 骰骨（os cuboideum）（图179）位于外侧楔骨的外侧，位于跟骨的前方，并位于第四和第五跖骨基底部的后方。

The upper surface of the bone is rough, the medial surface has facets for articulation with the lateral cuneiform and the navicular bones. The lateral edge of the bone bears the tuberosily of the cuboid bone *(tuberositas oisis cuboidei)* (see Fig. 169) which faces downwards. In front of it arises the groove for the tendon of the peroneus longus muscle *(sulcus tendinis musculus peronei longi)* (see Fig. 167) which passes over to the lower surface of the bone and crosses it obliquely from the posterolateral to the [anteromcdi.il](http://anteromcdi.il)  areas, according to the passage of this tendon.

> 骨的上表面粗糙，内侧表面有凹面与外侧骨丘（外侧骨块）和舟骨形成关节。骨的外侧缘承托着立方骨的粗隆（cuboid bone tuberosity，tuberositas cuboidei）（见图169），该粗隆朝向下方。其前方有腓骨长肌腱沟（sulcus tendinis musculus peronei longi）（见图167），该肌腱沟延伸至骨的下表面，从后外侧方向斜穿至前内侧区域，根据该肌腱的走行路径。

T h e posterior surface of the bone bears a facet for articulation with a similar facet on the calcaneum.

> 骨的后表面有一个关节面，与跟骨上的相似关节面相接。

The anterior surface of the cuboid bone has facets which are separated by a ridge. They serve for articulation with the fourth and fifth metatarsal bones.

> 距骨前表面有多个面，被一条嵴分隔开。这些面与第四和第五跖骨形成关节。

![](_page_167_Picture_15.jpeg)

175. *Right navicular bone (os naviculare)* (%). A—posterior aspect; B — anterior aspect.

> 右舟骨（os naviculare）（%）。A—后侧；B—前侧。

![](_page_168_Picture_2.jpeg)

176. *Right medial cuneiform bone (os cuneiforme mediate)* (%) A —medial aspect; B —lateral aspect.

B

179. *Right cuboid bone (os cuboideum)*  A—lateral aspect; B —medial aspect; C —posterior aspect.

#### 11.1.2 T H E METATARSAL BONES

> 11.1.2 趾骨骨块

The metalarsal bones *(ossa mttatarsalia)* (Figs 143, 166-170) are five thin tubular bones located in front of the tarsus. Each metalarsal bone has a body or shaft *(corpus)\** the posterior, proximal end called the base *(basis)\** and an anterior, distal end called the head *(caput).*

> 金属距骨（ossa metatarsalia）（图143，166-170）是位于距骨前方的五块细长的管状骨。每块金属距骨具有一个主体或骨干（corpus），其后方、近端端称为基底（basis）*，而前方、远端端称为头（caput）*。

The bones are numbered from the medial side of the foot, from the big toe to the little toe. The first metatarsal bone is shorter but thicker than the others. The second metatarsal is the longest. The bodies of the metatarsals are trihedral. The dorsal surface of the shaft is slightly convex, the two inferior plantar surfaces meet downwards to form a ridge.

> 足部的骨从足内侧开始编号，从大脚趾到小脚趾依次排列。第一跖骨较短但较粗。第二跖骨是最长的。跖骨体呈三棱形。跖骨体的背侧表面略呈凸起，两个足底侧表面向下相接形成一条嵴。

The base is the most massive part of a metatarsal. It is wedgeshaped and the expanded part of the first to fourth metatarsals faces upwards, while that of the little toe faces medially. The sides of the bases carry facets for articulation of the metatarsals with one another.

> 足底骨是跖骨中最粗大的部分。其形态呈楔形，第一至第四跖骨的底面朝上，而第五跖骨（小趾侧）的底面朝内。跖骨底的两侧具有关节面，用于与其他跖骨形成关节。

The posterior surfaces of the bases have facets for union with the tarsal bones. On the inferior surface of the base of the first metatarsal bone is a tubercle of the first metatarsal bone *(tuberositas ossis metatarsalis I).* The lateral surface of the base of the fifth metatarsal bone also has a tubercle *(tuberositas ossis mttatarsalis V)* which can be easily felt through the skin. The anterior ends, or heads, of the metatarsal bones arc flattened on the sides. The peripheral parts of the heads bear spherical articular surfaces for articulation with the phalanges of the toes. The inferior surface of the head of the first metatarsal bone carries on each side a small smooth area for the sesamoid bones *(ossa sesamoidea)* of the big toe. The head of the first metatarsal is easily fell through the skin.

> 足底骨（跖骨）基底部的后表面具有凹面，与跗骨相接。第一跖骨基底部的下表面有一个第一跖骨结节（tuberositas ossis metatarsalis I）。第五跖骨基底部的外侧表面也有一个结节（tuberositas ossis metatarsalis V），可通过皮肤轻易触及。跖骨的前端（即跖骨头）两侧呈扁平状。跖骨头的外周部分具有球形关节面，与趾骨相接。第一跖骨头的下表面在两侧各有一个小的光滑区域，为大脚趾的籽骨（ossa sesamoidea）所占据。第一跖骨头可通过皮肤轻易触及。

In addition to the sesamoid bones in the first metatarsophalangeal joint, one sesamoid bone is found in the interphalangeal joint of the big toe, and occasional sesamoid bones are found in the peroneus longus tendon on the plantar surface of the cuboid bone.

> 除第一跖趾关节中的籽骨外，大脚趾的趾间关节中也存在一块籽骨，偶尔在距骨跖面的腓骨长肌腱中也可发现籽骨。

#### 11.1.3 T H E BONES OF TH E DIGITS OF TH E FOOT

> 11.1.3 足部指（趾）骨

The bones (phalanges) of the digits of the foot *(ossa digitorum pedis)* (see Figs 143, 16b-170) are similar to those of the hand in shape, number, and interrelationship. Each phalanx has a body (shaft) *(corpus pkalangis),* a posterior (proximal) end, or base *(basis pkalangis),* and an anterior (distal) end, or head *(caput pkalangv,).*  The heads of the proximal and middle phalanges are pulleyshaped.

> 足部指(趾)骨（足底指骨，ossa digitorum pedis）（见图143、16b-170）在形状、数量及相互关系上与手部指骨相似。每块指骨具有一个骨干（体，corpus phalangis）、一个后方（近侧）端，即基底（basis phalangis），以及一个前方（远侧）端，即头（caput phalangis）。近侧和中间指骨的头部呈滑轮状。

The distal end of each distal phalanx carries a tuberosity *(tuberositas pkalangis distalis).*

> 每根远节指骨的远端均携带一个隆起（tuberositas phalangis distalis）。

# DEVELOPMENT AND AGE FEATURES OF THE BONES OF THE LOWER LIMB

> 下肢骨骼的发育与年龄特征

The bones of the lower limbs develop as secondary bones (see Figs 3, 179a). The hip bone develops from three primary centres (or points) of ossification and several (up to eight) secondary (accessory) centres. The primary centres give rise to the ilium (which appears on the third month of the intrauterine period), the ischium (on the fourth month) and the pubis (on the fifth month ol intrauterine life); the secondary centres give origin to eminences, depressions, and borders of individual bones. In the region of the acelabulum the three bones are at first joined to one another by layers of cartilage in which secondary ossification centres appear later (by the age of 16-18 years). All centres fuse at 20-25 years of age. The pelvis as a whole undergoes changes in size and shape for the most part. However, sexual features characteristic of male and female adults begin differentiation from the age of 8-10 years. The pelvis is higher in boys but wider in girls.

> 下肢的骨发育为次级骨（见图3、图179a）。髋骨由三个初级骨化中心（或点）和若干个（最多八个）次级（副）骨化中心形成。初级骨化中心分别形成髂骨（在宫内期第三个月出现）、坐骨（在宫内期第四个月出现）和耻骨（在宫内期第五个月出现）；次级骨化中心则形成各骨的突出部、凹陷部和边缘。在髋臼区域，这三个骨最初由软骨层连接，之后在16～18岁左右出现次级骨化中心。所有骨化中心在20～25岁时融合。整个骨盆在大小和形状上发生显著变化。然而，男性和女性成年特征的分化从8～10岁开始。男孩的骨盆较高，而女孩的骨盆较宽。

The femur develops from five ossification centres one of which is primary (diaphyseal) and four are secondary. The primary centre (which appears at the beginning of the second intrauterine month) gives rise to the shaft of the bone. The secondary centres of ossification appear in different periods: the centre for the distal femoral epiphysis appears at the end of the intrauterine period; an ossification centre appears in the cartilaginous femoral head at the end of the first or beginning of the second year; the third centre ai ises in the cartilage of the greater trochanter at the age of 3, and the fourth appears in the cartilage of the lesser trochanter at the age of 8 years. All these bone structures fuse with the shaft of the bone between the ages of 16 and 20.

> 股骨由五个骨化中心形成，其中一个是原发性（骨干部），其余四个为继发性。原发性骨化中心（在胎儿第二个月初出现）形成骨的骨干。继发性骨化中心出现的时间不同：远端股骨骺的骨化中心在胎儿期结束时出现；股骨头软骨中的骨化中心在第一年末或第二年初出现；第三个骨化中心在3岁时出现在大转子的软骨中；第四个骨化中心则在8岁时出现在小转子的软骨中。所有这些骨结构在16至20岁之间与骨干融合。

The patella forms in cartilage from a single ossification centre in the second year in girls and in the fourth year in boys and ossification is completed by the age of 16-20.

> 髌骨在女孩中于第二年、男孩中于第四年从一个骨化中心开始在软骨中形成，骨化过程在16-20岁完成。

The tibia develops from four ossification centres. One is diaphyseal and appears in the second month of intrauterine life. Three ossification centres are secondary among which the upper epiphyseal point arises in the ninth month of intrauterine life, the lower epiphyseal centre during the first year of life, and the ossification centre for the tuberosity of the tibia appears at the age of

> 胫骨由四个骨化中心发育形成。其中一个是骨干的，出现在胎儿期的第二个月。另外三个为次级骨化中心，其中近端骨骺点在胎儿期第九个月出现，远端骨骺中心在出生后第一年出现，胫骨粗隆的骨化中心则在年龄达到 [!note]- 术语说明
“tuberosity of the tibia” 通常译作“胫骨粗隆”，是胫骨近端的一个显著隆起部位。

13. All centres fuse with the shaft at different periods ranging from 16-18 to 20-24 years or age.

> 所有中心在16-18至20-24岁不等的年龄段与骨干融合。

T h e fibula develops from three ossification centres. The primary (diaphyseal) centre appears in the middle of the second month of intrauterine life and gives origin to the shaft and areas of the epiphysis. The other two are secondary, epiphyseal, centres; the lower one arises in the first year of life and the upper centre, at the age of 3-5 years. The lower epiphyseal centre of ossification fuses with the shaft at 17-20 and the upper ossification centre at 19-21 years of age.

> 腓骨由三个骨化中心发育形成。初级（骨干）骨化中心在胎儿宫内生活第二个月的中段出现，负责形成骨干及骺部的大部分区域。另外两个为次级（骺部）骨化中心；下部骺骨化中心在出生后第一年内形成，上部骺骨化中心则在3-5岁时形成。下部骺骨化中心通常在17-20岁时与骨干融合，上部骺骨化中心则在19-21岁时与骨干融合。

The bones of the foot develop in the following manner. The talus arises from one ossification centre which appears in the last months of intrauterine life; ossification continues to the age of 8 years.

> 足部骨骼的发育方式如下。距骨起源于一个骨化中心，该中心在胎儿晚期开始出现，骨化过程持续到8岁。

The calcaneum forms from two ossification centres: the main, primary, centre appears on the sixth month of intrauterine life and the secondary centre by the age of 9 years and gives rise to the tuber cakanei. The centres fuse by 16-18 years of age.

> 跟骨由两个骨化中心形成：主要的、初生的骨化中心在胎儿宫内生活第六个月时出现，次级骨化中心在9岁左右出现，并形成跟骨结节。这两个骨化中心通常在16-18岁时融合。

The navicular bone develops from a single ossification centre which appears at the age of 3-5 years.

> 舟骨从一个 ossification 中心发育而来，该中心在 3-5 岁时出现。

Each cuneiform bone develops from its own ossification centre. The lateral bone begins ossification by the end of the first year of life, the intermediate bone at the age of 3, and the medial cuneiform bone at the age of 3-4 years.

> 每一块楔骨均来自其自身的骨化中心。外侧楔骨在生命第一年结束时开始骨化，中间楔骨在3岁时开始骨化，内侧楔骨则在3-4岁时开始骨化。

The cuboid bone develops from one ossification centre which usually appears before birth, less frequently at 3-6 months of age.

> 骰骨由一个骨化中心发育形成，该骨化中心通常在出生前出现，较少见的情况是在出生后3-6个月内出现。

Each of the five metatarsal bones develops from two ossification centres: a main, primary, and an accessory (secondary) centre. The main centre appears in the second to fifth metatarsals at the beginning of the third month of intrauterine life and in the first bone at the end of this month; the secondary centres form by the age of 4 years and fuse by the age of 17 in females and by the age of 20 in males.

> 每一块跖骨均从两个骨化中心发育而来：一个主要的（原发性）中心和一个辅助的（继发性）中心。主要中心在胎儿生命第三个月初出现于第二至第五跖骨，而在第一跖骨则出现在该月末；继发性中心在4岁时形成，并在女性17岁、男性20岁时融合。

Each phalanx develops from two ossification centres. The main centre appears between the third and ninth months of intrauterine life, the secondary centre arises by the age of 4. Fusion occurs between the ages of 15 and 20.

> 每块指骨均来源于两个骨化中心。主要骨化中心在宫内生活第三至第九个月期间形成，次级骨化中心在4岁左右出现。骨融合通常发生在15至20岁之间。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_171_Picture_2.jpeg)

179a. *Bones of right lower limb (ossa membri inferioris) of a newborn.*

> 179a. 右下肢（新生婴儿的下肢骨骼）的骨骼（ossa membri inferioris）

1 — hip bone (os coxae) 4—tibia

> 1 — 髋骨（骨盆骨） 4—胫骨

2^femu r 5 —patella

> 2^femu r 5 —髌骨

3^fibula 6—bones of fool (ossa pcdis)

> 3^腓骨 6—愚人骨（ossa pcedis）

# THE SCIENCE OF THE ARTICULATION OF BONES

> 骨关节的科学

Arthrologia

> 关节学

The articulations, or joints, of bones are separated into two groups: fibrous joints *(articulationes s. juncturaejibrosae)* and synovia! joints *farticulationes synoviaUsJ.* These two types of joints differ in the degree of mobility which they provide for the bones in the skeletal system and in the capacity for bearing mechanical loads.

> 骨骼的关节（articulations 或 joints）可分为两类：纤维性关节（articulationes s. juncturae fibrosae）和滑膜关节（articulationes synoviales）。这两大类关节在它们为骨骼系统提供的活动度以及承受机械负荷的能力方面存在差异。

## 1 FIBROUS JOINTS

> 1 纤维性关节

Fibrous joints *(articulationes* s. *juncturae jibrosae)* (Fig. 180) provide for contiguous (uninterrupted) union of bones by means of various types of connective tissue—dense connective tissue, cartilaginous tissue or bony tissue.

> 纤维性关节（articulationes s. juncturae fibrosae）（图180）通过不同类型的结缔组织（致密结缔组织、软骨组织或骨组织）使骨骼实现连续（不间断）的结合。

The group of fibrous joints formed by dense connective tissue includes syndesmoses, sutures, and gomphoses.

> 由致密结缔组织形成的纤维性关节群包括骨间膜、骨缝和牙周膜。

Syndesmoses are articulations in which the uniting compact connective tissue forms ligaments. For example, the pterygospinous ligament *(ligamentum pterygospinale)* arises from the spine of the sphenoid bone and is attached to the pterygospinous process on the lateral plate of the pterygoid process; the stylohyoid ligament *(ligamentum stylokyoideum),* a thin and long ligament arises from the styloid process, runs downwards and to the front, and is attached to the lesser horn of the hyoid bone, etc. Some ligaments are rich in elastic fibres, e.g. *ligamenta jlava* located between the vertebral arches, *ligamentum nuckae,* etc. Syndesmoses are also formed by wide ligaments uniting bones for a considerable distance; these are the interosseous membranes of the forearm and leg *(membrana interossea antebrackii* et *membrana interossea cruris).* The fontanelles of the skull *(fonticuli cranii),* formed of primary connective tissue, are also included in the group of syndesmoses.

> 关节盘（syndesmoses）是关节的一种类型，其连接的致密结缔组织形成韧带。例如，翼脊韧带（ligamentum pterygospinale）起源于蝶骨翼突的脊部，并附着于翼突的翼脊突上；舌骨舌突韧带（ligamentum stylohyoideum）是一种细长的韧带，起自舌突，向下向前延伸，附着于舌骨的小角。还有一些韧带富含弹性纤维，例如位于椎弓之间的黄韧带（ligamenta flava），以及脐韧带（ligamentum nuchae）等。关节盘也可由连接骨骼较长距离的宽韧带形成，如前臂和下肢的骨间膜（membrana interossea antebrachii et membrana interossea cruris）。头骨的囟门（fonticuli cranii），由原始结缔组织构成，也属于关节盘的范畴。

Sutures *(suturae)* unite the bones of the skull cap and the bones of the face. They are formed by short bands of dense connective tissue running between the borders of neighbouring bones and penetrating them. The sutures ossify with age due to replacement of the compact connective tissue by bony tissue.

> 骨缝（suturae）将颅盖骨各骨和面骨各骨连接在一起。它们由短而密集的致密结缔组织束构成，位于相邻骨的边缘之间并延伸至骨内。随着年龄增长，骨缝因致密结缔组织被骨组织替代而骨化。

According to the outline of the sutures and the ways in which the opposed surfaces of the bones are fitted to each other, the following types of sutures are distinguished: serrated suture *(sutura serrata),* squamous suture *(sutura squamosa),* and flat suture *(sutura plana).* The bones of the skull cap are joined by squamous and serrated sutures. The facial bones mostly articulate by means of flat

> 根据骨缝的形态以及骨面相对接的方式，可将骨缝分为以下类型：锯齿状骨缝（sutura serrata）、鳞状骨缝（sutura squamosa）和平面状骨缝（sutura plana）。头骨盖骨的骨块主要通过鳞状骨缝和锯齿状骨缝相连。面部骨骼大多通过平面状骨缝相接。

sutures which provide accurate and even apposition. Still another type of suture is schindylesis (Gk *schindylesis* splintering) in which a plate of one bone fits into a groove in the other bone, as in the articulation between the sphenoid bone and the vomcr *(sutura sphenovomeriana).*

> 提供准确且均匀对合的缝合。另一种类型的缝合称为schindylesis（希腊语schindylesis，意为“劈裂”），其中一块骨的一片嵌入另一块骨的沟槽中，例如蝶骨与鼻骨之间的关节（蝶鼻缝，sutura sphenovomeriana）。

Gomphoses, or peg-and-socket joints *(articulationes dentoalveolares)* form when the roots of a tooth, which are surrounded by the periodontium, fit into the alveolar bone. The bands of dense connective tissue hold the teeth in the sockets. With age the hold grows weaker and the teeth get loose (this is discussed in detail in Vol.11. *The Digestive System).*

> gomphoses（牙槽突关节，articulationes dentoalveolares）是在牙齿根部被牙周膜包裹并嵌入牙槽骨中时形成的，其结构类似于榫卯关节。致密结缔组织束将牙齿固定于牙槽窝内。随着年龄增长，这种固定作用逐渐减弱，导致牙齿松动（此内容将在第11卷《消化系统》中详细讨论）。

Cartilaginous joints *(articulationes cartilaginae)* are a variety of fibrous joints formed by cartilaginous tissue. These are synchondroses and symphyses.

> 软骨关节（articulationes cartilaginae）是一种由软骨组织形成的纤维性关节，包括软骨联合（synchondroses）和软骨结合（symphyses）。

Synehondroses are formed by continuous plates of cartilage that join the edges of the bones and restrict movements. There are many of them in the skeletal system of children and adolescents in whom they join some of the bones (e.g. the diaphyses with the epiphyses of the long bones, one sacral vertebra with another, etc.). These are temporary synehondroses in which the cartilage is replaced by bone with age. Cranial synehondroses (sphcno-occipital, sphenopetrous, petro-occipital, posterior and anterior intraoccipital) and synchondrosis of the sternum (the articulation between the manubrium and xiphoid process) are permanent synehondroses maintained in the skeletal system of an adult.

> 软骨联接（synehondroses）是由连续的软骨板形成，连接骨的边缘并限制运动。在儿童和青少年的骨骼系统中，这类软骨联接较多，它们连接一些骨骼（例如长骨的骨干与骨骺之间，一个骶椎与另一个骶椎之间等）。这些是暂时性的软骨联接，随着年龄增长，软骨会被骨组织替代。颅部的软骨联接（如枕骨-蝶骨、蝶骨-枕骨、岩骨-枕骨、后内耳道和前内耳道软骨联接）以及胸骨的软骨联接（胸骨柄与剑突之间的连接）则是永久性的软骨联接，在成人的骨骼系统中持续存在。

Symphyses arc formed by a plate of fibrous cartilage which contains a cavity. Such joints exist between the vertebral bodies, namely the intervertebral symphysis, or intervertebral disc *(sym~ pkysis intervertebralis)* (Fig. 181), the manubriosterna! symphysis *(symphysis manubriosternalis)* (Figs 180, 195), and the pubic symphysis *(symphysis pubica)* (Figs 180, 218).

> 关节盘是由一块纤维软骨板构成，其中包含一个腔隙。此类关节存在于椎体之间，称为椎间关节盘，或椎间盘（symphysis intervertebralis）（图181），胸骨柄与胸骨体的关节盘（symphysis manubriosternalis）（图180、195），以及耻骨联合（symphysis pubica）（图180、218）。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_174_Picture_0.jpeg)

180. Fibrous joints (articulationes fibrosae).

> 纤维性关节（articulationes fibrosae）。

- 1 syndesmosis A—ligamentum flava
- B-interosseous membrane of leg (membrana interossea cruris)
- 2 suture (sutura)
- A—serrate suture (sutura serrata)
  B—squamous suture (sutura squamosa)
  C—plane suture (sutura plana)

> 1 纤维连结（syndesmosis）
A—黄韧带（ligamentum flava）
B—小腿骨间膜（membrana interossea cruris）

2 纤维缝（sutura）
A—锯齿状缝（sutura serrata）
B—鳞状缝（sutura squamosa）
C—平面缝（sutura plana）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_175_Picture_2.jpeg)

![](_page_175_Picture_3.jpeg)

<sup>3 —</sup>synchondrosis A—of manubrium sterni (synchondrosis manubriosternalis) B—of xiphoid process (synchondrosis xiphosternalis) C—spheno-occipital (synchodrosis sphenooccipitalis)

> <sup>3 —</sup>软骨联合 A—胸骨柄软骨联合（synchondrosis manubriosternalis） B—剑突软骨联合（synchondrosis xiphosternalis） C—蝶枕软骨联合（synchondrosis sphenooccipitalis）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_176_Picture_0.jpeg)

## 2 180a. *Types of synovial joints* (^).

> 2 180a. 滑膜关节的类型 (^)

- A—spheroid or ball-and-socket joint (articulalio spheroidea s. cotyhca), shoulder joint (articulalio humeri);
- B—variety of ball-and-socket joint, hip joint (articulalio coxae);
- C —variety of hinge joint (ginglymus), talocrural joini (articulatio talocruralis);
- D—ellipsoidal joint (articulatio ellipsoidca), radiocarpal joint (articulatio radiocarpea);
- E—pivot joint (articulatio trochoidea), proximal radioulnar joint (articulatio radioulnaris proximalis);
- F—hinge joint (ginglymus), interphalangeal joint (articulatio interphalangea);
- G —saddle joint (articulatio sellares), carpometacarpal joint of thumb (articulatio carpometacarpea pollicis).

> A—球窝关节（articulatio spheroidea s. cotyhca），肩关节（articulatio humeri）；  
B—球窝关节的一种，髋关节（articulatio coxae）；  
C—铰链关节（ginglymus）的一种，踝关节（articulatio talocruralis）；  
D—椭圆关节（articulatio ellipsoidca），桡腕关节（articulatio radiocarpea）；  
E—旋转关节（articulatio trochoidea），近侧桡尺关节（articulatio radioulnaris proximalis）；  
F—铰链关节（ginglymus），指间关节（articulatio interphalangea）；  
G—鞍状关节（articulatio sellares），拇指腕掌关节（articulatio carpometacarpea pollicis）。

## 3 SYNOVIAL JOINTS

> 3 滑膜关节

Interrupted articulations of bones, or synovial joints *(articulations synoviales)* (Fig. 180a) are the most commonly encountered type of articulations in the human body. They permit free movements. The joint is called simple *(articulatio simplex),* if it is formed by two bones, and compound *(articulatio composite)* if it is formed by three or more bones.

> 骨的中断性关节，或滑膜关节（滑膜关节，articulations synoviales）（图180a）是人体中最常见的关节类型。它们允许自由运动。若关节由两块骨形成，称为简单关节（articulatio simplex）；若由三块或更多骨形成，则称为复合关节（articulatio composite）。

Each synovial joint has compulsory structural elements, in the absence of which union of bones cannot be related to this group of joints, and accessory elements which determine the structural and functional distinctions of a synovia! joint.

> 每个滑膜关节都有必需的结构成分，缺少这些成分，骨骼的联合就无法归属于这一类关节；同时还具有附属成分，这些成分决定了滑膜关节在结构和功能上的特异性。

The compulsory elements of a synovial joint are the articular cartilages *(cartilagincs articulares)* covering the articular surfaces *(fades articulates),* the articular capsule *(capsula articularis),* and the joint cavity *(cavitas articularis).*

> 滑膜关节的必要组成部分包括覆盖关节面（fades articulates）的关节软骨（cartilagines articulares）、关节囊（capsula articularis）以及关节腔（cavitas articularis）。

The articular cartilage is usually hyaline *(cartilago hyalina)* or less frequently fibrous *(cartilagoJibrosa).* It covers the articular surfaces on the opposed parts of the articulating bones. Therefore, one surface of the cartilage is fused with the bone surface which it covers, while the other projects freely into the joint.

> 关节软骨通常是透明软骨（cartilago hyalina），较少情况下为纤维软骨（cartilago fibrosa）。它覆盖在参与关节的两骨相对面的关节面上。因此，软骨的一侧与所覆盖的骨面融合，而另一侧则自由地伸入关节腔。

The articular capsule *(capsula articularis)* encloses the articulating ends of the bones without passing over to the articular surfaces and is continuous with the periosteum of these bones. The capsule is made up of fibrous connective tissue and consists of two layers, or membranes. The outer capsular ligament *(membrana Jibrosa* s. *stratum Jibrosum)* is formed of dense fibrous connective tissue and serves as a mechanical factor. It is continuous with an inner synovial membrane *(membrana synooialis* s. *stratum synoviale)* which forms synovial folds *(plica synovialis)* penetrating between the ends of the articulating bones at places and increasing the congruence of the surfaces forming the joint. This membrane secretes a synovial fluid *(synovia)* into the joint which lubricates the articular surfaces of the bones, provides nourishment for the articular cartilage, acts as a shock absorber, and changes mobility at the joint by changing viscosity. The working surface of the membrane increases not only due to the folds but also due to synovial villi *(oilli synoviales)* projecting into the joint cavity.

> 关节囊（capsula articularis）包裹着骨的关节端，不越过关节面，并与这些骨的骨膜连续。关节囊由纤维结缔组织构成，分为两层或两膜。外层囊韧带（membrana fibrosa s. stratum fibrosum）由致密的纤维结缔组织构成，起着机械作用。它与内层滑膜膜（membrana synovialis s. stratum synoviale）连续，该滑膜膜在骨的关节端之间形成滑膜褶皱（plica synovialis），使关节面更加契合。此膜将滑膜液（synovia）分泌入关节腔，润滑骨的关节面，为关节软骨提供营养，起到缓冲作用，并通过改变黏度来改变关节的活动性。膜的工作面不仅因褶皱而增加，也因滑膜绒毛（villi synoviales）伸入关节腔而增加。

The joint cavity is a closed narrow space limited by the articulating bone surfaces and the articular capsule and is filled with synovia] fluid, The cavity is air-tight.

> 关节腔是一个被关节面和关节囊所限制的密闭狭窄空间，内充满滑膜液。该腔隙是密闭的。

The various accessory structures of the joints arc ligaments *(ligamenfa),* articular discs *(disci articulares),* interarticular fibrocartilages or articular menisci *(menisci articulares),* and articular labra *(labra articularia).*

> 关节的各种辅助结构包括韧带（ligamenta）、关节盘（disci articulares）、关节间纤维软骨或关节半月板（menisci articulares）以及关节唇（labra articularia）。

The ligaments are bands of dense fibrous connective tissue which strengthen the articular capsule and limit or direct movements at the joint. In relation to the articular capsule, the ligaments may be extracapsular *(ligamenta extracapsularia),* intracapsular *(ligamenta intracapsularia),* or capsular *(ligamenta capsularia),*  located in the capsule between the fibrous and synovial membranes. Practically ail joints possess ligaments. The extracapsular ligaments intertwine with the outer parts of the fibrous layer of the capsule; the capsular ligaments are thickenings of this layer; the

> 韧带是由致密结缔组织形成的束状结构，其作用是加强关节囊并限制或引导关节的运动。根据其与关节囊的关系，韧带可分为囊外韧带（ligamenta extracapsularia）、囊内韧带（ligamenta intracapsularia）和囊膜韧带（ligamenta capsularia），位于纤维膜与滑膜之间的关节囊内。实际上，几乎所有关节都具有韧带。囊外韧带与关节囊纤维层的外层交织在一起；囊膜韧带则是该层的增厚部分；[!note]- 术语说明  
“capsular ligaments” 通常译作“囊膜韧带”，指位于关节囊纤维层内的增厚部分，具有加固关节的作用。

intracapsular ligaments are intra-articular in location but are covered by the synovial membrane which separates them from the joint cavity.

> 关节囊内韧带位于关节内，但被滑膜膜覆盖，从而与关节腔分开。

The articular discs are pads of hyaline or fibrous cartilage wedged between the articular bone surfaces. They are attached to the articular capsule and divide the joint cavity into two compartments. The discs increase the congruency of the articular surfaces and, consequently, the range and variety of movements. Besides, thty act as shock absorbers by reducing jolts and shaking during movement. Such discs exist, for instance, in the sternoclavicular and mandibular joints.

> 关节盘是楔夹在关节骨面之间的透明软骨或纤维软骨垫。它们附着于关节囊，并将关节腔分为两个腔室。关节盘增加了关节面的匹配程度，从而扩大了运动的范围和种类。此外，它们还通过减少运动过程中产生的冲击和震动，起到缓冲作用。例如，关节盘存在于胸骨-锁骨关节和下颌关节中。

The articular menisci, or interarticular fibrocartilages *(menisci articulares),* in contrast to the discs, are not complete cartilaginous plates but crescent-shaped structures of fibrous cartilage. Each knee joint has two menisci, right and left, which arc attached by their outer edges to the capsule nearer to the tibia, while their sharp inner edge projects freely into the joint cavity. The menisci provide various movements at the joint and are shock absorbers.

> 关节软骨（interarticular fibrocartilages，menisci articulares），与椎间盘不同，它们不是完整的软骨板，而是呈新月形的纤维软骨结构。每个膝关节有两个软骨，左右各一个，它们的外缘附着于靠近胫骨的关节囊上，而其锐利的内缘则自由地伸入关节腔内。软骨为关节提供多种运动的可能，并起到缓冲作用。

An articular iabrum *(labra articularia)* is formed of dense fibrous connective tissue. It is attached to the edge of the acetabulum or glenoid cavity, making them deeper and increasing the congruency of the surfaces. The lip faces the joint cavity (shoulder and hip joints).

> 关节唇（labra articularia）由致密的纤维结缔组织构成。它附着于髋臼或肩胛盂的边缘，使这些窝更深，并增加关节面的契合度。关节唇面向关节腔（肩关节和髋关节）。

Joints differ in the shape of the articular surfaces and mobility of the articulating bones. According to the shape of the articular surfaces, the following synovial joints are distinguished: spheroid or ball-and-socket *(articulationes spkeroidea* s. *cotylicae),* plane *(articulationes plana),* ellipsoidal or condyloid *(articulationes ellipsoideae* s. *condylares),* saddle *(articulationes- sellares),* ovoid *(articulationes ovoidales),* pivot *(articulationes trockoideae),* hinge *(articulationes ginglymi)*  and bicondylar *(articulationes bicondylaris).*

> 关节在关节面的形状和参与关节的骨的活动性方面有所不同。根据关节面的形状，可将滑膜关节分为以下类型：球窝关节（articulationes spheroidea s. cotylicae），平面关节（articulationes plana），椭圆关节或髁关节（articulationes ellipsoideae s. condylares），鞍状关节（articulationes sellares），卵圆关节（articulationes ovoidales），旋转关节（articulationes trochoideae），铰链关节（articulationes ginglymi）以及双髁关节（articulationes bicondylaris）。

Movements at a joint are determined by the shape of the articular surfaces. Ball-and-socket and plane joints, in which the generatrix is a segment of a circumference, allow movement about three mutually perpendicular axes; transverse, antcroposterior (sagittal), and vertical. For instance, the following movements are possible at the shoulder joint: flexion *(flexio)* and extension *(extensio)* about the transverse axis with the movement occurring in the sagittal plane; abduction *(abductio)* and adduction *(adductio)* about the antcroposterior axis, in which case the movement is made in the frontal plane; and finally, rotation *(rotatio)* about the vertical axis including pronation *(pronatio)* (medial rotation) and supination *(supinatio)* (lateral rotation), with the movement occurring in the horizontal plane. These movements are very limited in plane joints (the flat articular surface in this case is regarded as a small segment of a large-diameter circumference). They occur at a large amplitude in ball-and-socket joints in which circular movement or circumduction *(circumductio)* occurs in addition, with the apex of the circumduction centre corresponding to the centre of the spheroidal joint, while the periphery of the limb describes the base of a cone.

> 关节的运动由关节面的形状决定。球窝关节和平面关节（其中母线为圆周的一部分）允许围绕三个相互垂直的轴进行运动，即横轴、前后轴（冠状轴）和垂直轴。例如，肩关节可以进行以下运动：围绕横轴进行的屈曲（flexio）和伸展（extensio），此时运动发生在冠状面上；围绕前后轴进行的外展（abductio）和内收（adductio），此时运动发生在矢状面上；最后，围绕垂直轴进行的旋转（rotatio），包括前旋（pronatio，即内旋）和后旋（supinatio，即外旋），此时运动发生在水平面上。这些运动在平面关节中受到很大限制（此时平坦的关节面被视为一个大直径圆周的小段）。而在球窝关节中，这些运动可以在较大范围内进行，除了上述运动外，还可进行圆周运动（circumductio），其圆周运动的顶点对应球窝关节的中心，而肢体的外围则描述出一个圆锥的底面。

Joints allowing movement about only two axes are called biax-

> 仅允许围绕两个轴进行运动的关节称为双轴关节。

ial. Such arc the ellipsoid (e.g. the wrist joint) and saddle joints (e.g. the carponuta carpal joint of the thumb).

> 此类关节包括椭圆关节（例如腕关节）和鞍状关节（例如拇指的腕掌关节）。

Uniaxial joints are those with a cylindrical or pulley-shaped articular surface. In a cylindrical (trochoid) joint the generatrix moves parallel to the rotation axis. Such is the median atlantoaxial joint in which the rotation axis passes vertically through the dens of the second cervical vertebra or the superior radioulnar joint. A variety of this type of joint is the hinge joint (ginglimus) in which the generatrix is inclined in relation to the rotation axis (as if bevelled). The humcroulnar and interphalangeal joints serve for illustration.

> 单轴关节是指具有圆柱形或滑轮状关节面的关节。在圆柱形（ trochoid）关节中，母线（ generatrix）平行于旋转轴。例如，中轴寰枢关节即为此类关节，其旋转轴垂直穿过第二颈椎的齿突，或上尺桡关节。此类关节的一种变体是铰链关节（ginglimus），其中母线相对于旋转轴呈倾斜关系（如同斜切）。肱尺关节和指间关节可作为示例。

The bicondylar joints are a variety of the ellipsoid joints. These are ellipsoid joints working in combination with one another (e.g. the atlanto-occipital and the knee joints).

> 双髁关节是椭圆关节的一种。这些椭圆关节相互配合共同发挥作用（例如寰枕关节和膝关节）。[!note]- 术语说明 “双髁关节” 是指具有两个髁状结构的关节，常见于下肢，如膝关节。

The ovoid joints are a variety of plane joints with an ovoid arti cular surface. Basically, flat surfaces are saddle- or ovoid-shaped almost in all cases.

> 椭圆形关节是一种具有椭圆形关节面的平面关节。基本上，平坦表面在大多数情况下呈鞍形或椭圆形。

At some joints of the skeletal system movements occur only in conjunction with movements at the neighbouring joints, i.e. anatomically isolated joints are united by a common function. Such functional combination of joints must be borne in mind in studying their structure and analysing the character of movements.

> 在骨骼系统的某些关节中，运动仅在邻近关节运动的同时发生，即解剖学上孤立的关节通过共同的功能而联合。在研究这些关节的结构和分析运动特性时，必须考虑到这种关节的功能组合。

# ARTICULATIONS OF THE BONES OF THE TRUNK AND THE HEAD

> 躯干和头骨的骨连接

*Juncturae trunci et capitis*

> 关节连接 / 头部连接

## 1 ARTICULATIONS OF THE TRUNK BONES

> 1 躯干骨的关节

## 2 ARTICULATIONS OF THE VERTEBRAL COLUMN

> 2 脊柱关节

These articulations are as follows: (1) intervcrtebral discs unit- processes of the vertebrae.

> 这些关节如下：(1) 椎间盘——椎体的椎体突起。

IV separate vertebrae ;in joined to one another by means of ing adjacent vertebral bodies; (2) zygapophyscal joints uniting various articulations to form the vertebral column *(columna vcrtc-* arches and processes of adjacent vertebrae; (3) ligaments of the *kalis).* vertebral column which stretch between the bodies, arches, and

> (1) 四个分离的椎骨，通过相邻椎体相互连接；  
(2) 关节突关节，将各种关节联合起来形成椎骨列（椎骨弓及椎突等结构）；  
(3) 棘间韧带，这些韧带连接于椎体、椎弓及椎突之间，构成椎骨列。  

[!note]- 术语说明  
“zygapophyscal joints” 通常译作“关节突关节”，是连接相邻椎骨的关节结构。  
“ligaments of the kalis” 应为“棘间韧带”，是连接相邻椎骨棘突的韧带。

#### 2.1.1 **INTERVERTEBRAL DISCS**

> 2.1.1 椎间盘

Intervcrtebral discs (cartilages) are located between the bodies of (wo adjacent vcrtebra'c in the cervical, thoracic, and lumbar segments of the vertebral column.

> 椎间盘（软骨）位于颈椎、胸椎和腰椎段相邻椎体之间。

An inlervertebral disc *(discus intervcrtebralis)* (Figs 181-183) is formed of fibrous cartilage. A peripheral part called the anulus h'brosus and a centrally located nucleus pulposus are distinguished in it

> 椎间盘（discus intervertebralis）（图181-183）由纤维软骨构成。其外周部分称为纤维环（anulus fibrosus），中央部分则为髓核（nucleus pulposus）[!note]- 术语说明
“anulus fibrosus” 通常译作“纤维环”，是椎间盘的外层结构，具有抵抗压力的作用。"nucleus pulposus" 译作“髓核”，是椎间盘的中央部分，呈胶状，具有缓冲作用。

The collagen fibres forming the anulus fibrosus are arranged concentrically, obliquely (one across another), and spirally. Their ends merge with the periosteum of the vertebral bodies. The cenlul part of the disc (nucleus pulposus) is very resilient and is a peculiar elastic layer. In lateral movements of the vertebral column it is displaced to the side of extension. On cross-section of the intervertebral disc ihe nucleus pulposus, which is normally compressed, protrudes above the level of the anulus fibrosus. The intervertebral disc fuses with the hyaline cartilage covering the adjacent surfaces of the vertebral bodies and is similar in shape to these surfaces. There is no disc between the atlas and axis. The thickness of the discs differs and increases gradually towards the lower segments of the vertebral column; the discs of the cervical and lumbar segments are slightly thicker in front than at the back. The discs are much thinner m the middle part of the thoracic segment than in the proximally and distally located parts. The cartilaginous discs account for a quarter of the length of the vertebral column.

> 构成椎间盘纤维环的胶原纤维呈同心圆排列、斜行（相互交叉）和螺旋状排列。这些纤维的末端与椎体的骨膜相融合。椎间盘的中心部分（髓核）非常坚韧，是一种特殊的弹性层。在脊柱的侧向运动中，髓核会向伸展方向的侧方移动。在椎间盘的横断面上，通常被压缩的髓核会突出于纤维环的水平之上。椎间盘与覆盖相邻椎体表面的透明软骨融合，并在形状上与这些表面相似。在寰椎和枢椎之间没有椎间盘。椎间盘的厚度不一，向脊柱下部逐渐增厚；颈椎和腰椎段的椎间盘在前方比后方稍厚。胸椎段中部的椎间盘比其近端和远端部分要薄得多。软骨性椎间盘约占脊柱总长度的四分之一。

## 3 T H E ZYGAPOPHYSEAL **JOINTS**

> 3 节段间关节

The zygapophyseal joints *(junctura zygapopkysealis)* (see Figs process *(proccssusarticularis inferior)* of the next distal vertebra. The 182, 183, 184) form between the superior articular process *(proces-* articular capsule *(capsula articularis)* is attached to the margins of *sus articularis superior)* of one vertebra and the inferior articular the articular cartilage. The joint cavity *fcavum articulare)* is set in

> 椎间关节（zygapophyseal joints，junctura zygapophysealis）（见图182、183、184）由一个椎体的上关节突（processus articularis superior）与下一个椎体的下关节突（processus articularis inferior）形成。关节囊（capsula articularis）附着于关节软骨的边缘。关节腔（cavum articulare）位于关节软骨之间。

![](_page_180_Picture_0.jpeg)

181. *Intervertebral discs (disci intervertebrales);*  anterior aspect

> 椎间盘（disci intervertebrales）；前外侧方面

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_180_Picture_2.jpeg)

181a. *Intervertebral discs of the lumbar segment* (photograph) (Horizontal section through middle of disc.)

> 181a. 腰椎间盘（照片）（椎间盘中部的水平断面）

I — anulus fibrosus

> I — 纤维环

<sup>2—</sup>nucleus pulposus

> <sup>2—</sup>髓核 /no_think

<sup>3 —</sup>cavity of intervertebral disc in the articulation between lumbar and sacral vertebrae

> <sup>3 —</sup>腰骶椎体间椎间盘腔 /no_think

accordance with the position and direction of the articular surfaces and is nearer to the horizontal plane in the cervical segment, to the frontal plane in the thoracic segment, and to the sagittal plane in the lumbar segment.

> 根据关节面的位置和方向，其在颈椎段更接近水平面，胸椎段更接近冠状面，腰椎段更接近矢状面。

The zygapophyseal joints are of the plane type in the cervical and thoracic segments and pivot in the lumbar segment.

> 椎间关节在颈段和胸段为平面型，而在腰段为旋转型。

They are related functionally to joints allowing only a slight range of movements.

> 它们在功能上与仅允许轻微活动范围的关节相关。

The symmetric zygapophyseal joints are combined joints, i.e. those in which movement at one joint inevitably causes movement at the other because both are formed by processes on the same bone.

> 对称的关节突关节是联合关节，即在其中一个关节发生运动时，另一个关节必然随之发生运动，因为这两个关节均由同一骨的突起部分形成。

#### 3.1.1 THE LIGAMENTS OF THE VERTEBRAL COLUMN

> 3.1.1 脊柱韧带

The ligaments of the vertebral column (ligamenta columnae vertebralis) are divided into two groups, the group of long and the group of short ligaments.

> 椎骨的韧带（vertebral column ligaments，ligamenta columnae vertebralis）可分为两组，即长韧带组和短韧带组。

#### 3.1.2 THE LONG LIGAMENTS OF THE VERTEBRAL COLUMN

> 3.1.2 脊柱的长韧带

The long ligaments of the vertebral column are as follows.

> 椎骨的长韧带如下。

1. The anterior longitudinal ligament (ligamentum longitudinale anterius) (see Figs 182-187) runs on the anterior and partly on the lateral surfaces of the vertebral bodies from the anterior tubercle of the atlas to the sacrum where it merges with the periosteum of the first and second sacral vertebrae.

> 前纵韧带（ligamentum longitudinale anterius）（见图182-187）位于椎体的前方及部分侧方，从寰椎的前结节延伸至骶骨，在骶骨的第一、第二骶椎骨膜处与之融合。

In the lower parts of the vertebral column the anterior longitudinal ligament is much wider and stronger; it is joined loosely to the vertebral bodies and firmly with the intervertebral discs because it intertwines with the perichondrium covering them; on the sides of the vertebrae it is continuous with their periosteum. The deep layers of bunches of this ligament are shorter than the superficial layers. As a result they join two adjacent vertebrae, whereas the superficial, longer bunches stretch for the distance of four or five vertebrae. The anterior longitudinal ligament prevents extreme extension of the vertebral column.

> 在椎骨列的下部，前纵韧带较宽且较强；它与椎体连接较松散，而与椎间盘连接紧密，因为该韧带交织于覆盖椎间盘的软骨膜中；在椎体的两侧，该韧带与椎体的骨膜连续。该韧带的深层束比表层束短，因此能连接相邻的椎骨，而表层较长的束则可跨越四到五个椎骨的距离。前纵韧带可防止椎骨列发生过度的伸展。

2. The posterior longitudinal ligament (ligamentum longitudinale posterius) (see Figs 182-184, 187, 188) runs on the posterior surface of the vertebral bodies in the vertebral canal (canalis vertebralis). It arises on the posterior surface of the axis and is continuous with the membrana tectoria at the level of the upper two cervical vertebrae. Inferiorly this ligament stretches to the proximal part of

> 后纵韧带（ligamentum longitudinale posterius）（见图182-184、187、188）位于椎管（canalis vertebralis）内椎体的后表面。它起始于轴椎（atlas）的后表面，并与上两节颈椎处的膜性被覆层（membrana tectoria）相连。向下，该韧带延伸至椎体近端。

![](_page_181_Picture_14.jpeg)

182. Zygapophyseal joint (junctura zygapophysealis); superior aspect (\(^4\)\_5). (Third lumbar vertebra. Articulation between second and third lumbar vertebrae; section.)

> 椎间关节（junctura zygapophysealis）；上部（$^4\_5）。（第三腰椎。第二腰椎与第三腰椎之间的关节；横断面。）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_182_Picture_2.jpeg)

183. Ligaments and joints of vertebral column (ligamenta et juncturae columnae vertebralis); right aspect (\(^4/\_5\)).

> 椎骨的韧带与关节（ligamenta et juncturae columnae vertebralis）；右侧视图（$^4/\_5$）

(Lumbar segment. The vertebral canal is partly opened.)

> （腰段。椎管部分开放。）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_183_Figure_2.jpeg)

184. *Ligaments and joints of vertebral column (ligamenta et juncturae columnae vertebralis);*  posterior aspect (%).

> 椎骨的韧带与关节（ligamenta et juncturae columnae vertebralis）；后侧（posterior aspect）

(Lumbar segment. The arches and processes of twelfth thoracic and first and second lumbar vertebrae are removed.)

> （腰段。第十二胸椎和第一、第二腰椎的椎弓和椎突已被去除。）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_184_Picture_2.jpeg)

185. *Ligaments of vertebral column (ligamenta columnae vertebralis);*  anterior aspect (%).

> 椎骨韧带（ligamenta columnae vertebralis）；前方面（%）

(Lumbar segment. Bodies of first and second lumbar vertebrae removed by frontal section.)

> （腰段。通过矢状面切口移除了第一和第二腰椎体。）

the sacral canal. In contrast to the anterior longitudinal ligament, the posterior ligament is wider in the upper than in the lower parts of the vertebral column. It is fused firmly with the intervertebral discs and is slightly wider here than at the level of the vertebral

> 骶管。与前纵韧带不同，后纵韧带在椎骨柱的上部比下部更宽。它牢固地与椎间盘融合，并且在此处的宽度略大于椎体水平处。

bodies. It is joined loosely to the bodies of the vertebrae from which it is separated by a layer of connective tissue lodging a venous plexus. Like in the anterior longitudinal ligament, the superficial bundles are longer than the deep ones.

> 椎体。它松散地附着于其所属椎体的椎体上，二者之间被一层结缔组织分隔，该结缔组织内含有静脉丛。与前纵韧带类似，其表层束比深层束更长。

#### 3.1.3 T H E SHORT LIGAMENTS OF TH E VERTEBRAL COLUMN

> 3.1.3 脊柱短韧带

The short ligaments of the vertebral column are as follows.

> 椎骨的短韧带如下。

1. The yellow ligaments *(ligamenta jlava)* (see Figs 182, 183, 185) fill the spaces between the vertebral arches from the axis to the sacrum. They stretch from the inner surface and lower border of the vertebral arch to the external surface and upper border of the arch of the next lower vertebra, and their anterior edges limit the intervertebral foramen from the back.

> 黄韧带（ligamenta flava）（见图182、183、185）填充从枢椎至骶骨之间各椎骨椎弓之间的间隙。它们从上位椎骨椎弓的内面和下缘延伸至下位椎骨椎弓的外面和上缘，其前缘构成椎间孔的后界。

The ligamenta flava are made up of vertically running elastic bands lending them a yellow colour. They are strongest in the lumbar segment. The ligamenta flava are very resilient and clastic as a result of which they become shorter in extension of the trunk and act like muscles, holding the trunk in extension and relieving the tension of the muscles. In flexion they stretch and in this way also relieve the tension of the erector muscle of the spine (see *Muscles of the Back).* There are no ligamenta flava between the atlas and the axis; a connective-tissue atlanloaxial membrane is stretched here whose anterior margin limits posteriorly the intervertebral foramen *(foramen intervertebrale)* transmitting the second cervical nerve.

> 黄韧带由纵向排列的弹性束带构成，因此呈黄色。它们在腰段最为坚韧。由于黄韧带具有高度的弹性和韧性，因此在躯干伸展时，它们会缩短，起类似肌肉的作用，维持躯干的伸展状态，并缓解肌肉的紧张。在躯干前屈时，黄韧带会被拉伸，从而同样起到缓解脊柱竖脊肌紧张的作用（参见《背部肌肉》）。在寰椎与枢椎之间没有黄韧带，此处有一层连接组织构成的寰枢膜，其前缘在后方限制了椎间孔（椎间孔）的范围，椎间孔传递第2颈神经。[!note]- 术语说明
“Ligamentum flavum” 通常译作“黄韧带”，是脊柱韧带之一。

- 2. The intcrspinous ligaments *(ligamenta interspinalia)* (see Figs 183, 186, 187) are thin bands filling the spaces between the spinous processes of two adjacent vertebrae. They arc strongest in the lumbar segment of the vertebral column and least developed between the cervical vertebrae. They are joined to the ligamenta flava anteriorly and are continuous posteriorly with the supraspinous ligament at the apex of the spinous process.
- 3. The supraspinous ligament *(ligamentum supraspinale)* (see Fig. 183) is a continuous band running over the tips of the spinous processes in the lumbar and thoracic segments. It is lost downwards on the spinous processes of the sacral vertebrae; above it is continuous with the rudimentary *ligamentum nuchae* at the level of the prominent (seventh cervical) vertebra.

> 棘间韧带（ligamenta interspinalia）（见图183、186、187）是位于相邻椎骨棘突之间的细长韧带束。它们在脊柱的腰段最为强韧，在颈椎之间则发育较差。这些韧带在前方与黄韧带相连，并在棘突尖端处与棘上韧带连续。

棘上韧带（ligamentum supraspinale）（见图183）是一条连续的韧带束，位于腰段和胸段椎骨棘突的顶端。该韧带在骶骨椎骨的棘突上逐渐消失；其上方则与位于突出的（第七颈椎）椎骨水平处的原始项韧带相连。

![](_page_185_Picture_0.jpeg)

186. *Ligaments and joints of vertebral column (ligamenta, juncturae et articulationes columnae vertebralis);*  right side, lateral aspect (%).

> 椎骨的韧带与关节（ligamenta, juncturae et articulationes columnae vertebralis）；右侧，侧方视图（%）。

- 4. The ligamentum nuchae (see Fig. 186) is a thin triangular band of elastic and connective-tissue fibres. It ascends from the spinous process of the prominent vertebra along the .spinous processes of the cervical vertebrae and, expanding, is attached to the external occipital crest and the external occipital protuberance.
  - 5. The intertransverse ligaments *(Hgamenta intertransversaria)*

> 黄韧带（见图186）是由弹性纤维和结缔组织纤维组成的薄三角形带。它从突出椎体的棘突起始，沿颈椎的棘突向上延伸，并逐渐扩展，附着于外枕骨隆起和外枕骨突。  
横突间韧带（横突间韧带）

(see Fig. 184) are thin bands which are poorly developed in the cervical and partly in the ihoracic segments but are stronger in the lumbar part. They are paired ligaments joining the apices ol the transverse processes of adjacent vertebrae and limiting flexion of the spine to the contralatcral side. In the cervical segment they may be bifid or may be absent completely.

> （见图184）为薄带状结构，在颈段和部分胸段发育较差，但在腰段则较为强韧。它们是成对的韧带，连接相邻椎骨的横突尖端，限制脊柱向对侧弯曲。在颈段，这些韧带可能呈双叉状，也可能完全缺失。

#### 3.1.4 T H E SACROCOCCYGEAL JOIN T

> 3.1.4 骶尾关节

the bodies of the fifth sacral and first coccygeal vertebrae; the sac- (see Figs 218-219).

> 第五骶骨体和第一尾骨体；骶骨（见图218-219）。

The sacrococcygeal joint *(junctura sacrococcygea)* forms between This synchondrosis is strengthened by the following ligaments

> 骶尾关节（junctura sacrococcygea）由尾骨与骶骨形成。此软骨联合由以下韧带加强：

rococcygeal synchondrosis contains a small cavity in the intervene- 1. The lateral sacrococcygeal ligament *(ligamentum sacrococcy*bral disc. *geum laterale)* stretches between the transverse processes of the last

> 骶尾关节内含一小腔，即骶尾间盘。  
1. 侧骶尾韧带（ligamentum sacrococcybral laterale）位于最后一节椎体的横突之间。

![](_page_186_Picture_2.jpeg)

187. *Ligaments and joints of cervical vertebrae and occipital bone;* inner aspect (J/[).

> 颈椎和枕骨的韧带与关节；内侧方面 (J/[)

(Sagittal median section through occipital bone and first to fourth cervical vertebrae.)

> （经枕骨和第一至第四颈椎的矢状位中段断面。）

sacral and first coccygeal vertebrae and is a continuation of the inlertransverse ligament.

> 骶骨和第一尾骨椎体，是横突间韧带的延续。

- 2. The anterior sacrococcygeal ligament *fligamentum sacrococcygeum ventrale)* is a continuation of the anterior longitudinal ligament and consists of two bands located on the anterior surface of the sacrococcygeal joint. The fibres of these bands intersect.
- 3. The superficial posterior sacrococcygeal ligament *(ligamenium sacrococcygeum dorsale superficiale)* is stretched between the poste-

> 前骶尾韧带（ligamentum sacrococcygeum ventrale）是前纵韧带的延续，由位于骶尾关节前表面的两条带状结构组成，这两条带状结构的纤维相互交叉。  
浅层后骶尾韧带（ligamentum sacrococcygeum dorsale superficiale）位于骶尾关节的后方表面，其纤维呈放射状排列。  
[!note]- 术语说明  
“Ligamentum sacrococcygeum ventrale” 通常译作“前骶尾韧带”，是连接骶骨与尾骨的韧带之一，具有维持骨盆稳定性的作用。  
“Ligamentum sacrococcygeum dorsale superficiale” 通常译作“浅层后骶尾韧带”，位于骶尾关节后方，主要起到限制过度后伸的作用。

rior surface of the coccyx and (he lateral walls of the opening into the sacral canal and closes its fissure. It corresponds to the ligamenta (lava and the supraspinous ligaments of the vertebral column.

> 骶管裂孔的前壁由尾骨的前面构成，两侧壁则构成进入骶管的开口，并封闭其裂隙。它对应于椎骨的黄韧带和棘上韧带。[!note]- 术语说明 “Ligamenta (lava” 可能为笔误，正确应为“ligamenta flava”（黄韧带）。在翻译时已采用标准医学术语“黄韧带”。

4. The deep posterior sacrococcygeal ligament *fligamentum sacrococcygeae dorsale profundum)* is a continuation of the posterior longitudinal ligament.

> 深部后方骶尾韧带（ligamentum sacrococcygeae dorsale profundum）是后纵韧带的延续。

![](_page_187_Picture_2.jpeg)

188. Ligaments and joints of cervical vertebrae and occipital bone; inner aspect  $\binom{4}{5}$ .

> 颈椎和枕骨的韧带与关节；内侧方面

(Posterior parts of occipital bone and arches of first to fifth cervical vertebrae removed by frontal section.)

> （通过矢状面移除枕骨后部及第一至第五颈椎的椎弓。）

# ARTICULATIONS AND LIGAMENTS BETWEEN THE OCCIPITAL BONE AND THE ATLAS AND AXIS

> 枕骨与寰椎、枢椎之间的关节及韧带 /no_think

The atlanto-occipital joint (articulatio atlantooccipitalis) (see Figs 187, 188; 189–192) is paired. It is formed by the articular surfaces of the occipital condyles (condyli occipitalis) and the superior articular facet (fovea articularis superior) of the atlas. The longitudinal axes of the articular surfaces of the occipital bone and the atlas come a little closer to each other anteriorly. The articular surfaces of the occipital bone are shorter than those of the atlas. The articular capsule is attached to the margins of the articular cartilages. According to the shape of the articulating surfaces, this joint is included in the group of ellipsoid joints (articulatio ellipsoidea).

> 寰枕关节（articulatio atlantooccipitalis）（见图187、188；189–192）为双侧性关节。该关节由枕骨髁（condyli occipitalis）的关节面与寰椎的上关节面（fovea articularis superior）构成。枕骨与寰椎的关节面纵轴在前方略微靠近。枕骨的关节面较寰椎的关节面短。关节囊附着于关节软骨的边缘。根据关节面的形态，该关节属于椭圆关节（articulatio ellipsoidea）的一类。

At both joints, the right and the left, possessing separate articular capsules, movements occur simultaneously, i.e. the joints form a single combined joint. Nodding (anterior and posterior flexion) and slight sidewards movements of the head are possible.

> 在左右两个关节中，各自拥有独立的关节囊，运动同时发生，即这两个关节形成一个单一的联合关节。可以进行点头运动（前屈和后伸）以及头部轻微的侧向运动。

- The anterior atlanto-occipital membrane (membrana atlantooccipitalis anterior) (see Figs 186, 187) stretches for the whole distance of the gap between the anterior border of the foramen magnum and the superior margin of the anterior arch of the atlas; it merges with the upper end of the anterior longitudinal ligament.
- 2. The posterior atlanto-occipital membrane (membrana atlantooccipitalis posterior) (see Figs 186, 187, 189) stretches between the posterior border of the foramen magnum and the superior margin of the posterior arch of the atlas. In its anterior part is an opening transmitting vessels and nerves. It is an altered ligamentum flavum.

> 前寰枕膜（membrana atlantooccipitalis anterior）（见图186、187）延伸于枕大孔前缘与寰椎前弓上缘之间的整个间隙；它与前纵韧带的上端相融合。  
后寰枕膜（membrana atlantooccip.
[!note]- 术语说明  
“Ligamentum flavum” 通常译作“黄韧带”，是脊柱韧带之一。

The following three joints form in articulation of the atlas and axis; two are paired and one is unpaired.

> 以下三个关节构成寰椎与枢椎的关节；其中两个为成对关节，一个为不成对关节。

1. The lateral atlantoaxial joint (articulatio atlantoaxialis latera-

> 侧寰枢关节（articulatio atlantoaxialis lateralis）

![](_page_188_Picture_0.jpeg)

(Posterior parts of occipital bone and posterior arch of atlas are removed.)

> （ occipital bone 的后部及 atlas 的后弓被去除。）

> 寰椎与枢椎的韧带和关节；上部（3/4）。(寰椎前弓及侧块、枢椎的齿突部分被水平切面去除。)
