- 4 medial condyle of tibia 5 —head of fibula
- 9 lateral condyle of lemur 10—patella

> 1—股骨  
6—胫骨  
2—髁间窝  
7—胫骨粗隆  
3—胫骨外侧髁  
8—股骨内侧髁  
4—胫骨内侧髁  
5—腓骨头  
9—股骨外侧髁  
10—髌骨

iln arcuate ligament of the kiiee *(hgamentum popliteum arcuatum)*  (Fig. 226) is always found in this pan of the articular capsule. It stretches between the lateral femoral epicondyle and the middle part of the oblique ligament.

> 膝后外侧韧带（popliteum arcuatum ligamentum）（图226）始终位于关节囊的这一部分。它位于外侧股骨髁与斜韧带中部之间。

The following ligaments are in the cavity of the knee joint.

> 膝关节腔内的韧带包括以下韧带。

1. The anterior cruciate ligament *(Hgamentum cruciatum anterius)* runs from the lateral condyle of the femur forwards and medially and is attached to the tibia in the anterior intercondylar area.

> 前交叉韧带（Ligamentum cruciatum anterius）起自股骨的外侧髁，向前内侧延伸，并附着于胫骨前间窝区。

2. The posterior cruciate ligament *(Hgamentum cruciatum posterius)* stretches from the medial surface of the medial femoral condyle to the back and medially, crosses the anterior cruciate ligament, and is attached to the posterior intercondylar area of the tibia.

> 后交叉韧带（ligamentum cruciatum posterius）起自股骨内侧髁的内侧表面，向后方和内侧延伸，穿过前交叉韧带，并附着于胫骨髁间后部。

![](_page_232_Picture_2.jpeg)

230. *Right knee joint (articulatio genus);* anterior aspect (%). **(Articular capsule cut on lateral, anterior, and medial surfaces. Quadriceps femoris muscle with patella arc drawn away distally.)**

> 右膝关节（articulatio genus）；前侧（%）。 （关节囊在侧、前及内侧表面被切开。股四头肌与髌骨弧向远端拉开。）

- 3. The transverse ligament of the knee *(ligamenlum transversum genus)* connects the anterior surface of both menisci.
- *femorale anterius)* (Fig. 231) extends from the anterior part of the medial meniscus upwards and laterally to the medial surface of the lateral femoral condyle.
- 5. The posterior meniscofemoral ligament *(Ugamentum meniscofemoralis posterius)* (Fig. 232) runs from the posterior part of the lat-4. The anterior meniscofemoral ligament *(Ugamentum menisco-* eral meniscus upwards and medially to the medial surface of the medial femoral condyle.

> 膝关节的横韧带（ligamentum transversum genus）连接两个半月板的前表面。  
前膝膜股韧带（ligamentum meniscofemorale anterius）（图231）自内侧半月板的前部向上并向外延伸，连接至股骨外侧髁的内侧表面。  
后膝膜股韧带（ligamentum meniscofemorale posterius）（图232）自外侧半月板的后部向上并向内延伸，连接至股骨内侧髁的内侧表面。  

[!note]- 术语说明  
“ligamentum meniscofemorale anterius” 通常译作“前膝膜股韧带”，是连接半月板与股骨髁的韧带之一。  
“ligamentum meniscofemorale posterius” 通常译作“后膝膜股韧带”，也是连接半月板与股骨髁的韧带之一。

The knee joint is a combination of a hinge *(ginglymus)* and pivot *(trochoid)* joints and is related lo trochoginglymus.

> 膝关节是由一个铰链关节（ginglymus）和一个旋转关节（trochoid）组合而成，与 trochoginglymus 相关。

![](_page_233_Picture_6.jpeg)

231. *Right knee joint (articulatio genus);* anterior aspect *(<sup>x</sup> /\).*  (Articular capsule removed. Tendon of quadriceps femoris muscle together with patella are

> 右膝关节（articulatio genus）；前方（<sup>x</sup> /\）。 （关节囊已去除。股四头肌肌腱与髌骨一起 /no_think

drawn away distally.)

> （被向远端牵拉。）

![](_page_234_Figure_2.jpeg)

## 25 JOINTS OF THE LEG BONES

> 27 肢体骨关节

![](_page_235_Picture_3.jpeg)

The proximal ends of the leg bones form the superior tibiofibular joint *(articulatw tibwfibularis)* (Figs 232, 234, 236, 238). The articulating surfaces are the Hat articular facet of the head of the fibula *(fades articularis capitis fibulae)* and the fibular articular surface of the lateral tibial condyle *(fades articularis fibularis).*

> 下肢骨的近端形成上胫腓关节（关节名称为胫腓关节，articulatio tibiofibularis）（见图232、234、236、238）。参与关节形成的关节面包括腓骨头的横关节面（facies articularis capitis fibulae）和腓侧胫骨髁的腓关节面（facies articularis fibularis）。

The articular capsule *(capsula articularis)* is attached to the edges of the articulating surfaces; it is taut and strengthened by the anterior and posterior ligaments of the superior tibiofibular joint *(ligamenta capitis fibulae anterius* et *posterius).* The ligaments are on the anterior and posterior surfaces of the joint and extend from the tibia to the head of the Kbula. The proximal tibiofibular joint permits a small range of movements.

> 关节囊（capsula articularis）附着于关节面的边缘；其紧张程度由胫腓上关节的前、后韧带（ligamenta capitis fibulae anterius et posterius）加强。这些韧带位于关节的前、后表面，从胫骨延伸至腓骨头。胫腓近端关节允许进行小范围的运动。

The space between the two leg bones is filled by the intcrosseous membrane of the leg *(membrana interossea cruris).*

> 两条腿骨之间的空间由小腿的骨间膜（membrana interossea cruris）填充。

The fibres of the membrane descend and run laterally from the interosseous border of the tibia to that of the fibula. In the upper part of the membrane is a large opening transmitting vessels and nerves; in the lower part is a smaller opening transmitting vessels. The membrane is stronger in the lower part.

> 膜的纤维向下从胫骨骨间缘延伸至腓骨骨间缘，并向侧方运行。在膜的上部有一个较大的孔，通过该孔有血管和神经通过；在膜的下部有一个较小的孔，通过该孔有血管通过。膜的下部较厚。

The distal ends of the leg bones form the inferior tibiofibular syndesmosis (joint) *(syndesmosis* s. *articulatio tibiofibulans).*

> 下肢骨的远端形成下胫腓联合（关节）（syndesmosis s. articulatio tibiofibulans）。

On the anterior and posterior surfaces of this joint are short but strong ligaments stretching from the anterior and posterior edges of the fibular notch of the tibia to the lateral malleolus. These arc the anterior and posterior inferior tibiofibular ligaments *(ligamenta tibiofibularia anterius* et *posterius)* (Figs 234-236). There are also dense bands of connective-tissue fibres stretched for the whole distance between the fibular notch of the tibia and the rough surface of the lateral malleolus facing it.

> 该关节的前后面有短而强韧的韧带，从胫骨腓骨切迹的前后缘延伸至外踝。这些韧带称为前下胫腓韧带和后下胫腓韧带（ligamenta tibiofibularia anterius et posterius）（图234-236）。此外，从胫骨腓骨切迹到面对它的外踝粗糙表面，还有一条致密的结缔组织纤维束，贯穿整个距离。

> 234. *Right tibiofibular joint (articulatio tibiofibularis), interosseous membrane of leg (membrana interossea cruris), and tibiofibular syndesmosis (syndesmosis tibiofibularis)*; anterior aspect (%).

> 右胫腓关节（articulatio tibiofibularis）、小腿骨间膜（membrana interossea cruris）以及胫腓关节联结构（syndesmosis tibiofibularis）；前外侧方面（%）。

## 26 JOINTS OF THE FOOT

> 28 足部关节

## 27 **T HE ANKLE JOINT**

> 29 踝关节

tal ends of the libia and fibula and the articular surface of the of the lateral malleolus *(fades articularis malleoli).*  trochlea of the talus. The tibia articulates by means of its inferior The articular surface of the talus is pulley-shaped (trochlcar)

> 胫骨和腓骨的远端以及外踝关节面（外踝关节面）；距骨滑车。胫骨通过其下端与距骨滑车形成关节。距骨的关节面呈滑车形（滑车状）。

The ankle, or talocrural joint *(articulatio talocruralis)* (Figs 235, articular surface *(fades articularis inferior)* and the malleolar facet 236, 238-240) is formed between the articular surfaces of the dis- *(fades articularis malleolaris).* The fibula also bears an articular facet

> 踝关节，或称距小腿关节（articulatio talocruralis）（图235，关节面（下关节面）和骨髁面 236，238-240）由胫骨下关节面与距骨的骨髁面构成。腓骨上也具有一个关节面。

![](_page_236_Picture_7.jpeg)

235. *Ligaments and joints of right foot* (J4). (Dorsal surface.)

> 右足的韧带和关节（J4）。（背侧表面）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_237_Picture_2.jpeg)

236. *Ligaments and joints of right foot* (%) (Articular capsules arc removed/

> 右足韧带与关节 (%)（关节囊已去除）

![](_page_238_Picture_2.jpeg)

237. *Ligaments and joints of right foot* (/<sup>3</sup> (Plantar surface.)

> 右足的韧带和关节（/<sup>3</sup>（足底面。）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_239_Picture_2.jpeg)

238. *Joints and ligaments of right foot (] /i).*  **(Section through ankle joint and joints of foot.)**

> 右足关节与韧带（] /i）。 （经踝关节及足部关节的横断面。）

![](_page_240_Picture_2.jpeg)

238a. Joints and ligaments of right foot  $(\frac{1}{2})$ . (Section through ankle joint and joints of foot.)

> 238a. 右足关节与韧带 （经踝关节及足部关节切开）

on top, while on the sides it carries flat malleolar facet of the lateral and malleolar facet of the medial surface of the talus *(fades malleotaris lateralis* et *medialis).* The leg bones grasp the trochlea of the talus like a fork.

> 上方，而其两侧则承载距骨的外侧跟面和内侧跟面（fades malleolares lateralis et medialis）。小腿骨像叉子一样抓住距骨的 trochlea。

The articular capsule *(capsule articularis)* is attached for a great distance to the edge of the articular cartilage and only on the anterior surface of the body of the talus it separates from the cartilage 10 be attached to the neck of the talus. The anterior and posterior parts of the capsule are weak.

> 关节囊（capsule articularis）附着于关节软骨边缘的较大范围，仅在距骨体的前表面处，囊与软骨分离，以附着于距骨颈。囊的前部和后部较薄弱。[!note]- 术语说明
“articular capsule” 译为“关节囊”，是关节的纤维性包膜，包裹关节面。

The ligaments of the ankle joint run on its sides.

> 踝关节的韧带位于其两侧。

- 1. The medial (deltoid) ligament *(ligamentum mediate [\deltoi](file:///deltoi)deumj)* (Figs 235, 236, 238). It is subdivided into the following parts:
- (a) the anterior talotibial part *(purs tibiotalaris anterior)* running downwards and forwards from the anterior border of the medial malleolus to be attached to the posieromedial surface of the talus;
- (b) the tibionavicular part *(pars tibionauieularis)* stretching from the medial malleolus to the dorsal surface of the navicular bone; it is longer than the aforementioned part;
  - (c) the calcaneotibial part *(pars tibiocalcanea)* stretching be-

> 内侧（三角肌）韧带（ligamentum mediate deltoideum）（图235、236、238）。该韧带可分为以下部分：  
(a) 前距胫部（前距胫韧带，pars tibiotalaris anterior），从内踝前缘向下向前延伸，附着于距骨的后内侧表面；  
(b) 胫舟部（胫舟韧带，pars tibionavicularis），从内踝延伸至舟骨的背侧表面；该部分比上述部分更长；  
(c) 跟胫部（胫跟韧带，pars tibiocalcanea），延伸至...

tween the tip of the medial malleolus and the sustentaculum tali;

> 在内踝尖与跟骨结节之间；

(d) the posterior talotibial part *(pars tibiotalaris posterior)* running from the posterior border of the medial malleolus downwards and laterally to be attached to the posieromedial parts of the body of the talus.

> (d) 后跟胫部部分（pars tibiotalaris posterior），自内踝后缘向下并向外延伸，附着于距骨体的后内侧部分。

The following ligaments are on the lateral surface of the ankle joint.

> 以下韧带位于踝关节的外侧表面。

- 1. The anterior talofibular ligament *(ligamentum talqfibulare anterius).* It passes from the anterior border of the lateral malleolus to the lateral surface of the neck of the talus.
- 2. The calcaneofibular ligament *(ligamentum calcaneojibulare)*  which arises on the lateral surface of the lateral malleolus, runs downwards and backwards, and is attached to the lateral surface of the calcaneum.
- 3. The posterior talofibular ligament *(ligamentum talofibulare posterius).* It stretches almost horizontally from the posterior border of the lateral malleolus to the lateral tubercle of the posterior process of the talus.

> 前距腓韧带（ligamentum talofibulare anterius）。其从外踝前缘延伸至距骨颈的外侧表面。  
跟腓韧带（ligamentum calcaneofibulare）。其起自外踝的外侧表面，向下并向后延伸，附着于跟骨的外侧表面。  
后距腓韧带（ligamentum talofibulare posterius）。其几乎水平地从外踝的后缘延伸至距骨后突的外侧突起。

The ankle joint is a variant of the hinge joint of the cochlear type.

> 踝关节是杯状铰链关节的一种变异形式。

## 28 T H E SUBTALAR JOINT

> 30 跟距关节

The subtalar (talocalcanean) joint *(articulatio subtalaris)*  (Figs 236, 238, 241) is formed by the posterior calcanean facet for the talus *(fades articularis posterior calcanei),* and the posterior calcanean facet of the talus *(fades articularis calcanea posterior tali).*

> 距下关节（距骨-跟骨关节）（articulatio subtalaris）（图236、238、241）由距骨的后跟骨关节面（fades articularis posterior calcanei）和跟骨的后距骨关节面（fades articularis calcanea posterior tali）构成。

The articular capsule *(capsula articularis)* is loose and is attached for the most part to the edge of the articular cartilages and only in front (on the talus) and behind (on the calcaneum) it deviates slightly from the margins of the articulating surfaces.

> 关节囊（capsula articularis）较为松弛，大部分附着于关节软骨的边缘，仅在前方（在距骨上）和后方（在跟骨上）略微偏离关节面的边缘。[!note]- 术语说明
“articular cartilages” 通常译作“关节软骨”，指覆盖在关节头和关节窝表面的透明软骨，具有缓冲和减少摩擦的作用。

The joint is strengthened by the following ligaments.

> 关节由以下韧带加固。

- 1. The interosseous talocalcanean ligament *(ligamentum talocalcaneum interosseum)* lies in the sinus tarsi and its ends are attached to the groove of the calcaneum and the groove of the talus.
- 2. The lateral talocalcanean ligament *(ligamentum talocalcaneum lateraU)* is stretched between the superior surface of the neck of the talus and the superolateral surface of the calcaneum.
- 3. The medial talocalcanean ligament *(ligamentum talocalcaneum mediate)* runs from the posterior process of the talus to the sustentaculum tali.

> 跗骨间韧带（interosseous talocalcaneal ligament，ligamentum talocalcaneum interosseum）位于跟骨窦内，其两端附着于跟骨沟和距骨沟。  
外侧跗骨韧带（lateral talocalcaneal ligament，ligamentum talocalcaneum laterale）位于距骨颈的上表面与跟骨上外侧表面之间。  
内侧跗骨韧带（medial talocalcaneal ligament，ligamentum talocalcaneum mediate）自距骨后突延伸至距骨滑车。

## 29 T H E TALOCALCANEONAVICULAR JOIN T

> 31 跟距舟关节 /no_think

The talocalcaneonavicular joint *(articulatio talocalcaneonavicularis)* (Fig. 235) is formed by the articular surfaces of the talus, calcaneum, and navicular bone. The talus forms the articular head, the calcaneum and navicular bone supply the articular socket.

> 距骨舟骨关节（articulatio talocalcaneonavicularis）（图235）由距骨、跟骨和舟骨的关节面构成。距骨形成关节头，跟骨和舟骨构成关节窝。

The articular capsule *(capsula articularis)* is attached to the edges of the articular cartilages.

> 关节囊（capsula articularis）附着于关节软骨的边缘。

The following ligaments reinforce the capsule.

> 以下韧带加强了关节囊。

1. The lalonavicular ligament (dorsal) *(ligamentum talonaviculart)* stretches between the neck of the talus and the navicular bum-.

> 距舟韧带（背侧）（ligamentum talonaviculare）位于距骨颈与舟骨体之间。

2. The plantar calcaneonavicular ligament *(ligamentum calcaneonaviculare plantare).* It passes from the sustentaculum tali to the plantar surface of the navicular bone. In the upper part it is continuous with the navicular fibrous cartilage which contributes to the formation of the articular socket of the joint.

> 足底跟舟韧带（plantar calcaneonavicular ligament，ligamentum calcaneonaviculare plantare）。它从跟骨结节延伸至舟骨的足底面。在上部，它与舟骨纤维软骨连续，后者参与构成关节的关节窝。

According to shape, the talocalcaneonavicular joint is a spheroid joint *(articulatio spheroidea)* but it permits movements only in one plane, about the axis, approximately in the sagittal direction.

> 根据形态，距骨 navicular 关节（talocalcaneonavicular joint）属于球窝关节（articulatio spheroidea），但它仅允许在一个平面内运动，围绕轴心，大致在矢状方向上进行。

## 30 T HE CALCANEOCUBOID JOIN T

> 32 跟骨骰关节

The calcaneocuboid joint *(articulatio calcaneocuboidea)* (Fig. 235) is formed by a facet on the posterior surface of the cuboid bone *(fades articularis posterior ossis cuboidei)* and a facet on the calcaneum *(fades articularis cuboidea calcanei).* The articulating surfaces are sad-

> 跟骨骰关节（articulatio calcaneocuboidea）（图235）由骰骨后表面的关节面（fades articularis posterior ossis cuboidei）和跟骨的关节面（fades articularis cuboidea calcanei）共同构成。关节面为凹面，

die-shaped. The medial part of the articular capsule *(capsula articularis)* is attached to the edge of the articular cartilage and is taut; the lateral part is attached at some distance from the edge of the cartilage.

> 梨形。关节囊（capsula articularis）的内侧部分附着于关节软骨的边缘，并处于紧张状态；外侧部分则附着于软骨边缘的一定距离处。

Ligaments, which are stronger on the plantar surface, reinforce the joint.

> 韧带在足底面较强，可加强关节。

1. The long plantar ligament *(ligamentum plantarc longum)* is the strongest. It arises on the inferior surface of the tuber calcanci, runs forwards, bridges the groove of the cuboid bone to form an osteofibrous canal, and reaches the bases of the four lateral metatarsal bones. The deep bundles of this ligament are shorter and are attached to the tuberosity of the cuboid bone.

> 长足底韧带（ligamentum plantare longum）是最强的。它起自跟骨结节的下表面，向前行进，跨越骰骨沟形成骨纤维管，并到达四个外侧跖骨基底部。该韧带的深部束较短，附着于骰骨的突起处。

2. The short plantar ligament *(ligamentum calcaneocuboideum plantare)* is located deeper than the long plantar ligament. Its bundles are in direct contact with the articular capsule and connect the plantar surfaces of the calcaneum and cuboid bone.

> 短足底韧带（足底跟舟韧带）位于长足底韧带深面。其束与关节囊直接接触，连接跟骨和舟骨的足底面。

The calcaneocuboid joint resembles a saddle joint *(articulatio sellaris)* in shape but functions as a uniaxial joint.

> 跟骨与舟骨关节在形态上类似于鞍状关节（articulatio sellaris），但其功能为单轴关节。

## 31 T HE TRANSVERSE TARSAL JOIN T

> 33 跖跗关节

The transverse tarsal joint *(articulatio tarsi transversa)* (Figs 235, 238) unites two joints, the talocalcaneonavicular *(articulatio talocalcaneonavicularis)* and the calcaneocuboid *(articulatio calcaneocuboidea)*  joints. The line of the joint is S-shaped with the medial part being convex anteriorly and the lateral pan convex posteriorly. The joints are isolated anatomically but have a common bifurcated ligament *(ligamentum bifurcatum).* This ligament arises on the dorsal surface of the calcaneum at its anterior border and separates immediately into two ligaments. One is situated laterally and is called

> 跨跗关节（articulatio tarsi transversa）（图235、238）由两个关节组成：跟舟-navicular 关节（articulatio talocalcaneonavicularis）和跟骰关节（articulatio calcaneocuboidea）。该关节的关节线呈S形，其内侧部分向前凸出，外侧部分向后凸出。这两个关节在解剖上是分开的，但具有一个共同的分叉韧带（ligamentum bifurcatum）。该韧带起自跟骨前缘的背侧表面，并立即分为两条韧带。其中一条位于外侧，称为[!note]- 术语说明
“ligamentum bifurcatum” 在中文医学文献中通常译为“分叉韧带”，指在解剖结构中因形态分叉而得名的韧带。

the medial calcaneocuboid ligament *(ligamentum calcaneocuboideum};*  it runs to the dorsal surface of the cuboid bone. The other is medial to it and is called the lateral calcaneonavicular ligament *fligainentum calcaneonaviculare)\* it passes to the navicular bone.

> 内侧跟舟韧带（ligamentum calcaneocuboideum），其走行于舟骨的背侧表面。另一种位于其内侧，称为外侧跟舟韧带（ligamentum calcaneonaviculare），其走行至舟骨。

The bifurcated ligament is also known as 'the key' to the transverse tarsal joint because with all the ligaments surrounding this joint being cut it holds the articulating bones in place and the foot can be exarticulated at this joint during operation only when this ligament is transected.

> 分叉韧带也被称为“横跗关节的钥匙”，因为当所有围绕该关节的韧带都被切断时，只有切断此韧带，才能在手术中将足部在此关节处分离。

## 32 T HE CUNEONAVICULAR JOIN T

> 34 楔骨-navicular关节

The cuneonavicular joint *(articulatio cuneonavicularis)* {Fig. 235) is a complex articulation in the formation of which the uavicular, cuboid, and the three cuneiform bones take part. The following articulations form here: joints between the anterior articular surfaces of the navicular bone and the posterior articular surfaces of the medial, intermediate, and lateral cuneiform bones, and joints between the contiguous surfaces of the cuboid, navicular, and cuneiform bones.

> 距舟关节（articulatio cuneonavicularis）{图235} 是一个复杂的关节，其形成涉及舟骨、骰骨以及三块楔骨。在此处形成的关节包括：舟骨前关节面与 medial、intermediate 和 lateral 楔骨后关节面之间的关节，以及骰骨、舟骨和楔骨相邻面之间的关节。

The joint cavity between the navicular and cuneiform bones is in the frontal plane and from it branch out to the front another three joint cavities, one between the medial and intermediate cuneiform bones, another between the intermediate and lateral cuneiform bones, and the third between the lateral cuneiform and cuboid bones; a cavity also extends to the back between the navicular and cuboid bones.

> 舟骨与楔骨之间的关节腔位于冠状面，从中向前延伸出另外三个关节腔：一个位于内侧楔骨与中间楔骨之间，另一个位于中间楔骨与外侧楔骨之间，第三个位于外侧楔骨与骰骨之间；同时，舟骨与骰骨之间也有一腔向后延伸。

The articular capsule *(capsula articularis)* is attached along the edge of the articular cartilage. The joint cavity communicates with the cavity of the tarsometatarsal joint *(articulatio tarsomttatarsca)* in the region of the second metatarsal through the joint cavities between the medial, intermediate and lateral cuneiform bones.

> 关节囊（capsula articularis）附着于关节软骨的边缘。关节腔通过舟骨、中间楔骨和外侧楔骨之间的关节腔，与第2跖骨区域的距舟关节（articulatio tarsometatarsal）的腔体相通。

The following ligaments strengthen the cuneonavicular joint.

> 以下韧带加强了骨舟关节。

- 1. The dorsal cuneonavicular ligaments *(ligamenta cuneonavicularia dorsalia)* stretch on the dorsal surface of the joint between the navicular and the three cuneiform bones.
  - 2. The dorsal cubonavicular ligament *(ligamentum cuboidtonavi-*

> 关节背侧舟状骨-三楔骨韧带（ligamenta cuneonavicularia dorsalia）位于舟状骨与三块楔骨关节的背侧表面。  
关节背侧骰骨-舟状骨韧带（ligamentum cuboidotnaviculare dorsale）位于骰骨与舟状骨关节的背侧表面。

*culare dorsale)* lies laterally to the dorsal cuneonavicular ligament and connects the dorsal surfaces of the cuboid and navicular bones.

> （背侧）距骨骰骨韧带位于背侧骰骨-navicular 韧带的外侧，并连接骰骨和舟骨的背侧表面。[!note]- 术语说明 “dorsal cuneonavicular ligament” 通常译作“背侧骰骨-舟骨韧带”，是足部关节稳定结构之一。

- 3. The dorsal intercuneifonn ligaments *(ligamenta intercuneijormia dorsalia)* are on the dorsal surface of the joint between the medial, intermediate and lateral cuneiform bones.
- 4. The plantar cubonavicular ligament *(ligamentum cuboideonaviculare plantare)* (Fig. 241) lies on the plantar surface of the joint between the cuboid and navicular bones.
- 5. The plantar cuncocuboid ligament *(ligamentum cuneocuboideum plantare)* connects the plantar surfaces of the lateral cuneiform and cuboid bones.
- 6. The plantar cuneonavicular ligaments *(ligamenta cuneonavicularia plantaria)* are stretched between the plantar surface of the navicular and the three cuneiform bones.
- 7. The plantar intercuneifonn ligaments *(ligamenta intcrcuncijormia plantaria)* are situated on the plantar surface between the cuneiform bones.

> 背侧间舟骨韧带（ligamenta intercuneiformia dorsalia）位于 medial、intermediate 和 lateral 舟骨之间的关节背侧表面。  
跖侧跟舟韧带（ligamentum cuboideonaviculare plantare）（图 241）位于 cuboid 骨与 navicular 骨之间关节的跖侧表面。  
跖侧舟 cuboid 韧带（ligamentum cuneocuboideum plantare）连接 lateral 舟骨与 cuboid 骨的跖侧表面。  
跖侧舟舟韧带（ligamenta cuneonavicularia plantaria）位于 navicular 骨与三个舟骨之间的跖侧表面。  
跖侧间舟骨韧带（ligamenta intercuneiformia plantaria）位于舟骨之间的跖侧表面。

In addition to the ligaments described above, a series of short strong ligaments are located between the adjacent bones in the joint cavities: the interosseous cuneocuboid ligament *(ligamentum cuneocuboideum interosseum),* interosseous intercuneifonn ligaments *(ligamenta intercuneijormia interossea).* The cuneonavicular joint is an articulation permitting a limited range of movements.

> 除上述韧带外，关节腔内相邻骨之间还有一系列短而强韧的韧带：骨间舟月韧带（cuneocuboideum interosseum ligamentum），骨间舟状韧带（intercuneiformia interossea ligamentum）。舟状骨与月骨关节是一种允许有限活动的关节。

241. *Ligaments and joints of right foot*  (Plantar surface.)

> 右足的韧带与关节（足底面）

### 34.1 THE TARSOMETATARSAL JOINTS

> 36.1 跗骨间关节

The tarsometatarsal joinls *(articulationes tarsometatarseae)*  (Figs 235, 238) join the bones of the tarsus to the bones of the metatarsus. The following three tarsometatarsal joints are distinguished: (1) between the medial cuneiform and the first metatarsal bones; (2) between the intermediate and latent^ cuneiform and the second and third metatarsal bones; (3) between the cuboid and the fourth and fifth metatarsal bones. The joint between the medial cuneiform and first metatarsal bones is formed by articular surfaces which are slightly saddle-shaped, the other bones articulate by means of flat surfaces. The line formed by the cavity of the tarsometatarsal joints is irregular because the second metatarsal bone is longer than the other metatarsals, while the lateral cuneiform bone projects forwards a little as compared to the anterior part of the cuboid bone.

> 距骨-跖骨关节（articulationes tarsometatarseae）（图235、238）连接足部距骨与跖骨。以下三种距骨-跖骨关节被区分：（1）内侧楔骨与第一跖骨之间；（2）中间楔骨与外侧楔骨与第二、第三跖骨之间；（3）骰骨与第四、第五跖骨之间。内侧楔骨与第一跖骨之间的关节由略微呈鞍状的关节面构成，而其他关节则通过扁平面进行连接。距骨-跖骨关节的关节腔所形成的线是不规则的，因为第二跖骨比其他跖骨长，同时外侧楔骨较骰骨前部向前突出一点。[!note]- 术语说明  
“intermediate and latent^ cuneiform” 为原文排版错误，应为“intermediate and lateral cuneiform”，即“中间楔骨与外侧楔骨”。

The articular capsule *(capsula articularis)* of each tarsometatarsal joint is attached along the edge of the articular cartilage and is strengthened by the following ligaments.

> 每个距骨舟骨关节的关节囊（capsula articularis）附着于关节软骨的边缘，并由以下韧带加强。

- 1. The dorsal tarsometatarsal ligaments *(ligamenta tarsometatarsea dorsalia)* are situated on the dorsal surface of the joints.
- 2. The plantar tarsometatarsal ligaments *(ligamenta tarsometatarsea planlaria)* which are on the plantar surface.
- 3. The interosseous metatarsal ligaments *(ligamenta metatarsea interossea)* stretch between the bases of the metatarsal bones.
- 4. The interosseous tarsometatarsal ligaments *(ligamenta cuneometatarsea interossea)* join the cuneiform bones to the metatarsal bones. The medial one joins the medial cuneiform bone to the base of the second metatarsal bone and is 'the key' to the tarsometatarsal joints. These joints permit a limited range of movements.

> 足舟骨间背侧韧带（ligamenta tarsometatarsea dorsalia）位于关节的背侧表面。  
足舟骨间跖侧韧带（ligamenta tarsometatarsea plantaria）位于关节的跖侧表面。  
骨间足骨韧带（ligamenta metatarsea interossea）连接足骨的基底部之间。  
骨间足舟骨韧带（ligamenta cuneometatarsea interossea）连接舟骨与足骨。其中，内侧的韧带连接内侧舟骨与第二足骨的基底部，被认为是足舟骨关节的“关键”部分。这些关节允许有限的活动范围。

## 35 T HE INTERMETATARSAL JOINTS

> 37 足部跖骨间关节

The intermetatarsal joints *(articulationes intermetatarseae)* form between the bases of the metatarsal bones. The direction of ligaments strengthening them is on the whole similar to that of the ligaments of the hand.

> 跖骨间关节（articulationes intermetatarseae）由跖骨基底部形成。加强这些关节的韧带方向整体上与手部韧带的方向相似。

The articular capsules *fcapsulae articulates)* are reinforced by the following ligaments: the interosseous metatarsal ligaments *(lig-* *amenta metatarsea interossea),* the dorsal metatarsal ligaments *(ligamenta metatarsea dorsalia),* and the plantar metatarsal ligaments *(ligamenta metatarsea plantaria).*

> 关节囊（capsulae articulares）由以下韧带加强：骨间足底韧带（ligamenta metatarsea interossea）、足背骨间韧带（ligamenta metatarsea dorsalia）以及足底骨间韧带（ligamenta metatarsea plantaria）。

The gaps between the individual metatarsal bones arc called interosseous spaces of the metatarsus *(spatia interossea metatarsi).*

> 各个跖骨之间的间隙称为跖骨间间隙（metatarsal interosseous spaces，spatia interossea metatarsi）。

#### 35.1.1 T HE METATARSOPHALANGEAL JOINTS

> 37.1.1 趾骨-跖骨关节

The metatarsophalangeal joints *(articulationes metatarsophalangeae)* (sec Figs 235, 237, 238) are formed by the articular surfaces of the heads of the metatarsals and the bases of the proximal phalanges. The heads of the second and third metatarsal bones are of an irregular spherical shape and their dorsal surface is rather narrow.

> 跖骨与趾骨关节（metatarsophalangeal joints，articulationes metatarsophalangeae）（参见图235、237、238）由跖骨头部与趾骨近节趾骨基底的关节面构成。第二和第三跖骨头部呈不规则球形，其背侧表面较为狭窄。

The articular capsules *(capsulae articulares)* are attached along the edge of the articular cartilage and are loose. The capsule is thin

> 关节囊（capsulae articulares）附着于关节软骨的边缘，结构较为松弛。囊壁较薄。

in the dorsal part, but is strengthened by the plantar ligaments *(ligamenta plantaria)* on the plantar surface and by the collateral ligaments *(ligamenta collateralia)* on the sides. In addition, the deep transverse ligament of the sole *(ligamentum metatarsea transversa profunda)* stretches between the heads of the metatarsal bones.

> 在背侧部分，但被足底的足底韧带（ligamenta plantaria）加强，并被两侧的副韧带（ligamenta collateralia）加固。此外，足底的深横韧带（ligamentum metatarsea transversa profunda）位于跖骨头部之间。

The metatarsophalangeal joints are of the ball-and-socket type *(articulatio spheroidea).*

> 跖骨与趾骨关节属于球窝关节（articulatio spheroidea）。

#### 35.1.2 THEINTERPHALANGEALJOINT S

> 37.1.2 指骨间关节

The interphalangeal joints of the toes *(articulationes interpkalangeae pedis)* (Figs 235, 237, 239) join the proximal phalanges to the middle phalanges and the middle phalanges to the distal ones. Their articular capsules *(capsulae articulares)* are thin and strength-

> 足趾的趾间关节（articulationes interphalangeae pedis）（图235、237、239）连接近节趾骨与中节趾骨，中节趾骨与远节趾骨。其关节囊（capsulae articulares）较薄，坚韧。

<*\\<* (1 by the collateral ligaments *(ligamenta collateralia)* on the sides and by the plantar ligaments *(ligamenta plantaria)* on the plantar surface.

> 由足侧方的侧副韧带（ligamenta collateralia）和足底面的足底韧带（ligamenta plantaria）所构成。

The interphalangeal joints are of the hinge type *(ginglymus).*

> 趾间关节属于铰链型关节（ginglymus）。

# DEVELOPMENT AND AGE FEATURES OF BONE ARTICULATIONS

> 骨关节的发育及年龄特征

The bones are laid down at the end of the second month of intrauterine life as thickenings of the mesenchyme between the cartilaginous ends of the future bones (Fig. 241a). Th e mesenchyme resorbs and a space, i.e. the cavity of the future joint, appears in it. Thus, at ihe site of the joint the bones come in contact with one another by means of cartilagionous articular surfaces, whereas the mesenchyme surrounding the formed joint cavity blends with the perichondrium and gives rise to the articular capsule. When two spaces appear in the mesenchyme located between the articular surfaces, it separates these cavities to form later a disc which completely separates one cavity from the other; a bilocular joint forms. A meniscus forms if the central part of the disc fails to develop.

> 胎儿在宫内生活第二个月末，骨骼开始形成，表现为未来骨骼软骨端之间的中胚层增厚（图241a）。中胚层被吸收，在其中形成一个空间，即未来关节的腔隙。因此，在关节部位，骨骼通过软骨性关节面相互接触，而围绕形成关节腔的中胚层则与软骨膜融合，形成关节囊。当在关节面之间的中胚层中出现两个腔隙时，这些腔隙被分隔开，最终形成一个盘状结构，完全将两个腔隙分开；形成双腔关节。如果盘状结构的中央部分未能发育，则形成半月板。

Almost all of the elements of the joints of adults are also present in the joints of the newborn {Figs 241b and c). The most active factor determining the formation of a joint after birth are the muscles which exert an action on the given joint, i.e. the work of the joint.

> 几乎所有成人的关节成分在新生儿的关节中也都能找到（见图241b和c）。出生后决定关节形成的主要活跃因素是作用于该关节的肌肉，即关节的活动。

The specific features of some elements in certain joints can be seen in Figs 241b and c. In the shoulder and hip joints, for instance, the articular labrum is poorly pronounced and the glenoid cavity of the scapula and the acetabulum of the hip bone are not deep enough. The articular capsule is relatively thick.

> 某些关节中某些结构的具体特征可参见图241b和图241c。例如，在肩关节和髋关节中，关节唇不明显，肩胛骨的关节窝和髋骨的髋臼均不够深，关节囊相对较厚。

Some age features can be found in the joints of the hand. In the newborn (he articular disc in the distal radioulnar joint is still

> 手部关节中可以发现一些年龄相关的特征。在新生儿中（远端桡尺关节的关节盘仍[未完全形成]。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_247_Picture_5.jpeg)

**<sup>1 —</sup> hip joint 3—menisci** of knee joint

> <sup>1 —</sup> 髋关节 3—膝关节半月板

<sup>2—</sup>**knee joint 4— joints of foot**

not formed, while the articular disc in the mandibular joint closely resembles that in an adult. This is determined by the functional differences between these two joints.

> 未发育形成，而下颌关节的关节盘则与成人的关节盘非常相似。这由这两种关节的功能差异所决定。

["he development ol bone articulations is directly dependent on the formation of the bony and connective-tissue structures and muscular tissue.

All elements encountered in the joints of an adult arc demonstrated in the joints of the newborn (Figs 241b and c) but as if prototypes. Subsequently they continue their formation and acquire the geometrical shapes of the articular surfaces characteristic of each joint of an adult.

> 成人关节中所见的所有结构在新生儿关节中均可见到（图241b和c），但呈现出原型状态。随后这些结构继续发育，并逐渐形成具有成人各关节特有特点的关节面几何形状。

# THE SCIENCE OF THE MUSCLES

> 肌肉科学

*Myologia*

> 肌学

The muscles *fmusculi)* form the locomotor and weight-bearing apparatus together with the bones and their joints and are its active element.

> 肌肉（musculi）与骨骼及其关节共同构成运动和承重装置，是其主动成分。

There are more than 600 skeletal muscles *fmusculi skeleti)* in the human body and their total mass accounts for up to 28-35 per cent of the body mass in females, up to 40-45 per cent in males, and up to 45-55 per cent in athletes. Up to 50 per cent of the total weight of the skeletal muscles fall to the share of the muscles of the lower limbs, up to 30 per cent to those of the upper limbs, and up to 20 per cent to the share of the muscles of the head and trunk.

> 人体内共有超过600块骨骼肌（musculi skeleti），其总质量在女性中约占体重的28-35%，在男性中约占40-45%，在运动员中则可达45-55%。骨骼肌总重量中，下肢肌肉约占50%，上肢肌肉约占30%，头颈部及躯干肌肉约占20%。

Most muscles (Figs 244, 248) have a belly *(venter)* and a head *(caput).* The head is that end of the muscle which faces the proximal part of the body. The ends of the head and belly are tightly fused with tendons *(tendae)* by means of which the muscle is attached to the bone. The attachment occurs mostly through tendon fibres perforating the periosteum to enter the bone tissue (perforating fibres).

> 大多数肌肉（图244、248）都有一个肌腹（venter）和一个肌头（caput）。肌头是指朝向身体近端部分的肌肉末端。肌头和肌腹的末端通过肌腱（tendae）紧密连接，从而将肌肉附着于骨骼。这种附着主要通过穿通骨膜进入骨组织的肌腱纤维（perforating fibres）实现。

Most muscles have one belly and two tendons (Fig. 244) and, accordingly, two points of attachment, one proximal (origin of the muscle) and the other distal (insertion of the muscle). In analysing the positions and movements of the human body it is important to differentiate the mobile point and the fixed point. The fixed point often coincides with the point of origin of the muscle, the mobile point with the point of insertion. When a person is working or is engaged in sports the mobile point often becomes the fixed point and vice versa, and such differentiation is therefore conditional.

> 大多数肌肉有一个肌腹和两个肌腱（图244），因此有两个附着点，一个近端（肌肉的起点）和一个远端（肌肉的止点）。在分析人体的位置和运动时，区分活动点和固定点非常重要。固定点通常与肌肉的起点重合，而活动点则与肌肉的止点相对应。当一个人进行工作或从事体育运动时，活动点往往会成为固定点，反之亦然，因此这种区分是相对的。

Tendons are usually long, but tendinous expansions, called aponeuroses (Fig. 245), are encountered. The aponeuroses are the tendons of the broad muscles, e.g. the oblique muscles of the abdomen. Some muscles (e.g. the rectus abdominis muscle) have tendinous intersections *(intersechones tendineae)* (Fig. 250) dividing them into parts.

> 肌腱通常较长，但有时会见到称为腱膜（aponeuroses，见图245）的腱性扩张部分。腱膜是扁肌的肌腱，例如腹斜肌。一些肌肉（如腹直肌）具有腱性交界（intersechones tendineae，见图250），将肌肉分隔成若干部分。

As an organ the muscle consists of a foundation of skeletal striated muscular tissue, a connective-tissue framework forming its internal soft skeleton, and the vessels and nerves.

> 作为器官，肌肉由骨骼肌组织构成，其内部形成软骨支架的结缔组织框架，以及血管和神经。

The structural-functional tissue unit is the muscle fibre *(myojibra),* in which functions the apparatus of muscular contraction.

> 结构-功能组织单位是肌纤维（肌原纤维），其中发挥作用的是肌收缩装置。

The muscle fibre is a cell which may reach 10-12 cm in length and 70-80 u in thickness. Each fibre is covered by a membrane called sarcolemma and contains sarcoplasm and many nuclei. In the sarcoplasm are the organoids of the muscle cells, among which are organoids possessing specific function. These are myofibrils

> 肌纤维是一个细胞，其长度可达10-12厘米，厚度约为70-80微米。每根肌纤维均被一层称为肌膜（sarcolemma）的膜包裹，内含肌浆（sarcoplasm）和许多细胞核。在肌浆中存在肌细胞的细胞器样结构，其中一些具有特定功能的细胞器样结构称为肌原纤维（myofibrils）。

*(myojibrillae)* containing myoBlaments *(myofilamenta)* which are minutest thread-like structures of protein nature capable of contracting. The physico-optical properties of the myofibrils differ along the distance of the muscle fibre and make it striated (striped) due to the presence of alternating bands. Under polarized light some of them glisten, these are anisotropic discs double-refracting light, others fail to refract light and are dark isotropic discs. Each muscle fibre and small groups of fibres are surrounded by a connective-tissue membrane called the endomysium. Larger complexes of muscle fibres, or muscle bands or fasciculi *(fasciculi musculares),* and the whole muscle are enclosed in a connective tissue membrane known as the perimysium. Vessels and nerves pass to the muscle fibres in the layers of the connective tissue.

> （肌节）含有肌原纤维（肌丝），后者是具有收缩功能的最小丝状蛋白质结构。肌原纤维的物理光学性质沿肌纤维长度方向有所不同，由于存在交替排列的明暗带，从而使肌纤维呈现横纹（条纹）状。在偏光下，部分肌原纤维会发亮，这些是各向异性盘，具有双折射性；另一些则不折射光线，呈现暗色的各向同性盘。每根肌纤维以及小群肌纤维都被一种结缔组织膜包围，称为内膜（内肌膜）。较大的肌纤维复合体，或肌束（肌束，fasciculi musculares），以及整块肌肉则被一种称为外膜（外肌膜）的结缔组织膜包围。血管和神经通过结缔组织层进入肌纤维。

Each muscle has a developed network of blood vessels. The contraction of a muscle promotes rapid flow of blood, i.e. the muscle is a peculiar 'pump' which forces the blood forward. Under conditions of reduced motor activity (hypokinesia) in an inactive mode of life or when prolonged bed-rest is necessary this function of the skeletal muscles is excluded as a result of which the blood flows slower, metabolic processes are reduced and stasis occurs. In contrast, under conditions of motor activity the reserve capillaries open, new capillaries form, and nutrition of the skeletal muscles improves.

> 每块肌肉都具有发达的血管网络。肌肉的收缩可促进血液的快速流动，即肌肉是一种特殊的“泵”，可将血液向前推动。在活动减少（运动减少症）的生活方式或需要长期卧床的情况下，骨骼肌的这一功能被排除，导致血流减慢、代谢过程减弱并出现淤血。相反，在运动活动条件下，储备毛细血管开放，新毛细血管形成，骨骼肌的营养状况得到改善。

The nerve apparatus of the skeletal muscles are represented by receptors which perceive stimulation, and effectors which transmit the stimulus from the central nervous system to the muscle. The number of nerve endings in the muscle is determined by the degree of its functional activity. Physical exertion of a dynamic character increases their number.

> 骨骼肌的神经装置由感受刺激的感受器和将刺激从中枢神经系统传递至肌肉的效应器组成。肌肉中神经末梢的数量由其功能活动程度决定。具有动态性质的体力活动会增加其数量。

The skeletal muscles differ in shape, relation to the joints, location, inner structure, etc.

> 骨骼肌在形状、与关节的关系、位置、内部结构等方面存在差异。

Topographically, the muscles are separated into those of the head, neck, back, chest, abdomen, upper limbs, and lower limbs.

> 从解剖位置来看，肌肉可分为头肌、颈肌、背肌、胸肌、腹肌、上肢肌和下肢肌。

According to the inner structure, the following muscles are distinguished (Fig. 271): fusiform, or spindle-sphaped *(musculi fusifortnes),* the greater part of whose fibres run parallel to the long axis of the muscle, and penniform muscles *(musculi pennati)* which resrnible a feather because of the manner in which the fibres are attached to the tendon. In this group are distinguished unipennatc muscles *(musculi unipennali)* (Fig. 246) in which the muscle fibres approach the tendon from only one direction, bipennate muscles *(musculi bipennati)* (Fig. 247) in which the fibres approach the tendon form both sides, and multipennate muscles *(musculi multipen-*

> 根据肌肉的内部结构，可将其分为以下类型（图271）：梭形肌（musculi fusiformes），其大部分肌纤维平行于肌肉的长轴；以及羽毛状肌（musculi pennati），因其肌纤维附着于肌腱的方式类似羽毛而得名。在这一组中，可进一步区分：单羽状肌（musculi unipennati）（图246），其肌纤维仅从一个方向接近肌腱；双羽状肌（musculi bipennati）（图247），其肌纤维从肌腱的两侧接近；以及多羽状肌（musculi multipennati）。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_252_Picture_2.jpeg)

242. *Muscles of the human body* (anterior aspect)

> 人体肌肉（前面观）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_253_Picture_2.jpeg)

243. *Muscles of the human body* (posterior aspect).

> 人体肌肉（后侧视图）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_254_Picture_2.jpeg)

![](_page_254_Picture_3.jpeg)

(Extensor carpi radialis brevis muscle shown in illustration.)

> （桡侧腕短伸肌在示意图中展示。）

*nati)* which have a complex tendinous framework while the muscle fibres are attached to its numerous septa by bands.

> 具有复杂腱性支架的肌束，其肌纤维通过束带附着于其众多隔膜上。

The shape of the muscles is diverse (Fig. 271). Muscles can be quadrate *(musculi quadrati),* triangular *(musculi triangulares),* cruciate *(musculi cruciati),* circular *(musculi orbicularcs),* muscles with two heads *(biceps)* (Fig. 248) and more *(triceps, quadriceps),* muscles with two bellies *(biventer)* (Fig. 249); muscles which are attached by means of several slips *(serrate muscles)* or by means of aponeurosis *(broad muscles),* etc.

> 肌肉的形态多样（图271）。肌肉可呈方形（quadrati肌），三角形（triangulares肌），十字形（cruciati肌），环形（orbicularis肌），双头肌（biceps）（图248）以及更多头的肌肉（如三头肌triceps、四头肌quadriceps），双腹肌（biventer）（图249）；还有通过多个肌束（锯齿状肌serrate肌）或通过腱膜（阔肌）附着的肌肉等。

According to function, the following muscles are distinguished: flexors *(musculi jlexores),* extensors *(musculi extensores),* adductors *(musculi adductores),* abductors *(musculi abductores),* rotators *(musculi rotatores),* levators *(musculi levatores)^* depressors *(musculi depressores),*  erectors *(musculi erectores),* sphincters *(musculi xpkincteres),* articular

> 根据功能，以下肌肉可作如下区分：屈肌（musculi flexores）、伸肌（musculi extensores）、收肌（musculi adductores）、展肌（musculi abductores）、旋转肌（musculi rotatores）、提肌（musculi levatores）、降肌（musculi depressores）、竖肌（musculi erectores）、括约肌（musculi sphincteres）、关节肌（articular）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_254_Picture_7.jpeg)

245. *Broad muscle.*

> 宽肌。

(Obliquus externus abdominis muscle shown in illustration.)

> （腹外斜肌在示意图中显示。）

muscles *(musculi articulares)* which reinforce a joint and pass into it or are attached to the articular capsule, cutaneous muscles *(musculi cutanei)* which are inserted into the skin and move it, pronators *(musculi pronatores),* supinators *(musculi supinatores),* tensors *(musculi tensores),* and others.

> 肌肉（肌腱关节肌，musculi articulares）加强关节并进入关节或附着于关节囊；皮下肌肉（肌腱皮肤肌，musculi cutanei）插入皮肤并使其运动；旋前肌（肌腱旋前肌，musculi pronatores）、旋后肌（肌腱旋后肌，musculi supinatores）、张肌（肌腱张肌，musculi tensores）等其他类型。

Functionally muscles arc united to form groups responsible for motor activity of one type. These are functional working groups.

> 从功能上看，肌肉相互联合形成负责某一类型运动活动的肌群。这些称为功能工作肌群。

Each axis of rotation at a joint has its own pair of functional working groups. Uniaxia) joints have one pair (or two functional working groups), biaxial joints have two pairs (four groups of muscles), triaxial joints have three pairs (six groups of muscles).

> 每个关节的旋转轴都有其对应的肌功能工作群。单轴关节有一个配对（或两个功能肌群），双轴关节有两个配对（四个肌群），三轴关节有三个配对（六个肌群）。

Muscles which act in concert as components of one functional working group, i.e. which accomplish a single-type action, are called synergists. Muscles which act in opposition to another

> 协同肌是指那些作为单一功能工作组的组成部分而协同作用的肌肉，即执行单一类型动作的肌肉。拮抗肌是指那些作用方向与另一组肌肉相反的肌肉。

![](_page_255_Picture_2.jpeg)

246. *Unipennate muscle (musculus unipennatus).*

> 单羽状肌（musculus unipennatus）[!note]- 术语说明
“Unipennate muscle” 是解剖学中描述肌肉纤维排列方式的术语，指肌肉的肌纤维呈单羽状排列，即肌纤维从肌腱向两侧放射状排列。这种结构常见于某些特定的肌肉，如小腿的腓肠肌（gastrocnemius）。

(Flexor pollicis longus muscle shown in illustration.)

> （示意图中显示的桡侧屈肌）

![](_page_255_Picture_5.jpeg)

247. *Bipennate muscle (musculus bipennatus).*

> 双羽状肌（musculus bipennatus）

(Flexor hallucis longus muscle shown in illustration.)

> （足底长屈肌在图中显示。）

![](_page_255_Picture_8.jpeg)

248. *Biceps muscle.*

> 肱二头肌

(Biceps brachii muscle shown in illustration.)

> （图中展示了肱二头肌。）

group of muscles and are a component of another functional working pair are called antagonists. Such differentiation is conditional because in some movements syncrgists may act as antagonists and vice versa.

> 一组肌肉，作为另一组功能协同肌的一部分，被称为拮抗肌。这种区分是相对的，因为在某些运动中，协同肌可能起拮抗作用，反之亦然。

The skeletal muscles have an auxiliary apparatus (Fig. 250a) which makes their functioning easier. It includes fasciae, syuovial bursae, sheaths of tendons, trochlea of muscles, tendinous arches, and sesamoid bones.

> 骨骼肌具有辅助装置（图250a），使肌肉的功能更容易实现。该装置包括筋膜、滑膜囊、肌腱鞘、肌腹滑车、肌腱弓和籽骨。

The fasciae are fibrous membranes which form linings for body cavities and cover muscles (with the exception of the muscles o( the face) and organs. Superficial fasciae passing in the subcutaneous fat and deep (proper) fasciae are distinguished. The deep fasciae form the following structures for the skeletal muscles:

> 筋膜是纤维膜，形成体腔的衬里并覆盖肌肉（面部肌肉和器官除外）。浅筋膜穿过皮下脂肪，而深筋膜（真性筋膜）则与肌肉分开。深筋膜形成以下结构以包裹骨骼肌：

(1) fibrous canals;

> （1）纤维性管腔；

- (2) osteofibrous canals;
- (3) interosseous septa;
- (4) retinacula of tendons.

> (2) 骨纤维管；  
(3) 骨间隔；  
(4) 肌腱滑膜鞘。

Thus, the fasciae isolate groups of muscles and provide conditions for their free independent contraction.

> 因此，筋膜将肌肉群分隔开，并为它们的独立自由收缩提供了条件。

The supporting function lends fasciae particular significance, they are the site of origin and insertion of many muscles.

> 支持功能使筋膜具有特别重要的意义，它们是许多肌肉的起始点和附着点。

In case of local inflammation the fasciae limit the focus of affection and prevent its extension to the adjacent group of muscles. The interfascial spaces, in contrast, often serve as paths for the spread of the inflammatory process.

> 在局部发生炎症时，筋膜会限制病变范围，防止其扩散至邻近的肌肉群。相反，筋膜间隙则常作为炎症过程扩散的通道。

The synovial bursae *(bursae synooiaUs)* are thin-walled isolated sacs which do not communicate with the joint cavity. They are formed of a synovial membrane and contain synovial fluid. They

> 滑膜囊（bursae synoviae）是薄壁的独立囊状结构，不与关节腔相通。它们由滑膜膜构成，并含有滑膜液。

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_256_Picture_2.jpeg)

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_256_Picture_3.jpeg)

249. *Digastric (biventer) muscle (muscnlus digas tricus).*

> 二腹肌（digas tricus） / 肌肉

(Oniohyoid muscle shown in illustration.)

> （示意图中展示了舌骨肌。）

250. *Multigastric muscle.*  (Rectus abdominis muscle shown in illustration.)

> 多胃肌。（图中所示为腹直肌。）

may be located under the skin (subcutaneous synovia! bursa, *bursa synovialis subeutanea),* under fasciae (subfascial synovial bursa, *htrsa synovialis subjanialh),* between or under the muscles (subinuscular synovial bursa, *buna synovialis submuscularis),* and under tendons (subtendinous synovial bursa, *bursa synovialis subtendinea).* The bursae reduce friction and protect the muscle from injury and, as a result, make the work of the muscles easier.

> 可能位于皮肤下（皮下滑膜囊，subcutaneous synovia! bursa, bursa synovialis subeutanea）、筋膜下（筋膜下滑膜囊，subfascial synovial bursa, htrsa synovialis subjanialh）、肌肉之间或肌肉下方（肌间隙滑膜囊，subinuscular synovial bursa, buna synovialis submuscularis），以及肌腱下方（肌腱下滑膜囊，subtendinous synovial bursa, bursa synovialis subtendinea）。滑膜囊可减少摩擦，保护肌肉免受损伤，因此使肌肉活动更加顺畅。

The sheaths of tendons *(vaginae Undines)* are protective de\ ii i-s for muscle tendons at the places of their closest contact with the bone, mainly on the hand and tool. They reduce friction, make the work of the muscles easier, and reduce the risk of injury to that part of the tendon which borders upon the bone. The sheaths of tendons have an external fibrous layer *(stratum JibrosumJ* to the formation ol which contributes the fascia forming the osteofibrous canal, and an internal synovial layer *(stratum synoviaie).* in view of which they are called synovial sheaths of tendons *(vaginae synoviales tendinum).*

> 肌腱鞘（vaginae undines）是保护肌腱在与骨最密切接触部位的结构，主要位于手部和工具使用部位。它们可以减少摩擦，使肌肉运动更加顺畅，并降低肌腱与骨相邻部分受伤的风险。肌腱鞘具有外层纤维层（stratum fibrosum），其形成主要由构成骨纤维管的筋膜所贡献；并具有内层滑膜层（stratum synoviale）。因此，它们被称为滑膜性肌腱鞘（vaginae synoviales tendinum）。

The synovial layer forms a duplicature lining the tendon itself and the inner surface of the fibrous layer. A synovial cavity *(cavitas synovialis)* containing synovial fluid forms between the two synovial surfaces of the synovial layer. The site of junction of the synovial surfaces is called the mesoicndon *(mesotendineum).* The part of the synovial layer investing the tendon itself is known as the peritendincuin.

> 滑膜层形成一个皱襞，衬覆肌腱本身及其纤维层的内表面。滑膜层的两个滑膜面之间形成一个滑膜腔（cavitas synovialis），腔内含有滑液。滑膜面相交的部位称为中隔（mesotendineum）。覆盖肌腱本身的滑膜层部分称为腱周膜（peritendinum）。

Muscular pulleys *(trochleae musculari)* are encountered on the bom- in some parts of the skeleton. A tendinous arch *(arcus tendi-*

> 肌腱滑车（trochleae musculari）在骨骼的某些部分可被发现。腱性弓（arcus tendini）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_257_Picture_0.jpeg)

*neus)* forms here above the notch in a bone covered with a thin layer of cartilage. Passing under the arch over the trochlea, the tendon is held fast in place and changes its direction. A synovial bursa reducing friction is lodged between the tendon and the trochlea.

> （神经）在此处位于骨凹陷上方，覆盖有一层薄薄的软骨。肌腱在滑车上方拱形结构下通过，被固定在位并改变方向。在肌腱与滑车之间存在一个滑膜囊，以减少摩擦。

Sesamoid bones are present in the tendons of some muscles. One of the surfaces of such a bone is covered by cartilage and articulates with the articular surface of the other bone. The sesamoid bones are located close to the site of attachment of the tendon and increase the angle of the attachment, thus contributing to the improvement of the conditions for the work of the muscle and increasing the lever of action of the muscular traction. The patella is the largest sesamoid bone.

> 籽骨存在于某些肌肉的肌腱内。此类骨的一个表面被软骨覆盖，并与另一骨的关节面形成关节。籽骨位于肌腱附着点附近，可增大附着角度，从而改善肌肉工作的条件并增加肌肉牵引作用的力臂。髌骨是最大的籽骨。

The structure of skeletal muscles characteristic of an adult takes shape gradually. The muscles of a newborn possess all the components, but still resemble the foetal muscles in structure. The contractile apparatus of a skeletal muscle develops particularly intensively from the age of 3-4 years, and by the age of 7-8 the muscles are almost similar in structure to those of adults. The physiological properties of the muscles, however, begin to be established from the age of 12-14 years and the process is completed only by the second decade of life. The development of muscles reaches its peak by the third decade of life when the diameter of the muscle fibres is the greatest and the network of blood vessels, the nerve apparatus, and the connective-tissue framework are developed well.

> 骨骼肌的结构在成人的特征性形态逐渐形成。新生儿的肌肉已具备所有成分，但其结构仍类似于胎儿肌肉。骨骼肌的收缩装置在3-4岁时发育最为旺盛，到7-8岁时，肌肉结构已几乎与成人相似。然而，肌肉的生理特性则从12-14岁开始逐渐建立，这一过程直到生命的第二个十年才完成。肌肉的发育在第三个十年达到顶峰，此时肌纤维直径最大，毛细血管网、神经装置及结缔组织框架均发育良好。

Involution of the skeletal muscle begins at old age. The ratio of its components is disturbed: the striated muscular tissue atrophies, some of the blood vessels become empty, the number of nerve apparatus decreases. This is accompanied by growth of fibrous connective and fatly tissue. Physical exertion delays the development of involulional changes in the skeletal muscle.

> 骨骼肌的退化始于老年期。其组成成分的比例发生紊乱：横纹肌组织发生萎缩，部分血管变为空腔，神经装置的数量减少。同时伴有纤维性结缔组织和脂肪组织的增生。体力活动可延缓骨骼肌退行性变化的发展。

# MUSCLES OF THE TRUNK AND HEAD

> 躯干和头部的肌肉

*Musculi trunci et capitis*

> 躯干肌与头肌

## 1 MUSCLES AND FASCIAE OF THE TRUNK

> 1 躯干肌肉与筋膜

The human body except for ihe head and limbs is called ihe trunk *(truncus).*

> 人体除头部和四肢外的部分称为躯干（truncus）。

The muscles of the trunk are grouped as follows: (1) the muscles of the back *(musculi dorsi);* (2) ihe muscles of the neck *(musculi colli);* (3) the muscles of ihe chest *(musculi thoracis);* (4) the muscles of the abdomen *(musculi abdominis).*

> 躯干的肌肉可分为以下几组：(1) 背部肌肉（背肌，musculi dorsi）；(2) 颈部肌肉（颈肌，musculi colli）；(3) 胸部肌肉（胸肌，musculi thoracis）；(4) 腹部肌肉（腹肌，musculi abdominis）。

## 2 MUSCLES AND FASCIAE OF THE BACK

> 2 背部肌肉与筋膜

## 3 REGIONS OF THE BACK

> 3 背部的三个区域

The following regions of the back *(regiones dorsi)* (Fig. 252) are distinguished:

> 背部的以下区域（regiones dorsi）（图252）有所区分：

- (a) the nuchal region *(regio colli posterior),* an unpaired region occupying the posterior (occipital) parts of the neck;
- (b) ihe vertebral region *(regio oertebraiis),* an unpaired region corresponding to the contours of the vertebral column;
- (c) the scapular region *(regio scapularis),* corresponding lo ihe contours of ihe scapula;
- (d) ihe infrascapular region *(regio injrascapularis),* right and left, located below ihe scapula;
- (e) the lumbar region *(regio lumbalis),* right and left, bounded by the twelfth rib above and by the iliac crest below;
- (f) the sacral region *(regio sacralis),* an unpaired region corresponding to the contours of the sacrum.

> (a) 颈后区（regio colli posterior），为一不成对区域，占据颈部后部（枕部）；  
(b) 椎区（regio vertebrae），为一不成对区域，与椎体的轮廓相对应；  
(c) 肩胛区（regio scapularis），与肩胛骨的轮廓相对应；  
(d) 肩胛下区（regio infrascapularis），左右两侧，位于肩胛骨下方；  
(e) 腰区（regio lumbalis），左右两侧，上方由第十二肋骨界定，下方由髂嵴界定；  
(f) 骶区（regio sacralis），为一不成对区域，与骶骨的轮廓相对应。

A longitudinal sulcus dorsi passing on the midline is seen on inspection of the back; the spinous process of the vertebra promi-

> 在观察背部时，可见一条纵向的背中线沟；椎骨的棘突突出于该沟的中线位置。

nens and of the vertebrae distal to it are palpated along ihe sulcus. To ihe sides of the sulcus ihe contours of the sacrospinalis muscle are visible, in the upper part are also seen ihe contours of the scapula and its spine *(spina scapulae).* The superior border of the scapula is on ihe level with the second rib, the inferior angle is on the level wilh the seventh rib. On contraction of well-developed musculature a depression, a rhomboid area, is noticeable in the upper part of the back, in the centre of which is located the spine of the vertebra prominens.

> 沿沟槽可触及肋骨和其下方的椎骨。沟槽两侧可见骶棘肌的轮廓，上部还可看到肩胛骨及其棘突（spina scapulae）。肩胛骨的上缘与第二肋处于同一水平，下角则与第七肋处于同一水平。当发达的肌肉收缩时，在背部上部会出现一个凹陷区域，呈菱形，其中央为隆突椎的棘突。

This depression corresponds to the expanded part of the tendon of ihe trapezius muscle. The iliac crests can be felt in the lower part of ihe back. Another rhomboid depression is outlined here and bounded above by the spinous process of the fifth lumbar vertebra, on the sides by the posterior superior iliac spine, and below by the coccyx.

> 此凹陷对应于斜方肌肌腱的扩张部分。髂嵴可在背部下部触及。此处还勾勒出另一个菱形凹陷，其上方由第五腰椎的棘突界定，两侧由坐骨大切迹构成，下方则由尾骨构成。

![](_page_260_Figure_0.jpeg)

251. *Outlines of trunk muscles* (posterior aspect).

> 躯干肌肉概要（后侧视图）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_261_Picture_0.jpeg)

252. *Regions of the trunk and lines of skin incisions.*

> 躯干区域及皮肤切口线。

{Blue line—boundaries of regions; red line—skin incisions niosi suitable for exposure of muscles which are being prepared.)

> {蓝线—区域边界；红线—适合暴露正在准备的肌肉的皮肤切口。}

#### 3.1.1 MUSCLES OF THE BACK

> 3.1.1 背部肌肉

Superficial and deep muscles of the back *(musculi dorsi)* are distinguished.

> 背肌可分为浅层肌和深层肌（背肌群）。

- I. *Superficial muscles of the back.* 
  - A. Muscles of the back related to the upper limb:
  - 1. The trapezius muscle *(musculus trapezius).*
  - 2. The latissimus dorsi muscle *(musculus latisstmus dorsi).*
- 3. The rhomboid major and minor muscles *(musculi rhomboidei major* et *minor).* 
  - 4. The levator scapula muscle *(musculus levator scapulae).*
  - B. Muscles of the ribs:
- 5. The serratus posterior superior muscle *(musculus serratus posterior superior).*
- 6. The serratus posterior inferior muscle *(musculus serratus posterior superior).*

> I. 背部浅层肌肉  
A. 与上肢相关的背部肌肉：  
- 斜方肌（musculus trapezius）  
- 背阔肌（musculus latissimus dorsi）  
- 大菱形肌和小菱形肌（musculi rhomboidei major et minor）  
- 肩胛提肌（musculus levator scapulae）  
B. 肋骨相关的肌肉：  
- 上斜方肌（musculus serratus posterior superior）  
- 下斜方肌（musculus serratus posterior inferior）

II. *Deep (proper) muscles of the back.*

> 二、背部的深（真）肌

- A. Long muscles:
- 1. The spleuius capitis muscle *(musculus splenius capitis).*
- 2. The splenius cervicis muscle *(musculus splenius cervicis).*
- 3. The sacrospinalis muscle *(musculus erector trunci spinae).*
- 4. The transversospinalis muscle *(musculus transversospinalis).*
- B. Short muscles:
- 1. The interspinales muscles *(musculi inter spinales).*
- 2. The intcrtransverse muscles *(musculi intertransversarti).*
- 3. The levatores coslarum muscles *(musculi levatores costarum).* 
  - 4. The group of suboccipital muscles.

> A. 长肌：
脾脏肌（splenius capitis）。
颈脾肌（splenius cervicis）。
骶棘肌（erector spinae）。
横突棘肌（transversospinalis）。

B. 短肌：
棘间肌（inter spinales）。
横突间肌（intertransversarii）。
肋提肌（levatores costarum）。
枕下肌群。

The deep muscles of the back are covered by the lumbar fascia *(fascia thoracolumbalis).*

> 背部深部肌肉被腰筋膜（胸腰筋膜）覆盖。

#### 3.1.2 JPERF :iAL MUSCLES O F TH E BACK

> 3.1.2 背部肌肉 /no_think

1. The Irapczius muscle *(musculus trapezius)* (see Figs 243, 253) is a flat and broad muscle occupying a superficial position on the back of the neck and upper part of the back. It is triangular with the base facing the vertebral column and the apex directed to the acromion of the scapula. The trapezius muscles of both sides meet to form a trapczoid. The muscle takes origin from the external occipital protuberance, the superior nuchal line, ligamentum nuchae and supraspinous ligament of all thoracic vertebrae. The tendinous bundles of the muscle are short and only in the region of the lower cervical and upper thoracic vertebrae they are very long and form a diamond-shaped tendinous area.

> 斜方肌（musculus trapezius）（见图243、253）是一块扁平而宽大的肌肉，位于颈部后方及背部上部的浅层。其形态呈三角形，底面朝向椎体，顶点指向肩胛骨的喙突。两侧的斜方肌相会形成一个菱形区域。该肌起自外侧枕骨隆起、上项线、颈枕韧带以及所有胸椎的项韧带。该肌的肌腱束较短，仅在下颈椎和上胸椎区域，肌腱束较长，形成一个菱形的肌腱区。

The muscular fibres pass radially to the scapula and are inserted into the spine ol the scapula, the acromion, and the acromial part of the clavicle.

> 肌纤维呈放射状走向肩胛骨，并附着于肩胛骨的脊柱面、肩峰以及锁骨的肩峰部。

Action: on contraction all the fibres draw the scapula to the vertebral column; contraction of the upper fibres raises the scapula, contraction of the lower fibres lowers it. When the scapula is fixed, both trapezius muscles pull the head backwards; contraction of one of the muscles tills the head to the corresponding side.

> 作用：当肌纤维收缩时，所有纤维均将肩胛骨拉向椎骨列；上部纤维的收缩使肩胛骨上提，下部纤维的收缩使肩胛骨下垂。当肩胛骨固定时，两块斜方肌均将头向后拉；当一侧斜方肌收缩时，头则向该侧倾斜。[!note]- 术语说明
“trapezius muscles” 通常译作“斜方肌”，是颈部和肩部的重要肌肉之一。

Innervation: branch of accessory nerve to sternomastoid muscle and cervical nerves (C<sup>3</sup> -C<sup>4</sup> ).

> 支配：副神经分支至胸锁乳突肌及颈神经（C<sup>3</sup> - C<sup>4</sup>）。

Blood supply: transverse cervical, occipital, suprascapular, and intercostal arteries.

> 供血动脉：横颈动脉、枕动脉、肩胛上动脉和肋间动脉。

2. The latissimus dorsi muscle (see Figs 243, 253, 254) is a flat muscle located superficially in the lower part of the back, but its superior fibres arc covered in the initial part by the trapezius muscle. It arises from the spinous processes of the lower five or six thoracic vertebrae, the superficial (posterior) layer of the thoracolumbar fascia, the posterior part of the outer lip of the iliac crest, and lower four ribs. The lateral border of the tendinous part of this muscle, the posterior border of the external oblique muscle of the abdomen and the iliac crest below form the lumbar triangle *(trigonum lumbale),* or Peril's triangle; the floor (anterior wall) of the triangle is the internal oblique muscle of the abdomen. Above this triangle is a small rhomboid area covered posteriorly by the latissimus dorsi muscle and bounded above by the twelfth rib and lower border of the serratus posterior inferior muscle, medially by the sacrospinalis muscle and laterally by the upper border of the internal oblique muscles of the abdomen; the floor (anterior wall) of this area is formed by the aponeurosis of the transversus abdonrinis muscle.

> 斜方肌（见图243、253、254）是一块扁平的肌肉，位于背部的下部浅层，但其上部肌束在起始部分被斜方肌覆盖。该肌起自下五至六胸椎的棘突、胸腰筋膜的浅层（后层）、髂嵴的外侧缘的后部以及下四根肋骨。该肌腱部的外侧缘、腹外斜肌的后缘及髂嵴下缘共同形成腰部三角（lumbar triangle，又称Peril三角）；该三角的底面（前壁）为腹内斜肌。该三角上方是一个小的菱形区域，其后方被斜方肌覆盖，上方由第十二肋和下部的肋间后肌下缘界定，内侧由骶棘肌界定，外侧由腹内斜肌上缘界定；该区域的底面（前壁）由腹横肌的腱膜构成。

The upper fibres of the latissimus dorsi muscle pass laterally and the lower fibres ascend obliquely and laterally to cover the posterior surface of the lower ribs. The muscle receives here accessory fibres in the form of three or four slips and also covers the inferior angle of the scapula and the inferior margin of the teres major muscle *(musculus teres major)* (sometimes receiving an additional fibre). Proximally the latissimus dorsi muscle forms the posterior wall of the axillary fossa, approaches the humerus and is inserted in the crest of its lesser tubercle. The bursa of the latissimus dorsi muscle *(bursa subtendinea musculi latissimi dorsi)* is located here.

> 背阔肌的上部肌束向侧方延伸，下部肌束则斜向上方并侧方，覆盖下肋的后表面。该肌在此处接受三到四条辅助肌束，并覆盖肩胛骨的下角以及大圆肌的下缘（有时还会接受额外的一条肌束）。在近端，背阔肌构成腋窝的后壁，接近肱骨，并插入其小结节的嵴处。背阔肌的滑囊（背阔肌滑囊，bursa subtendinea musculi latissimi dorsi）位于此处。

Action: draws the arm to the trunk and pulls the upper limb backwards to the midline and at the same time rotates it medially (pronation). When the arm is fixed the muscle pulls the trunk up on the arm or contributes to upward displacement of the lower ribs in respiratory excursions and is thus an accessor)' muscle of respiration.

> 动作：将上肢向躯干方向牵拉，并将上肢向中线后方拉，同时进行内旋（前旋）。当上肢固定时，该肌可将躯干向上提拉于上肢之上，或参与呼吸运动中下肋骨的上移，因此是呼吸的辅助肌。

Innervation: nerve to the latissimus dorsi muscle (C7, C8).

> 支配：背阔肌神经（C7, C8）。

Blood supply: the thoracodorsal, circumflex humeral, and intercostal arteries.

> 供血：胸背动脉、肩胛上动脉和肋间动脉。

3. The rhomboid major muscle *(musculus rhomboideus major)*  (Figs 254, 257) is a muscle of the second layer. It lies under the trapezius muscle between the scapulae and is shaped like a flat and wide rhomboid plate. It arises from the spinous processes of the upper four thoracic vertebrae. Its fibres pass laterally and slightly downwards to be inserted into the medial border of the scapula.

> 斜方肌大肌（musculus rhomboideus major）（图254、257）是第二层的肌肉。它位于斜方肌深面，介于两块肩胛骨之间，呈扁平宽大的菱形板状。其起始点为上四节胸椎的棘突。肌纤维向侧方和略向下走行，最终附着于肩胛骨的内侧缘。

3a. The rhomboid minor muscle *(musculus rhomboideus minor)*  arises from the spinous processes of the lower two cervical vertebrae and is inserted into the medial border of the scapula. Both rhomboid muscles are often separated by a small layer of connective tissue.

> 3a. 小菱形肌（musculus rhomboideus minor）起自下两节颈椎的棘突，止于肩胛骨的内侧缘。两侧小菱形肌常被一层薄的结缔组织分隔。

Action: draw the scapula closer to the vertebral column along an oblique line directed to the midline and upwards.

> 动作：沿一条向中线和上方倾斜的线，将肩胛骨拉近椎骨柱。

Innervation: nerve to the rhomboids (Gt-C<sup>e</sup> ).

> 支配：斜方肌的神经（Gt-C<sup>e</sup>）。

![](_page_263_Picture_0.jpeg)

253. Superficial muscles of the back (musculi dorsi) (1/6).

> 背部浅层肌（背肌）（1/6）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_264_Picture_0.jpeg)

254. *Superficial muscles of the back and posterior region of the neck (] /e).*

> 背部浅层肌肉及后颈部区域（图 /e）。

(First, second, and third layers. The trapezius muscles and left latissimus dorsi muscle are removed.)

> （第一层、第二层和第三层。斜方肌和左侧背阔肌已被切除。）

![](_page_265_Picture_0.jpeg)

255. *Muscles of the back and posterior region of the neck (%).*

> 背部及颈部后区肌肉 (%)

(Third layer of superficial muscles. First' and second layers of deep muscles. Muscles and bones of shoulder girdle are removed.)

> （浅层肌肉的第三层。深层肌肉的第一层和第二层。肩带肌肉和骨骼已被移除。）

Blood supply: the transverse cervical, suprascapular, and intercoslal arteries.

> 血供：颈横动脉、肩胛上动脉和肋间动脉。

4. The levator scapulae muscle *(musailus levator scapulae)* (Figs 254, 257) is a muscle of the second layer. It is elongated, thick and located in the posterolateral parts of the neck under the trapezius muscle. It originates as four separate slips from the posterior tubercles of the transverse processes of the upper four cervical vertebrae and runs downwards and slightly laterally to be inserted into the upper part of the medial border of the scapula and its superior angle.

> 斜方肌（musculus levator scapulae）（图254、257）是第二层的肌肉。它呈长形、较粗大，位于斜方肌深面的颈后外侧部分。该肌起自第1至第4颈椎横突后结节的四个独立肌束，向下并向外侧延伸，最终附着于肩胛骨内侧缘的上部及其上角。

Action: raises the scapula, its upper angle in particular, causing rotation as a result of which the lower angle is displaced towards the vertebral column; when the scapula is fixed the cervical part of the vertebral column is inclined backwards and to tin side of the contracting muscle.

> 作用：提升肩胛骨，特别是其上角，导致旋转，从而使下角向椎骨柱方向移动；当肩胛骨固定时，颈椎部分的椎骨柱向收缩肌的侧后方倾斜。

Innervation: nerve to the rhomboids (C+, C\$).

> 支配：斜方肌的神经（C₆，C₇）。

Blood supply: the transverse cervical, superficial cervical, and ascending cervical arteries.

> 血供：横突颈动脉、浅层颈动脉和升支颈动脉。

5. The serratus posterior superior muscle *(musailus serratus posterior superior)* (Fig. 255) is thin and covered by the rhomboid muscle.

> 斜方肌覆盖的上斜方肌（musculus serratus posterior superior）（图255）较为纤薄。

It arises from the lower part of the ligamentum nuchae and the spinous processes of the lower two cervical and upper two thoracic vertebrae. It runs obliquely downwards and laterally and is inserted by means of four slips into the external surface of the second, third, fourth, and fifth ribs slightly laterally of their angles.

> 它起自项韧带的下部以及下两个颈椎和上两个胸椎的棘突。它斜向下外侧方向走行，并通过四条肌腱附着于第二、第三、第四和第五肋骨的肋角稍外侧的外表面。

Action: raises the upper ribs, takes part in the act of inspiration.

> 作用：提升肋骨上缘，参与吸气动作。

Innervation: intercostal nerves (Tr^-Tr^).

> 支配神经：肋间神经（Tr^-Tr^）。

Blood supply: intercostal and deep cervical arteries.

> 供血：肋间动脉和深颈动脉。

6. The serratus posterior inferior muscle *(musculus serratus posterior inferior)* (see Figs 254, 255, 257), like the posterior superior muscle, is flat and thin and is located under the latissimus dorsi muscle. It arises from the superficial layer of the lumbar fascia at the level of the lower two thoracic and upper two lumbar vertebrae. Its fibres pass upwards obliquely and laterally and are inserted as four slips into the external surface of the lower four ribs.

> 斜方肌下部（musculus serratus posterior inferior）（见图254、255、257），与斜方肌上部类似，呈扁平、薄片状，位于背阔肌的深面。其起始点位于腰背筋膜的浅层，位于下两节胸椎和上两节腰椎水平处。其肌纤维向上、斜向外侧方向走行，并分为四束，插入到下四根肋骨的外侧面。

Action: lowers the lower ribs and thus takes part in the act of respiration.

> 作用：降低下肋骨，从而参与呼吸动作。

Innervation: the intercostal nerves (Th<sup>g</sup> -Th1 2 ).

> 支配：肋间神经（T<sup>g</sup> -T12）。

Blood supply: the intercostal arteries.

> 血供：肋间动脉。

## 4 DEEP MUSCLES OF TH E BACK

> 4 背部深层肌肉

The deep (proper) muscles of the back are classified as long and short muscles

> 背部的深部（真）肌肉按其长度分为长肌和短肌。

#### 4.1.1 LONG MUSCLES OF TH E BACK

> 4.1.1 背部的长肌

- I. The splenius capitis muscle *(musculus splenius capitis)* (see Figs 254, 255, 257) arises from the ligamentum nuchae and the spinous processes of the lower five cervical and upper three thoracic vertebrae and is inserted into the lateral parts of the superior nuchal line and the posterior border of the mastoid process.
- II. The splenius cervicis muscle *(musculus splenius cervicis)* arises from the spinous processes of the lower five cervical and upper five thoracic vertebrae and is inserted into the posterior tubercles of the transverse processes of the upper two or three cervical vertebrae.

> I. 斜方肌颈部（musculus splenius capitis）（见图254、255、257）起自项韧带和第5颈椎至第3胸椎的棘突，止于枕外隆凸的侧部及乳突的后缘。  
[!note]- 术语说明  
“Ligamentum nuchae” 通常译作“项韧带”，是连接头颈的韧带结构。

II. 斜方肌颈部（musculus splenius cervicis）起自第5颈椎至第5胸椎的棘突，止于第2或第3颈椎横突的后结节。

Action: bilateral contraction pulls the head and neck backwards. In unilateral contraction the head and neck are rotated to the side of the acting muscle.

> 作用：双侧收缩可使头颈部向后牵引。单侧收缩时，头颈部向作用肌所在侧旋转。[!note]- 术语说明
“acting muscle” 译为“作用肌”，指引起某一动作的肌肉，在此处指单侧收缩时起作用的肌肉。

Innervation: the greater occipital nerve and third and fourth cervical nerves (C<sup>2</sup> -C<sup>4</sup> ).

> 支配神经：枕大神经及第3、4颈神经（C<sup>2</sup> - C<sup>4</sup>）。

III. The sacrospinalis muscle *(musculus erector spinae)* (see Figs 255-257) is the strongest and longest muscle of the back. It fills completely the depression in the back formed to the sides of the spinous processes and extending to the angles of the ribs. The muscle lakes origin from the posterior part of the iliac crest, the dorsal surface of the sacrum, the spinous processes of the lower lumbar vertebrae, and partly from the superficial layer of the lumbar fascia. Ascending, the muscle separates in the lumbar region into the following three parts: (1) the laterally located iliocostocervicalis muscle; (2) the medially situated spinalis muscle and (3) the longissimus muscle located between them.

> III. 棘肌（erector spinae muscle）（见图255-257）是背部最强壮且最长的肌肉。它完全填充由椎棘突两侧形成的背部凹陷区域，并延伸至肋骨的肋角。该肌起自髂嵴的后部、骶骨的背侧、下腰椎的椎棘突，以及腰背筋膜浅层的部分区域。在腰部，该肌向上分为以下三部分：（1）位于外侧的髂肋颈肌；（2）位于内侧的棘肌；（3）位于二者之间的最长肌。

- 1. The iliocostocervicalis muscle *(musculus iliocostalis)* (sec Figs 255-257) is inserted by numerous muscular and tendinous slips into the angles of all the ribs and the transverse processes of the lower cervical vertebrae; it is divided topographically into the musculi iliocostalis lumborum, thoracis, and cervicis:
- (a) the iliocostalis muscle *(musculus iliocostalis lumborum)* takes origin from the posterior part of the transverse tubercles of the sacrum and lumbar fascia, passes laterally and upwards, giving off eight or nine slips which arc inserted into the angles of the lower eight or nine ribs by thin and narrow tendons;
- (b) the costalis muscle *(musculus iliocostalis thoracis)* arises near the angles of the lower five or six ribs, ascends slightly obliquely and laterally and is inserted by thin, narrow tendons into the angles of the upper five or seven ribs;
- (r) the costocervicalis muscle *(musculus iliocostalis cervicis)* takes origin from the angles of the upper five or seven ribs, also ascends obliquely and laterally, and is inserted by three slips into the posterior tubercles of the transverse processes of the fourth, fifth, and sixth cervical vertebrae.

> 髂肋肌（musculus iliocostalis）（参见图255-257）由许多肌性及腱性细束附着于所有肋骨的肋角以及下颈椎椎体的横突；根据其位置可分为髂肋腰肌（musculi iliocostalis lumborum）、胸肋肌（musculus iliocostalis thoracis）和颈肋肌（musculus iliocostalis cervicis）：

(a) 髂肋腰肌（musculus iliocostalis lumborum）起自骶骨横突的后部及腰背筋膜，向侧上方走行，发出8或9条细束，以细长的肌腱附着于下8或9对肋骨的肋角；  
(b) 胸肋肌（musculus iliocostalis thoracis）起自下5或6对肋骨的肋角，稍向斜上方及外侧走行，以细长的肌腱附着于上5或7对肋骨的肋角；  
(c) 颈肋肌（musculus iliocostalis cervicis）起自上5或7对肋骨的肋角，也向斜上方及外侧走行，并以3条细束附着于第4、5、6颈椎椎体横突的后外侧突。

Innervation: the spinal nerves (C3-C8; Thi-Th^ ; L]).

> 支配神经：脊神经（C3-C8；Thi-Th^ ; L]）

- 2. The longissimus muscle *(musculus longissimus)* (see Figs 255-257) is medial of the costocervicalis muscle and stretches from the sacrum to the base of the skull. The following three parts are distinguished in it topographically:
  - (a) the longissimus thoracis muscle *(musculus longissimus thora-*

> 最长肌（musculus longissimus）（见图255-257）位于肋颈肌的内侧，从骶骨延伸至颅底。根据其位置，最长肌可分为以下三个部分：
(a) 胸最长肌（musculus longissimus thoracis）

*as)* arises from the transverse processes of the lumbar and lower six or seven thoracic vertebrae and passes upwards to be inserted into the angles of the lower ten ribs and posterior parts of the transverse processes of all thoracic vertebrae;

> as) 起自腰椎及下六七节胸椎的横突，向上行至插入第10肋骨下缘及所有胸椎横突的后部；

- (b) the longissimus cervicis muscle *(musculus longissimus cervicis)*  originates from the transverse processes of the upper four or five thoracic and lower cervical vertebrae, stretches upwards, and is inserted into the transverse processes of the second, third, fourth, and fifth cervical vertebrae;
- (c) the longissimus capitis muscle *(musculus longissimus capitis)*  takes origin from the transverse processes of the upper three thoracic and lower three or four cervical vertebrae, extends upwards, and is inserted into the posterior border of the mastoid process.

> (b) 颈最长肌（musculus longissimus cervicis）起自上四至五节胸椎及下颈椎的横突，向上延伸，止于第二、第三、第四及第五颈椎的横突；  
(c) 头最长肌（musculus longissimus capitis）起自上三节胸椎及下三至四节颈椎的横突，向上延伸，止于乳突的后缘。

Innervation: the spinal nerves (C)-C<sup>8</sup> ; Th|-Th1 2 ; L1-L5; SrS<sup>2</sup> ).

> 支配神经：脊神经（C<sub>1</sub>–C<sub>8</sub>）；T<sub>1</sub>–T<sub>12</sub>；L<sub>1</sub>–L<sub>5</sub>；S<sub>1</sub>–S<sub>2</sub>）。

- 3. The spinalis muscle *(musculus spinalis)* (see Figs 255-257) runs along the spinous processes and is divided topographically into the following three parts:
- (a) the spinalis thoracis muscle *(musculus spinalis thoracis)* takes origin from the spinous processes of the upper two or three lumbar and lower two or three thoracic vertebrae and extending upwards is inserted into the spinous processes of the eighth to second thoracic vertebrae;
- (b) the spinalis cervicis muscle *(musculus spinalis cervicis)* arises from the spinous processes of the upper two thoracic and lower two cervical vertebrae, runs upwards, and is inserted into the spinous processes of the fourth, third, and second cervical vertebrae;
- (c) the spinalis capitis muscle *(musculus spinalis capitis)* is a poorly developed part of the spinalis muscle and is often absent. It takes origin from the spinous processes of the upper thoracic and lower lumbar vertebrae and extends upwards to be inserted near to the external occipital protuberance.

> 棘肌（musculus spinalis）（见图255-257）沿椎骨的棘突走行，按其位置可分为以下三部分：  
(a) 胸棘肌（musculus spinalis thoracis）起自上两至三节腰椎和下两至三节胸椎的棘突，向上走行并附着于第八至第二胸椎的棘突；  
(b) 颈棘肌（musculus spinal, cervicis）起自上两节胸椎和下两节颈椎的棘突，向上走行，并附着于第四、第三和第二颈椎的棘突；  
(c) 头棘肌（musculus spinalis capitis）是棘肌中发育较差的一部分，常缺失。它起自上胸椎和下腰椎的棘突，向上走行并附着于外侧枕骨隆突附近。  

[!note]- 术语说明  
“spinalis thoracis” 通常译作“胸棘肌”，是背部深层肌肉之一。  
“spinalis cervicis” 通常译作“颈棘肌”，位于颈部深层。  
“spinalis capitis” 通常译作“头棘肌”，为脊柱棘肌的延伸部分，位于头部后方。

Action: the whole erector spinac muscle acts as a strong extensor of the vertebral column on bilateral contraction. It holds the trunk erect. In contraction on one side the vertebral column bends to the same side. The upper fibres pull the head to the side of the contracting muscle. Some of the fibres (the costalis muscle) pull the ribs downwards.

> 作用：整个竖脊肌在双侧收缩时，作为椎体柱的强大伸肌发挥作用，使躯干保持直立。当单侧收缩时，椎体柱向同一侧弯曲。上部肌纤维将头部拉向收缩肌一侧。其中一些肌纤维（肋间肌）可将肋骨向下拉。

Innervation: the spinal nerves (C,-C<sup>8</sup> ; Th,-Th1 2 ; L,-Ls; SrSi).

> 支配神经：脊神经（C1-C8；Th1-Th12；L1-L5；S1-S5）。

4. The transversospinalis muscle *(musculus transversospinalis)*  (Figs 258-260) is covered by the erector spinae muscle and fills the depression between the spinous and transverse processes for the whole distance of the vertebral column. Its relatively short muscu lar fibres run obliquely from the transverse processes of one vertebra to the spinous processes of the contiguous vertebra above. According to the length of the muscle fibres, i.e. the number of vertebrae that they bridge, the following three parts are distinguished in the transversospinalis muscle: (1) the semispinalis muscle whose fibres bridge five and more vertebrae; (2) the multifidus muscle whose fibres bridge two to four vertebrae and are covered by the semispinalis muscle; (3) the rotatores muscles occupying the deepest position.

> 横突棘肌（musculus transversospinalis）（图258-260）位于竖脊肌深面，填充了椎骨棘突与横突之间的凹陷，沿整个椎骨列分布。其相对较短的肌纤维呈斜行方向，从一个椎骨的横突延伸至其上方相邻椎骨的棘突。根据肌纤维的长度，即其跨越的椎骨数目，横突棘肌可分为以下三部分：（1）半棘肌，其肌纤维跨越五个或更多椎骨；（2）多裂肌，其肌纤维跨越两个至四个椎骨，位于半棘肌深面；（3）回旋肌，位于最深的位置。

- 1. The semispinalis muscle *(musculus semispinalis)* (Figs 258-260) is separated into three parts topographically:
- (a) the semispinalis thoracic muscle *(musculus semispinalis thoracis).* Its fibres run between the transverse processes of the lower six and the spinous processes of the upper seven thoracic vertebrae, each bridging six or seven vertebrae;
- (b) the semispinalis cervicis muscle *(musculus semispinalis cervicis);* its fibres are stretched between the transverse processes of the upper thoracic and the spinous processes of the lower seven cervical vertebrae and bridge five vertebrae;
- (c) the semispinalis capitis muscle *(musculus semispinalis capitis);*  it is lodged between the transverse processes of the upper five thoracic and the lower three or four cervical vertebrae and extends to the nuchal area of the occipital bone. A medial and lateral parts are distinguished in this muscle. The venter of the medial part is interrupted by a tendinous intersection.

> 半棘肌（musculus semispinalis）（图258-260）在解剖学上可分为三部分：
(a) 胸部半棘肌（musculus semispinalis thoracis）。其肌纤维在下六节胸椎的横突与上七节胸椎的棘突之间运行，每束肌纤维跨越六至七节椎骨；
(b) 颈部半棘肌（musculus semispinalis cervicis）。其肌纤维在上胸椎的横突与下七节颈椎的棘突之间延伸，跨越五节椎骨；
(c) 头部半棘肌（musculus semispinalis capitis）。该肌位于上五节胸椎的横突与下三至四节颈椎的棘突之间，并延伸至枕骨的项区。该肌可进一步分为内侧部和外侧部。内侧部的肌腹被一条肌腱性交界中断。

Action: contraction oi all parts ol the semispinalis muscle extends the upper segments of the vertebral column and pulls the head back or holds it in this position. Contraction of the muscle on one side causes slight rotation.

> 作用：所有部分的半棘肌收缩可使椎骨的上段伸展，并将头部向后拉或维持此位置。一侧半棘肌收缩可引起轻微的旋转。

Innervation: the spinal nerves (C<sup>2</sup> -C<sup>8</sup> ; Th|-Thi <sup>2</sup> ).

> 支配：脊神经（C<sup>2</sup> -C<sup>8</sup> ; Th|-Thi <sup>2</sup> ）。

- 2. The multifidus muscle *(musculus multifidus)* (Figs 258-260) is covered by the semispinalis muscle and in the lumbar region by the longissimus muscle. Its fibres run for the whole distance of the vertebral column between the transverse and spinous processes of the vertebrae (up to the second cervical) and bridges two, three or four vertebrae. It arises from the posterior surface of the sacrum, the posterior part of the iliac crest, the mamillary processes of the lumbar vertebrae, the transverse processes of the thoracic vertebrae, and the articular processes of the lower four cervical vertebrae and is inserted into the spinous processes of all vertebrae with the exception of the atlas.
- 3. The rotatores muscles *(musculi >otatores)* (Figs 259, 260) are the deepest part of the transversospinalis muscle and are separated into the following parts topographically:
  - (a) the cervical rotatores muscles *(musculi rotatores cervicis);*
  - (b) the thoracic rotatores muscles *(musculi rotatores thoracis);*
  - (c) the lumbar rotatores muscles *(musculi rotatores lumborum).*

> 多裂肌（musculus multifidus）（图258-260）被半棘肌覆盖，在腰区则被最长肌覆盖。其肌纤维沿着椎骨的横突与棘突之间（直至第二颈椎）延伸，跨越两、三或四节椎骨。多裂肌起自骶骨的后表面、髂嵴的后部、腰椎的乳头突、胸椎的横突以及下四节颈椎的关节突，并止于所有椎骨的棘突，但不包括寰椎。

旋转肌（musculi rotatores）（图259、260）是横脊肌中最深层的部分，按其位置可分为以下部分：
(a) 颈部旋转肌（musculi rotatores cervicis）；
(b) 胸部旋转肌（musculi rotatores thoracis）；
(c) 腰部旋转肌（musculi rotatores lumborum）。

They lake origin from the transverse processes of all vertebrae (except the atlas) and the mamillary process of the lumbar vertebrae; overlapping one vertebra, they are inserted into the spinous processes of the next vertebrae above, the adjacent parts of their arches, and the base of the arches of two contiguous vertebrae.

> 它们起源于所有椎骨（除寰椎外）的横突以及腰椎的乳头突；它们重叠一个椎骨，插入到上方椎骨的棘突、相邻部分的椎弓以及两个相邻椎骨的椎弓基部。

Action: contraction of the transversospinalis muscles on both sides extends the vertebral column; contraction of a muscle on one side rotates the column to the contralateral side.

> 作用：双侧横突棘肌收缩可使椎体柱伸展；一侧肌肉收缩则使椎体柱向对侧旋转。[!note]- 术语说明 "transversospinalis muscles" 通常译作“横突棘肌”，是脊柱后侧肌群的一部分，包括多裂肌、回旋肌等。

Innervation: the spinal nerves (C<sup>2</sup> -Cn; Th,-Th ( 2 ; L|-L^).

> 支配神经：脊神经（C<sup>2</sup> -Cn; Th,-Th ( 2 ; L|-L^）

![](books/sinelnikov/pic/_page_268_Picture_2.jpeg)

256. *Muscles of the back and posterior region of the neck (%).*

> 背部及颈部后区的肌肉 (%)

(Long deep muscle s of the back; second superficial layer.)

> （背深部肌群；第二层浅层肌）
